Also Known As: Titus the Priest of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Titus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hieromonk of the Kiev Near Caves who, after a bitter quarrel with another monk, was reconciled on what seemed his deathbed; through forgiveness he was healed and lived out his days in humility, a witness to the power of reconciliation.
Notes: Often invoked for the healing of enmity and the reconciliation of those at strife.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Titus the Soldier of the Kiev Caves
Also Known As: Titus the Former Soldier of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Titus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former soldier wounded in battle who, leaving the world, entered the Kiev Caves and pleased God by his monastic struggles and repentance.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Thalelaeus the Hermit of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Thalelaeus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Syrian ascetic who enclosed himself in a small hut and wept continually for his sins. Numbered among the unmercenary physicians, he healed the sick and converted many pagans, reposing about the year 460.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints Asclepius and James of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Asclepius and James, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two Syrian hermits who struggled in stillness near the village of Nimuza, described among the ascetics of the region by Theodoret of Cyrus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Stephen of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former courtier of Constantinople who devoted his wealth and life to founding and serving a hospice for the aged and infirm, reposing about the year 614.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Julian, Eunus, Besas and Macarius of Alexandria
Also Known As: Julian; Eunos (Kronion); Beza (Besas) the soldier; Mekaros (Macarius)
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Julian, Eunus, Besas and Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Martyrs of Alexandria under the emperor Decius. Julian, crippled and unable to walk, was carried to his confession; his servant Eunus and the soldier Besas, who defended them, and Macarius were all put to death for Christ.
Notes: Commemorated together as one company of martyrs.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Leander, Bishop of Seville
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Leander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Seville and brother of Saints Isidore, Fulgentius and Florentina. He labored to bring the Visigoths from Arianism to the Orthodox faith and reformed the worship of the Spanish Church, reposing about the year 600.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Basil the Confessor
Also Known As: Basil, companion of Procopius of Decapolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk and companion of Saint Prokopios of Decapolis who suffered for the veneration of the holy icons under the iconoclast emperor Leo the Isaurian, enduring torment for the faith and reposing in peace about the year 750.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Nicholas of Pskov, Fool-for-Christ
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Nicholas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A fool-for-Christ of Pskov who labored in this hard ascetic feat for over thirty years, receiving gifts of wonderworking and prophecy. He is famed for boldly rebuking Tsar Ivan the Terrible and so sparing the city of Pskov from the slaughter visited upon Novgorod. He reposed in 1576.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Proterius, Patriarch of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Proterius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Alexandria who upheld the Orthodox faith of the Council of Chalcedon against the Monophysites. He was set upon by a heretical mob and slain on Great and Holy Saturday in 457.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Nestor, Bishop of Magydos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Nestor, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Magydos in Pamphylia who gave himself up to the authorities to spare his flock during the persecution of Decius. After confessing Christ under torture, he was crucified about the year 250.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Marina and Kyra of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mothers Marina and Kyra, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two sisters of Berea in Syria who, leaving their noble home, enclosed themselves in a small open courtyard bound with heavy chains, keeping silence and fasting for some forty years. They reposed about the year 450.
Notes: Commemorated together as sisters.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Domnica of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Domnica, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Syrian ascetic and companion in struggle of Saints Marina and Kyra, who built a small hut and wept continually in prayer, eating only lentils, reposing about the year 460.
Notes: Distinct from Venerable Domnica of Constantinople (Jan 8).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable John Cassian the Roman
Also Known As: John Cassian; Cassian the Roman; Ioannes Kassianos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John Cassian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in Lesser Scythia, he embraced monastic life in Bethlehem and Egypt, learning from the desert fathers, then settled in Gaul where he founded monasteries at Marseilles. His Institutes and Conferences transmitted the wisdom of the Egyptian fathers to the West, including the teaching on the eight passions. He reposed about the year 435.
Notes: Leap-day saint: commemorated Feb 29; in non-leap years his commemoration is transferred to Feb 28.
Works by the Saint: The Institutes of the Cenobitic Life; The Conferences of the Fathers
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John-Barsanuphius, Bishop of Damascus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch John-Barsanuphius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Damascus who, drawn to stillness, withdrew from his see and lived as an ascetic in the Nitrian desert of Egypt, pleasing God in humility and prayer.
Notes: Leap-day saint: in non-leap years the commemoration transfers to Feb 28.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Theoktistus, Abbot of Pelekete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Theoktistus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the Pelekete monastery who suffered for the holy icons under the iconoclast emperor Constantine Copronymus, being tormented with boiling tar and receiving the crown of martyrdom.
Notes: Leap-day saint: in non-leap years the commemoration transfers to Feb 28.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Leo of Cappadocia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Leo, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr who offered himself in exchange for captive monks, laying down his life out of love for the brethren.
Notes: Leap-day saint: in non-leap years the commemoration transfers to Feb 28.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Germanus of Dacia Pontica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Germanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Companion and spiritual brother of Saint John Cassian, with whom he journeyed to the monasteries of Bethlehem and the deserts of Egypt to learn from the fathers; he was ordained priest and shared in Cassian's labors.
Notes: Leap-day saint: in non-leap years the commemoration transfers to Feb 28.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Eudokia of Heliopolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyr Eudokia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Samaritan woman of Heliopolis in Phoenicia who had led a sinful and worldly life before her conversion to Christ, after which she gave her wealth to the poor, embraced monastic asceticism, and was beheaded for her faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrius of Zelenets
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Martyrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born Menas and orphaned young, he was raised by a priest and became a monastic ascetic, founding the Holy Trinity monastery at Zelenets near Veliki Luki in Russia.
Notes: Also commemorated Nov 11.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Nestor, Tribimius, Marcellus, and Anthony of Perge
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Christian preachers at Perge in Pamphylia who fearlessly proclaimed Christ and were put to death during the persecution under the emperor Decius.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Antonina of Nicea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Antonina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian woman of Nicea in Bithynia who endured fierce tortures and imprisonment, remaining steadfast in refusing to offer sacrifice to idols during the reign of Maximian.
Notes: Also commemorated Jun 13.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Marcellus and Anthony
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two martyrs who were committed to the flames for their confession of Christ, their faith proving unbreakable through the ordeal of fire.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Domnina the Younger of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Domnina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A virgin ascetic of Cyrrhus in Syria who lived in great austerity in a small garden hut, and whose holy life was recorded by Blessed Theodoret of Cyrrhus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Agapius of Vatopedi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Agapius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monastic novice of the Vatopedi monastery on Mount Athos who was captured by raiders and carried into slavery, and is remembered as an exemplar of monastic obedience.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: David of Wales
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch David, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop and missionary of Wales who founded monasteries and a strict ascetic rule, became the chief shepherd of the Welsh church, and is honored as the patron saint of Wales.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Theodotus of Cyrenia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Theodotus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop in Cyprus who preached against idolatry and suffered for the faith during the persecution under the emperor Licinius in the early fourth century.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Arsenius of Tver
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Arsenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Monk of the Kiev Caves monastery who became Bishop of Tver, known for his piety and strict observance of monastic discipline; reposed in 1409.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Euthalia of Sicily
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Euthalia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young woman of Leontini in Sicily whose mother was healed through the saints and led the family toward Christ; she was martyred for confessing the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Troadius of Neocaesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Troadius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young man of Neocaesarea in Pontus who endured torture and was martyred under the emperor Decius; his suffering was revealed in a vision to Saint Gregory the Wonderworker.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Agathon of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Agathon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A desert ascetic of Egypt and contemporary of Saint Macarius, remembered for his extraordinary meekness and spiritual discernment.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: 400 Martyrs Slain by the Lombards in Sicily
Also Known As: Sanctulus the Presbyter; Hospicius the Hermit
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A company of Christians in Sicily put to death by the Lombards for refusing to worship idols; among them the names of the presbyter Sanctulus and the hermit Hospicius are preserved.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Sabbatius of Tver
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Sabbatius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian monastic who, with the blessing of Saint Arsenius of Tver, founded a strict monastery near Tver whose discipline influenced later ascetics.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Joachim of Ithaka
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Joachim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born on the Greek island of Ithaka in 1786, he endured a difficult childhood and later embraced the monastic and ascetic life.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Eutropius, Cleonicus, and Basiliscus of Amasea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Eutropius, Cleonicus, and Basiliscus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Brothers Eutropius and Cleonicus, with Basiliscus the nephew of the Great Martyr Theodore the Recruit, confessed Christ and suffered together at Amasea in Pontus under Diocletian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Piamoun the Virgin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Piamoun, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Egyptian virgin raised in piety by her Christian mother, she lived an ascetic life of fasting and prayer and was numbered among the desert saints recorded in the Lausiac History.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints Zenon and Zoilus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Zenon and Zoilus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two saints commemorated together in the Byzantine Synaxarion, of whom little is recorded.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John (Chrysostom) IV, Catholicos of Georgia
Also Known As: John IV of Georgia; John Chrysostom IV
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Catholicos of Georgia at the close of the tenth century, whose parents had prayed for his birth at the tomb of Saint Shio of Mgvime; he shepherded the Georgian Church into the new millennium.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Gerasimus of the Jordan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gerasimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A fifth-century ascetic from Lycia in Asia Minor who, after training in the Egyptian desert, settled by the Jordan River and founded a monastic community. He is famous for the story of taming a lion by removing a thorn from its paw.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Gerasimus of Vologda
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gerasimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk tonsured at the Kiev Caves who travelled north and laboured as the first monastic settler and wonderworker of the Vologda region of Russia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Joasaph of Snetogorsk
Also Known As: Joasaph of Pskov; Ioasaph of Snetogorsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Joasaph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the Snetogorsk monastery near Pskov who was slain together with his monks during a raid by German forces in the thirteenth century.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Prince Basil (Vasilko) of Rostov
Also Known As: Vasilko of Rostov; Basil of Rostov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prince Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prince of Rostov captured by the Mongols after the battle on the Sit River in 1238 who was killed for refusing to renounce his faith and serve his captors.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Prince Daniel of Moscow
Also Known As: Daniel of Moscow; Daniil of Moscow
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prince Daniel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The youngest son of Saint Alexander Nevsky and founder of the Moscow princely line, who ruled with humility, established monasteries, and took monastic vows before his death in 1303.
Notes: Principal feast is Mar 17 (also commemorated Aug 30, translation of relics). Mar 4 is a secondary commemoration. Aug 30 = uncovering of his relics (1652).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martyrs Paul and Juliana of Ptolemais
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Paul and Juliana, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A brother and sister of Ptolemais in Phoenicia who were tortured and put to death for confessing Christ during the reign of the emperor Aurelian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: James the Faster of Phoenicia
Also Known As: James the Ascetic; Jacob the Faster
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father James, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Syrian ascetic who lived enclosed in a cave for many years and received the gift of working wonders, but who, after a grievous fall, did profound penance and was restored.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Prince Wenceslas of the Czech Lands
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prince Wenceslas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A tenth-century duke of Bohemia raised in piety by his grandmother Saint Ludmila, murdered at the church door by his brother's men; March 4 commemorates the translation of his relics.
Notes: Principal feast is Sep 28 (martyrdom); Mar 4 is the translation of relics. Flag for reconciliation if already present at Sep 28.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Gregory of Constantia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Constantia in Cyprus, known from an early liturgical codex; few details of his life survive.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Vyacheslav (Leontiev) the Priest
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Vyacheslav, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian priest who suffered martyrdom during the Soviet persecutions of the twentieth century; few biographical details survive.
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Conon of Isauria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Conon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A native of Isauria in Asia Minor who from his youth was devoted to Christ and, according to his life, aided by the Archangel Michael. He confessed the faith and suffered martyrdom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Conon the Gardener
Also Known As: Conon the Gardener; Conon of Pamphylia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Conon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in Nazareth, he lived quietly tending a garden in Pamphylia. He was seized and tortured during the persecution under Emperor Decius and died confessing Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Onesimus of Isauria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Onesimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr beheaded by the sword for confessing his faith in Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin-Martyr Irais of Antinoe
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Virgin-Martyr Irais, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A virgin martyr of Antinoe in Egypt. Little detail of her life survives beyond her witness unto death.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Eulogius of Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Eulogius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A man who gave his inherited wealth to the poor and wandered preaching Christ and converting pagans, until he was arrested during persecution and martyred.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Eulampius of Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Eulampius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr of Palestine beheaded for his faith in Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Mark the Ascetic
Also Known As: Mark the Faster; Mark the Hermit; Mark the Monk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Mark, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in Athens, he became a monk in the desert of Egypt and was renowned for his ascetic life and knowledge of Scripture, said to know the whole Bible by heart. Several of his ascetic and theological discourses survive.
Works by the Saint: On Those Who Think They Are Made Righteous by Works; On the Spiritual Law
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Hesychius the Faster of Bithynia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hesychius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in the eighth century in a coastal town of Bithynia, he pursued a life of strict asceticism on Mount Maionis, enduring trials from demons, wild animals, and robbers.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable-Martyr Adrian of Poshekhonye
Also Known As: Adrian of Poshekhonye; Adrian of Yaroslavl
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyr Adrian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born at Rostov the Great to pious parents, he became a monk and founded a monastery in the Poshekhonye region. He was slain by robbers at the end of the sixteenth century.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John the New Martyr of Bulgaria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in Bulgaria in 1775, he refused to renounce Christ and accept Islam, and was put to death for his confession of the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: 42 Martyrs of Ammoria
Also Known As: 42 Martyrs of Amorion; Theodore Krateros; Aetios; Theophilos; Constantine Baboutzikos; Bassoes; Kallistos; Constantine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Ammoria, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Byzantine generals and officials captured by the Saracens at the fall of the city of Amorion in 838. After seven years of imprisonment they were put to death for refusing to renounce Christ for Islam.
Notes: Named as one commemoration; seven of the forty-two are known by name.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Hieromartyr Conon and his son Conon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Conon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A widower of Iconium who took up monastic life together with his son, also named Conon. Renowned for casting out demons, healing the sick, and preaching Christ among the pagans, father and son were both martyred for the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Arcadius of Cyprus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Arcadius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Cyprus from his youth during the reign of Constantine the Great. He was the spiritual teacher of the holy martyrs Julian the Physician and Eubolos, who suffered under Julian the Apostate.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyrs of Cherson
Also Known As: Basil of Cherson; Ephraim of Cherson; Capito of Cherson; Eugene of Cherson; Aetherius of Cherson; Elpidius of Cherson; Agathodorus of Cherson
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyrs of Cherson, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A group of seven bishops who carried the Gospel into the North Black Sea region around Cherson in the Crimea and were martyred for their preaching of Christ.
Notes: Named cluster commemorated together; individual names recorded in Also Known As.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Paul the Simple
Life Experience: Marriage; Difficult Marriage; Repentance from grave sin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Paul, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An elderly Egyptian farmer who, after discovering his wife's infidelity, left the world to become a disciple of St. Anthony the Great; he was called the Simple for his guilelessness and gentleness of heart.
Notes: Also commemorated Oct 4.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Paul the Confessor, Bishop of Plousias
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Paul, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Plousias in Bithynia who defended the veneration of holy icons from Scripture during the iconoclast persecution and suffered for his confession of the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Emilian of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Emilian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born Victorinus, he lived a sinful life into old age before repenting, entering the monastic life under the name Emilian, and serving God in humility for the rest of his days.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Theophylactus of Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theophylactus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A 9th-century bishop of Nicomedia known for his charity to the poor, the sick, and lepers, building churches and hospices. For defending the veneration of icons under the iconoclast emperor Leo the Armenian he was exiled, dying in banishment.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Lazarus of Murom
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Lazarus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Greek monk who came to Rus' and founded monastic life on Murom (Mursky) Island in Lake Onega in the far north, enduring great hardship among the local peoples. He reposed in 1391.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Athanasius of Murom
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Athanasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An abbot who labored at the Murom (Mursky) monastery founded by Saint Lazarus; his chains were long preserved as relics. He is commemorated together with Saint Lazarus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hermes of the Seventy
Also Known As: Hermas; Apostle Hermes; Hermes of Dalmatia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Hermes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Seventy Apostles, mentioned by Saint Paul in his Epistle to the Romans. He is said to have served as bishop in Dalmatia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Theodoritus of Antioch
Also Known As: Theodoret of Antioch; Theodoritus the Presbyter
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Theodoritus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A presbyter and keeper of the sacred vessels of the cathedral church of Antioch built by Constantine the Great. Under Julian the Apostate he was seized, tortured, and martyred for refusing to surrender the church's treasures.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Felix of Burgundy
Also Known As: Felix of Dunwich; Felix, Bishop of the East Angles; Felix of East Anglia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Felix, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A 7th-century Burgundian-Frankish missionary who became first bishop of the East Angles, establishing his see at Dommoc (Dunwich). He converted King Sigebert and helped found a school for the kingdom, evangelizing the region until his repose around 647.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: 40 Holy Martyrs of Sebaste
Also Known As: Kyrion; Candidus; Domnos; Hesychius; Heraclius; Smaragdus; Eunoicus; Valens; Vivianus; Claudius; Priscus; Theodulus; Eutyches; John; Xanthias; Helianus; Sisinius; Cyril; Aglaius; Aetius; Flavius; Acacius; Ekdikios; Lysimachus; Alexander; Elias; Gorgonius; Theophilus; Dometian; Gaius; Leontius; Athanasius; Cyril; Sacerdon; Nicholas; Valerius; Philoctimon; Severian; Chudion; Meliton
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Forty Martyrs of Sebaste, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Forty Roman soldiers of the Twelfth Legion at Sebaste in Lesser Armenia who refused to renounce Christ and were condemned to freeze to death overnight on a frozen lake. When one fell away and a guard named Aglaius took his place, the number of forty was restored, and they are honored among the Church's most beloved martyrs.
Notes: One row for the named cluster per grouping rules; representative individual names in Also Known As.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martyr Urpasianus of Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Urpasianus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A dignitary of Nicomedia who renounced his rank and confessed Christ during the persecution under Maximian, and was tortured and martyred for his faith.
Notes: Suffered c. 295-305 under Maximian Galerius.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Caesarius of Nazianzus
Also Known As: Caesarius, brother of St. Gregory the Theologian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Caesarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The younger brother of St. Gregory the Theologian, an esteemed physician trained in Alexandria who served at the imperial court yet held to his faith. He received baptism near the end of his life and reposed shortly after, around 368.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Righteous Tarasius of Liconium
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Tarasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A righteous man of Liconium commemorated on this day; little of his life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; biographical detail not preserved in the source.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Vitalius of Castronovo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Vitalius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born to wealthy parents in Sicily, he embraced the monastic life from his youth and is venerated as a venerable father of the pre-schism West.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Quadratus and Companions at Corinth
Also Known As: Codratus; Cyprian; Dionysius; Anectus; Paul; Crescens
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Quadratus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Quadratus was born near Corinth to a Christian mother who fled to the mountains during persecution. Living as a hermit and healer, he and his companions were arrested under the Decian persecution, dragged through the city, and beheaded for confessing Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martyr Quadratus at Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Quadratus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Quadratus came from a distinguished family and used his wealth to support and visit Christians imprisoned during the persecutions of Decius and Valerian. He and his companions were martyred at Nicomedia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Anastasia the Patrician of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Anastasia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A noblewoman of Constantinople in the reign of Justinian, Anastasia withdrew from the imperial court to escape worldly entanglement and the emperor's attentions. She lived in seclusion near Alexandria, eventually disguising herself as a monk and spending her remaining years in a desert cell.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John of Khakhuli the Oqropiri
Also Known As: John of Khakhuli; John Chrysostom of Khakhuli; Ioane Okropiri
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Khakhuli Monastery in the Tao region of Georgia, John was renowned for his holiness and learning, earning the epithet Oqropiri (Golden-mouthed) after St John Chrysostom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Michael the New Martyr
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Michael, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A New Martyr commemorated on this day; the surviving account preserves little beyond his name and martyrdom for the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Sophronius of Jerusalem
Also Known As: Sophronius the Wise; Sophronius the Sophist
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Sophronius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in Damascus around 560 and known for his learning and love of philosophy, he became Patriarch of Jerusalem and defended Orthodoxy against the Monothelite heresy. He led the Church through the Persian and Arab conquests of the Holy Land.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Euthymius of Novgorod
Also Known As: Euthymius II of Novgorod; John; Ioann
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Euthymius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born to pious parents who dedicated him to God and given the baptismal name John, he became a monk and was elected Archbishop of Novgorod in 1429. He was a notable builder of churches and patron of sacred arts, and his relics were found incorrupt.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Pionius of Smyrna
Also Known As: Hieromartyr Pionius; Limnus; Asclepiades; Macedonia; Sabina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Pionius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Smyrna who, with his companions, suffered martyrdom during the persecution under the Emperor Decius. He boldly confessed Christ before the authorities and was put to death for the faith.
Notes: Commemorated together with Limnus, Asclepiades, Macedonia, and Sabina.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Sophronius of Vratsa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Sophronius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born Stoyko Vladislavov in Kotel, he became Bishop of Vratsa and a leading figure of the Bulgarian National Revival. He composed sermons and an autobiography that helped shape the modern Bulgarian literary language.
Notes: Canonized by the Bulgarian Orthodox Church in 1964.
Works by the Saint: Nedelnik
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Sophronius the Recluse of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Sophronius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A recluse of the Far Caves of the Kiev Monastery who lived in great austerity, wearing a hairshirt and an iron belt and reciting the entire Psalter each day. He is numbered among the venerable fathers of the Kiev Caves.
Notes: May 11: also commemorated as a recluse of the Caves.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Theophanes the Confessor of Sigriane
Also Known As: Theophanes the Confessor; Theophanes the Chronicler
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theophanes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A nobleman of Constantinople who left married life by mutual consent to become a monk and abbot at Sigriane. He suffered imprisonment and exile for venerating the holy icons during the second iconoclast persecution, and is remembered as the author of an important Byzantine chronicle.
Works by the Saint: Chronographia
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Demetrius II the Self-Sacrificing, King of Georgia
Also Known As: Demetre II of Georgia; Demetrius the Self-Sacrificing; Tavdadebuli
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing King Demetrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: King of Georgia and great-grandson of Holy Queen Tamar, who reigned as a vassal under the Mongol Ilkhans. Summoned before the Khan and aware that he faced death, he gave himself up to spare his people from reprisal and was beheaded in 1289, earning the title Tavdadebuli, 'the self-sacrificing.'
Notes: Demetre II the Self-Sacrificing (reposed 1289). Distinct from King Demetre I (Damiane), the monk-hymnographer commemorated May 23. This row was previously conflated with Demetre I; its biography has been corrected.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Kirion II Catholicos-Patriarch of All Georgia
Also Known As: Kyrion II; Kirion II of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Kirion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned bishop and church historian who became the first Catholicos-Patriarch of Georgia after the restoration of the Georgian Church's independence in 1917. He was found murdered at Martqopi Monastery in 1918 under circumstances that remain unexplained.
Notes: Also commemorated Jun 27.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martyr Sabinus of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Sabinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Egypt, an administrator of the city of Hermopolis, who was betrayed while in hiding during the persecution under Diocletian and, after torture, was drowned in the Nile for refusing to deny Christ.
Notes: OCA commemorates Martyr Sabinus of Egypt on both Mar 13 and Mar 16 (Sabinus of Hermopolis); merged here as one saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Africanus, Publius, and Terence of Carthage
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Africanus, Publius, and Terence, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A group of Christians at Carthage who, refusing to sacrifice to idols, were tortured and put to death for confessing Christ.
Notes: Also commemorated on April 10. Oct 28 = the Carthage commemoration of the martyrs.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Publius, Bishop of Athens
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Publius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Athens, counted by the synaxarion among the Seventy and successor to Dionysius the Areopagite, who shepherded the Athenian church and was martyred for the faith.
Notes: OCA counts him among the Seventy; some Western sources identify him with Publius of Malta and place his feast on Jan 22.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martyr Alexander of Pydna
Also Known As: Alexander of Macedonia; Alexander the Presbyter
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A presbyter near Thessalonica who turned many from idolatry by his preaching and was beheaded after enduring tortures under the emperor Maximian Galerius.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Christina of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Christina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian woman in Persia who was scourged to death for her confession of Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Benedict of Nursia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Benedict, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Benedict left Rome as a young man to live as a hermit at Subiaco, then gathered disciples and founded a series of monasteries, crowned by the great house at Monte Cassino. His Rule, balancing prayer, work, and obedience, became the foundation of Western monasticism.
Works by the Saint: The Rule of Saint Benedict
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Saint Theognostus, Metropolitan of Kiev and All Russia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theognostus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Greek hierarch who succeeded Saint Peter as Metropolitan of Kiev and All Russia, ruling the Russian Church from Moscow through the mid-14th century and strengthening its ties with the Church of Constantinople.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Great Prince Rostislav-Michael of Kiev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Right-believing Prince Rostislav, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A son of Saint Mstislav the Great, Rostislav-Michael was a Great Prince of Kiev remembered as a devout ruler who supported the Church and worked for peace among the warring princes of his day.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Euschemon the Confessor, Bishop of Lampsacus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Euschemon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Lampsacus on the Hellespont, Euschemon was known for his ascetic life and defense of the holy icons, for which he suffered imprisonment under the iconoclast emperor Theophilus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Agapius, Publius, Timolaus, Romulus, Dionysius, and Alexander of Caesarea
Also Known As: Agapius; Publius (Pausis); Timolaus; Romulus; Dionysius; Dionysius; Alexander; Alexander
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Agapius and your companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A group of young Christians who suffered together at Caesarea in Palestine during the persecution under Diocletian. Refusing to offer pagan sacrifice, they were tortured and beheaded for confessing Christ.
Notes: Named cluster commemorated as one: Agapius, Publius (Pausis), Timolaus, Romulus, two named Dionysius, and two named Alexander.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Alexander of Side
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Side in Pamphylia arrested and interrogated during the persecution under the emperor Aurelian. After enduring severe tortures for his confession of Christ, he was put to death.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Nicander of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Nicander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A physician in Egypt who ministered to imprisoned Christians during the persecution under Diocletian, bringing them food and reverently burying the bodies of the martyrs. He was seized and martyred for these works of mercy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Papas of Lycaonia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Papas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Lycaonia who endured cruel tortures, including spiked boots, and was put to death at Diocaesarea during the reign of Maximian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Serapion, Archbishop of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Serapion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born near Moscow, he served as a parish priest, was widowed, and took monastic vows, later becoming abbot and eventually Archbishop of Novgorod.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Aristobulus of the Seventy, Bishop of Britain
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Aristobulus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Seventy Apostles and brother of the Apostle Barnabas, mentioned by St Paul in Romans 16:10. He is honored as a first preacher of the Gospel and a bishop in Britain.
Notes: Also commemorated Oct 31.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Hieromartyr Alexander, Pope of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Rome who guided the Church for about ten years and was martyred during the reign of the emperor Hadrian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Julian of Anazarbus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Julian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Anazarbus in Cilicia who endured prolonged suffering and was martyred at Antioch; his relics became a source of healing.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyrs Trophimus and Thalus of Laodicea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyrs Trophimus and Thalus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two brothers and presbyters of Laodicea who confessed Christ and were put to death under the persecution of Diocletian.
Notes: Named pair of brothers commemorated together; one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints Pimen of Salos and Anton of Meskheti
Also Known As: Pimen of Salosi; Pimen the Fool-for-Christ; Anton Meskhi; Anton of Meskheti
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Fathers Pimen and Anton, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Georgian ascetics who labored as missionaries among the peoples of the North Caucasus during the period of Mongol domination, Pimen taking on the podvig of folly for Christ.
Notes: Two missionaries commemorated together; one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Christodoulos the Wonderworker of Patmos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Christodoulos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Byzantine monk and ascetic born near Nicaea who, after years in monasteries of the Holy Land, Bithynia, and Asia Minor, founded the Monastery of Saint John the Theologian on Patmos in 1088.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Saint Ambrose the Confessor of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Ambrose, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian priest and later Catholicos-Patriarch who defended the independence of the Georgian Church and confessed the faith under Soviet persecution.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Alexis the Man of God
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Alexis, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born to a wealthy senatorial family in Rome, Alexis fled an arranged marriage on his wedding night to live as an unknown beggar, first in Edessa and later returning to live unrecognized as a poor man in his own father's house. He embraced voluntary poverty and homelessness for the sake of Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Macarius of Kalyazin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A boyar's son born near Kashin in 1400, Macarius was married but embraced monastic life after the deaths of his parents and his wife. He founded the Holy Trinity Monastery at Kalyazin and was renowned as a wonderworker.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Marinus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Marinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A zealous Christian during a time of persecution who destroyed the offerings the pagans were making to their idols and was put to death for his faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Gertrude of Nivelles
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Gertrude, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A 7th-century Frankish noblewoman who co-founded with her mother Itta the double monastery of Nivelles in present-day Belgium and served as its abbess, devoting herself to hospitality, care of the poor, and prayer. She is invoked by travelers and pilgrims.
Notes: Not on the OCA Mar 17 page; pre-schism Western saint with a Mar 17 feast. Region set to Gaul / France (no Belgium/Brabant term).
Sources: en.wikipedia.org

Name: Gabriel the Lesser
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Gabriel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An 18th-century figure of the Georgian Church who received a good education and pursued a life dedicated to God.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives few biographical details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Cyril, Archbishop of Jerusalem
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Cyril, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A native of Jerusalem who became a presbyter and then archbishop of the city around 350. He is renowned for his catechetical lectures instructing those preparing for baptism, and he endured repeated exile amid the Arian controversies.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Aninas the Wonderworker
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Aninas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A native of Chalcedon who entered the monastic life at the age of fifteen and withdrew to a life of extreme solitude in the desert near the Euphrates River, renowned for his ascetic feats and the gift of wonderworking.
Notes: OCA commemorates him on both Mar 13 and Mar 18.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Trophimus and Eucarpus of Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Trophimus and Eucarpus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Soldiers at Nicomedia under the persecution of Diocletian who were charged with hunting down Christians, but who were converted through a vision and themselves embraced martyrdom for Christ.
Notes: Named pair commemorated together; one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Nikolai of Zhicha
Also Known As: Nikolai Velimirovic; Nicholas of Zhicha; the Serbian Chrysostom
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Nikolai, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian bishop and prolific spiritual writer often called the Serbian Chrysostom, born in 1881. He was imprisoned in the Dachau concentration camp during the Second World War and later reposed in America in 1956. He is the author of the Prologue of Ohrid.
Notes: Glorified 2003. Also commemorated on the translation of his relics (some calendars May 3).
Works by the Saint: The Prologue of Ohrid; Prayers by the Lake
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Cyril of Astrakhan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Cyril, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hierarch associated with Astrakhan commemorated on this day; little biographical detail is available in the source.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA lists the name only with no life given.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Chrysanthus and Daria of Rome
Also Known As: Chrysanthus; Daria; Claudius the Tribune; Hilaria; Maurus; Jason; Diodorus the Presbyter; Marianus the Deacon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Chrysanthus and Daria, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Chrysanthus, son of an Egyptian patrician family that had moved to Rome, embraced Christianity after seeking out the Scriptures. He was married to Daria, a former pagan priestess he had converted, and the two lived together in chastity; both were martyred at Rome along with many companions.
Notes: Martyred at Rome under Numerian, c. 283. Companions include the tribune Claudius and his wife Hilaria, their sons Maurus and Jason, the presbyter Diodorus, and the deacon Marianus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Saint Innocent of Komel and Vologda
Also Known As: Innocent of Komel; Innocent of Vologda; Innocent Okhlyabinin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Innocent, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Descended from the Moscow princely Okhlyabinin family, Innocent became a monk under Saint Cyril of White Lake and was a disciple of Saint Nilus of Sora. He later founded a hermitage in the Komel forest near Vologda and reposed in the early 16th century.
Notes: Disciple of Saint Nilus of Sora; reposed c. 1521.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Pancharius at Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Pancharius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Pancharius, a friend of the emperor Diocletian, abandoned Christianity for paganism but was moved to repentance by a letter from his mother and sister. He openly confessed Christ again and was martyred at Nicomedia.
Notes: Martyred at Nicomedia under Diocletian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Holy Fathers slain at the Monastery of St Sabbas the Sanctified
Also Known As: Martyrs John, Sergius, and Patrick of Mar Saba; Monk-Martyrs of the Lavra of Saint Sabbas
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyrs of the Monastery of Saint Sabbas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A community of monks of the Great Lavra of Saint Sabbas the Sanctified near Jerusalem who were slain by Saracen raiders, refusing to abandon their monastery or their faith.
Notes: Named individuals include John, Sergius, and Patrick.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Euphrosynus of Blue Jay Lake (Sinozersky)
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable-Martyr Euphrosynus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian monk who, after time near Valaam, withdrew to a remote hermitage at Sinozero (Blue Jay Lake) in the Novgorod region, where he was slain during an invasion (d. 1612).
Notes: OCA dates him to the 16th c.; his repose (1612) places him in the early 17th c. (used here).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyrs Alexandra, Claudia, Euphrasia, Matrona, Juliana, Euphemia, and Theodora
Also Known As: Alexandra; Claudia; Euphrasia; Matrona; Juliana; Euphemia; Theodora
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Virgin-Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Seven Christian women martyred during the persecution under the emperor Maximian for their confession of Christ.
Notes: Named cluster commemorated together; one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Niketas the Confessor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Niketas the Confessor, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Apollonias in Bithynia who defended the veneration of the holy icons during the iconoclast persecution and suffered for the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Myron of Crete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Myron, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A New Martyr of Crete who confessed Christ under Ottoman rule; few biographical details survive.
Notes: Honest stub; century not given by the source (low confidence).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: James the Confessor
Also Known As: James the Confessor, Bishop of Catania; Jacob the Confessor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch James, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Studite monastery and a diligent student of Scripture, James endured persecution under the iconoclasts and confessed the veneration of the holy icons. He was later made Bishop of Catania in Sicily.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Cyril of Catania
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Cyril, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of the Apostle Peter who appointed him Bishop of Catania in Sicily. He was remembered as a wonderworker, including the healing of bitter water through prayer.
Notes: Originally from Antioch.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Thomas the Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Thomas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Thomas served as deacon and sacristan under Patriarch John the Faster before being elevated to Patriarch of Constantinople; he guided the Church in the early seventh century.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Seraphim of Vyritsa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Seraphim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born Basil Muraviev, a successful merchant who, with his wife's blessing, embraced monastic life and was later ordained. As an elder at Vyritsa near Petrograd he became renowned for spiritual counsel and prayer through the years of Soviet persecution.
Notes: Glorified by the Russian Orthodox Church in 2000; his principal feast is Apr 3 (glorification). OCA lists him on Mar 21.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Serapion of Thmuis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Serapion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Egyptian ascetic and companion of St Anthony the Great, known for his austerity. As Bishop of Thmuis in Lower Egypt he stood with St Athanasius against Arianism and wrote against the Manichaeans.
Notes: Distinct from Serapion the Sindonite, the wandering anchorite commemorated May 14.
Works by the Saint: On the Holy Spirit; Against the Manichaeans
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Enda of Aran
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Enda, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Once a warrior-king of Oriel in Ulster, Enda was converted through the influence of his sister St Fanchea and embraced the monastic life. He founded one of the earliest monasteries of Ireland on Inishmore in the Aran Islands.
Notes: Not on the OCA Mar 21 page (which lists 5); added as a pre-schism Western saint attested on the Mar 21 list.
Sources: en.wikipedia.org

Name: Drosis of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Drosis, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: According to tradition a daughter of the Emperor Trajan who embraced Christianity. With several companions she was seized for her faith and gave her life as a martyr, having helped to bury the bodies of slain Christians.
Notes: Traditionally numbered among martyrs who suffered with her companions; commemorated also in the homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Isaac the Confessor of Dalmatos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Isaac, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic who confronted the Arian emperor Valens and foretold his downfall during the persecution of the Orthodox. He later founded the Dalmaton Monastery in Constantinople and is honored as a confessor of the faith.
Notes: Founder of the Dalmaton Monastery; also commemorated May 30 and Aug 3. Principal feast May 30 (also Aug 3 with the Dalmatos fathers). Also commemorated Aug 3 with Sts Dalmatus and Faustus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Euthymius of Mount Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyr Euthymius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born Eleutherius in Demitsana of the Peloponnese, he became a monk of the Skete of the Forerunner (Prodromou) on Mount Athos. He confessed Christ before the Turks and was martyred, joining the company of the New Martyrs.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Kalliniki and Basilissa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Kalliniki and Basilissa, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two faithful women of Galatia who used their means to comfort and care for imprisoned Christians. For openly confessing the faith they were arrested and put to death as martyrs.
Notes: Grouped as one commemoration in the synaxarion.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable-Martyr Nikon of Sicily
Also Known As: Nikon of Sicily; Nikon and his 199 disciples; Nicon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyr Nikon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A soldier born near Naples of a pagan father and Christian mother, Nikon was baptized and became a monastic leader in Sicily. He and his 199 disciples were arrested and put to death for confessing Christ.
Notes: Named cluster commemorated as one: Nikon together with his 199 disciples.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Nikon of the Kiev Far Caves
Also Known As: Nikon of the Caves; Nikon, Abbot of the Kiev Far Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nikon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An early disciple of Saint Anthony at the Kiev Caves monastery, Nikon tonsured many of the brethren, including Saint Theodosius, and later served as abbot of the community.
Notes: Of the Kiev Caves (Pechersk). Reposed 1088.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Philetus the Senator
Also Known As: Philetus; Philetos; Lydia; Macedonius; Theoprepius; Amphilochius; Chronides
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Philetus and companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Philetus, a senator at the court of the emperor Hadrian, was condemned for confessing Christ together with his wife Lydia and their sons Macedonius and Theoprepius. They were martyred in Illyria.
Notes: Named cluster: Philetus, his wife Lydia, sons Macedonius and Theoprepius, and (per tradition) the commander Amphilochius and Chronides the notary. Martyred in Illyria under Hadrian (117-138).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Basil of Mangazea
Also Known As: Basil of Mangazea; Basil of Mangazeya; Vasily of Mangazea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Passion-Bearer Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A pious young man working for a merchant in the Siberian town of Mangazea, Basil was falsely accused of theft after a Pascha-night robbery. Tortured and struck dead though innocent, he died on Pascha of 1600; his incorrupt relics were revealed in 1652.
Notes: First saint glorified in Siberia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: New Martyr Luke of Mytilene
Also Known As: Luke of Mytilene; Luke of Saint Anne's Skete; Luke of Odrin; Luke the New Martyr
Life Experience: Repentance from grave sin; Persecution; Imprisonment
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Luke, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born at Adrianople (Odrin), Luke fell away to Islam in his youth but repented deeply and became an ascetic at Saint Anne's Skete on Mount Athos. After preparing for martyrdom, he confessed Christ at Mytilene and was hanged in 1802.
Notes: Born Adrianople/Odrin; ascetic at St Anne's Skete, Athos; martyred by hanging at Mytilene, 1802, aged 19.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Sergius (Srebryansky) the New Confessor of Tver
Also Known As: Sergius Srebryansky; Mitrofan Srebryansky; Sergius the New Confessor of Tver
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Sergius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A married parish priest and army chaplain, Mitrofan Srebryansky became confessor of the Marfo-Mariinsky Convent and was later tonsured the monk Sergius. Repeatedly arrested and exiled under the Soviets, he endured imprisonment with faith and reposed in 1948.
Notes: Glorified among the New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia (canonized 2000). Reposed 1948 at Vladychnya, Tver province.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; stjohndc.org

Name: Zacharias the Recluse
Also Known As: Zachariah the Recluse; Zacharias of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Zacharias, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Egyptian desert ascetic and recluse remembered for his deep concern for the poor and homeless, on whose account he was given a name marking his care for the outcast. OCA notes he has at times been erroneously identified with Saint Zachariah the Monk.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Artemon, Bishop of Seleucia
Also Known As: Artemon of Seleucia; Artemius of Seleucia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Artemon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A pious and virtuous man of Seleucia in Pisidia whom the Apostle Paul, passing through the city, ordained as its first bishop.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Zachariah the Ascetic of the Kiev Caves
Also Known As: Zacharias of the Kiev Caves; Zacharias the Faster
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Zachariah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves renowned for extreme fasting, taking only uncooked greens once a day. Commemorated among the saints of the Near Caves.
Notes: Reckoned to the thirteenth-fourteenth centuries.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Stephen of Kazan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Tatar convert to Christianity in Kazan who, healed of a weakness in his legs after baptism, suffered death for abandoning Islam for Christ.
Notes: Martyred in the period following the incorporation of Kazan.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Peter of Kazan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A newly-baptized Tatar convert of Kazan who was put to death for forsaking Islam and confessing Christ.
Notes: Martyred in the period following the incorporation of Kazan.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Irenaeus of Sirmium
Also Known As: Hieromartyr Irenaeus; Irenaeus Bishop of Sirmium
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Irenaeus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Sirmium in Pannonia who was arrested during the persecution under Diocletian and Maximian; refusing to sacrifice to the idols and unmoved by appeals to spare himself for the sake of his family, he was beheaded and his body cast into the river.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: 26 Martyrs of the Goths in the Crimea
Also Known As: Bathusius; Bercus; Arpilus; Abibus; Agnus; Reasus; Igathrax; Iscoeus; Silas; Signicus; Sonirilus; Suimbalus; Thermus; Phillus; Anna; Alla; Larissa; Monco; Uirko; Animais; Queen Gaatha; Princess Duklida
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of the Goths, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A group of twenty-six Christians among the Goths of the Crimea who were martyred for the faith under the Arian Gothic king Jungerich, several of them burned alive in a church; the relics were later gathered by Queen Gaatha and Princess Duklida.
Notes: Listed by the source as one commemoration; named individuals include the presbyter Bercus, the monk Arpilus, and a number of laymen and laywomen. Martyred c. 375.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Malchus of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Malchus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Syrian who fled an arranged marriage to become a monk near Chalcis; carried off into captivity and forced into servitude, he preserved his chastity and eventually returned to the monastic life.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Basil the New
Also Known As: Basil the Younger; Venerable Basil the New of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic who lived as a solitary near Constantinople in the tenth century; arrested and tortured on suspicion of being a spy, he was found to be a holy man of God and became renowned as a wonderworker and clairvoyant elder.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Montanus the Presbyter and Maxima
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Montanus and Maxima, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Singidunum (present-day Belgrade) and his wife who, during the persecution under Diocletian, refused to sacrifice to the idols; they were drowned for confessing Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: George the New Martyr of Sofia
Also Known As: New Martyr George of Sofia; George of Adrianople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A soldier serving in the Ottoman army who, when his fellow soldiers mocked Christ, openly confessed his faith; for refusing to renounce Christ he was put to death at Adrianople in 1437.
Notes: The Elder/Older New Martyr George of Sofia (d. 1437), distinct from George the goldsmith of Sofia/Kratovo (d. 1515, commemorated Feb 11).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Matrona of Thessalonica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Matrona, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian slave at Thessalonica who secretly attended church despite serving a Jewish mistress; when her faith was discovered she was beaten and bound, and died of her wounds rather than renounce Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Manuel and Theodosius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Manuel and Theodosius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two Christians who openly confessed Christ before a pagan governor and were put to death for the faith, around the year 304.
Notes: Suffered at Sirmium.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: John the Clairvoyant, Anchorite of Egypt
Also Known As: John the Anchorite; John of Lycopolis; John the Hermit
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A carpenter who in middle age withdrew to a hermitage near Lycopolis in Egypt, where he lived enclosed for decades and became renowned for prophecy and the gift of discerning hearts.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Prophet Hanani
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Hanani, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Old Testament seer who rebuked King Asa of Judah for trusting in a foreign alliance rather than in the Lord, and was imprisoned for his words (2 Chronicles 16).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hilarion the New of Pelecete
Also Known As: Hilarion the New; Hilarion of Pelekete; Hilarion the Abbot of Pelekete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hilarion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit from his youth who became abbot of the monastery of Pelekete near the Dardanelles. He suffered for the veneration of icons during the iconoclast persecution, enduring imprisonment and ill-treatment, and was renowned for gifts of grace.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Stephen the Wonderworker of Triglia
Also Known As: Stephen of Triglia; Stephen the Confessor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the monastery of Triglia who confessed the Orthodox veneration of icons under the iconoclast emperor Leo the Armenian. He endured persecution for the faith and was known as a wonderworker.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Eustratius of the Kiev Caves
Also Known As: Eustratius the Faster; Eustratius of the Near Caves; Eustratius the Martyr
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyr Eustratius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born to a wealthy family, he gave away his possessions and became a monk of the Kiev Caves Monastery. Taken captive during a raid and sold into slavery, he endured martyrdom for refusing to deny Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hilarion of Pskov
Also Known As: Hilarion of Gdov; Hilarion of Lake Gdov; Hilarion the Abbot of Ozersk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hilarion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint Euphrosynus of Pskov who founded a monastery near Lake Gdov on the border with Livonian territory, where he labored in asceticism and pastored the brethren.
Notes: Oct 21 = his Gdov/Pskov-Lake commemoration.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Jonah and Barachisius of Persia
Also Known As: Jonah; Barachisius; Martyrs of Persia under Sapor; companions of Jonah and Barachisius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Jonah and Barachisius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two Christian brothers who, during the persecution under the Persian king Sapor, visited and encouraged imprisoned confessors of Christ. They were arrested and put to severe tortures, dying as martyrs together with their companions.
Notes: Listed by the source as a named cluster commemorated together; companions are included here.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Boyan-Enravota of Bulgaria
Also Known As: Boyan; Enravota; Voin; Martyr Boyan Prince of Bulgaria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Boyan, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Bulgarian prince who came to faith in Christ during the reign of his pagan brother. Refusing to renounce Christianity, he was put to death and is honored as one of the first martyrs of the Bulgarian land.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: John of Manglisi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian ascetic spiritually formed in the David-Gareja wilderness who was consecrated bishop of Manglisi and founded a monastery. He labored for the faith in difficult circumstances during the 17th and early 18th centuries.
Notes: Lived 1668-1724; born in the 17th century, reposed in the 18th.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hesychius the Presbyter of Jerusalem
Also Known As: Hesychios of Jerusalem; Hesychius the Presbyter
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hesychius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned presbyter of Jerusalem who, after mastering the Holy Scriptures, devoted himself to the ascetic life and composed numerous spiritual treatises and scriptural commentaries.
Works by the Saint: On the Holy Scriptures
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Mark, Bishop of Arethusa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Mark, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Arethusa in Syria who under Constantine had destroyed a pagan temple and built a church in its place. When Julian the Apostate came to power, the pagans seized him and subjected him to cruel tortures, which he endured with steadfast courage.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Cyril the Deacon of Heliopolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Cyril, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon of Heliopolis in Phoenicia who under Constantine had zealously destroyed idols and pagan altars. During the reign of Julian the Apostate the pagans rose against him and put him to death with great cruelty.
Notes: Commemorated together with others martyred under Julian the Apostate.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: John the Anchorite of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born to a pious widow during a time of persecution, John was raised in prayer and the Scriptures by his mother. In maturity he withdrew to the Egyptian desert and lived as a solitary anchorite.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Eustathius the Confessor, Bishop of Kios
Also Known As: Eustace the Confessor; Eustathius of Bithynia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Eustathius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A meek and virtuous monk of Bithynia, esteemed for his faith and charity, who was raised to the episcopate of Kios. He suffered persecution as a confessor and reposed in peace.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Mark, Jonah, and Bassa of the Pskov Caves
Also Known As: Mark of the Pskov Caves; Jonah of the Pskov Caves; Bassa of the Pskov Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers and Mother, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Honored as founders of the Pskov Caves Monastery. Mark was the first hermit to settle in the natural caves by the Kamenets stream; Jonah established the monastery there, and Bassa, the wife of Jonah, became a nun and was among its first.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Nicetas of the Roslavl Forests
Also Known As: Niketas of Roslavl; Nikitas the Schemamonk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nicetas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A pilgrim from childhood who embraced the monastic life and dwelt as an ascetic in the Roslavl forests near the White Bluff Hermitage.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: St. Sophronius, Bishop of Irkutsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Sophronius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Eighteenth-century bishop of Irkutsk, born in the Chernigov region, who labored as a missionary and pastor across Siberia and is honored as a wonderworker.
Notes: Modern Russian saint; born 1703/1704, reposed 1771, glorified 1918. Flag for clergy/canonization review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Prophet Joad
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Joad, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Old Testament prophet sent to rebuke King Jeroboam for idolatry.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Sosthenes of the Seventy
Also Known As: Apostles Apollos, Cephas, Caesar, and Epaphroditus of the Seventy; Sosthenes of Corinth
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Sosthenes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Sosthenes, former ruler of the synagogue at Corinth and co-greeter in First Corinthians, commemorated together with the Apostles Apollos, Cephas, Caesar, and Epaphroditus of the Seventy.
Notes: Listed as a cluster of Apostles of the Seventy. Several have principal feasts elsewhere (Apollos and others on Dec 8); possible existing-row reconciliation.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: St. Euboula
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Euboula, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Pious mother of the Great Martyr Panteleimon of Nicomedia, who raised her son in the Christian faith; she reposed before his martyrdom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: St. John the Silent
Also Known As: John the Hesychast; John the Sabbaite
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Colonia who left his see for the monastic life and became a renowned hesychast at the Lavra of St. Savva in Palestine.
Notes: Principal feast commonly Dec 3; commemorated Mar 30 in the OCA listing.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: St. Zosimus, Bishop of Syracuse
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Zosimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Raised in monastic life from childhood at the monastery of St. Lucy in Sicily, he later became bishop of Syracuse.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Hypatius the Wonderworker, Bishop of Gangra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Hypatius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Gangra in Paphlagonia who took part in the First Ecumenical Council at Nicaea in 325. He was slain by heretics and is venerated as a wonderworker and healer.
Notes: Attended the First Ecumenical Council (325).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: St. Jonah, Metropolitan of Moscow and All Russia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Jonah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk who rose to become Metropolitan of Moscow and All Russia in the 15th century, marking the autocephaly of the Russian Church. He was a wonderworker whose relics are venerated in the Dormition Cathedral of the Moscow Kremlin.
Notes: Reposed 1461; among the great hierarchs of Moscow. Also commemorated Jun 15.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Hypatius the Healer of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hypatius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves who labored in strict fasting, vigil, and prayer, gaining the gift of healing the sick. His relics rest in the Far Caves of the Kiev Caves Lavra.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Apollonius, Ascetic, of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Apollonius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A desert father of the Egyptian Thebaid who from youth devoted himself to asceticism and gathered around him a community of some five hundred monks.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Avdas, Bishop of Persia, and Martyr Benjamin the Deacon
Also Known As: Abdas of Persia; Avda; Benjamin the Deacon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Avdas and Martyr Benjamin, pray to God for us.
