Brief Life: Avdas, a bishop in Persia, was executed for refusing to rebuild a Zoroastrian fire-temple, sparking a persecution of Christians. The deacon Benjamin suffered martyrdom with him for the faith.
Notes: Grouped cluster: Avdas (bishop) and Benjamin (deacon) listed as one commemoration; both names in Also Known As. Possibly the same Persian bishop as the Sep 5 commemoration (OS-0229, Abdas of Persia); kept separate as a distinct synaxarion entry — flag for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Hypatius the Abbot of Rufinianus in Chalcedon
Also Known As: Hypatius of Rufinianus; Hypatius the Abbot
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hypatius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An educated young man who embraced the monastic life and became abbot of the monastery of Rufinianus near Chalcedon, known for his zeal against paganism and heresy.
Notes: Reposed c. 446.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: St. Acacius the Confessor, Bishop of Melitene
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Acacius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Melitene who boldly confessed Christ and was tortured during the Decian persecution but survived, earning the title Confessor.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Euthymius of Suzdal
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Euthymius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Trained in monastic life at the Nizhny Novgorod Caves under Saint Dionysius, he became the founding abbot of the Savior Monastery at Suzdal, where he was renowned for his ascetic labors.
Notes: His relics were uncovered July 4, sometimes commemorated then.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Abraham of Bulgaria
Also Known As: Abraham the Martyr of Bulgaria; Abraham the New Martyr
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Abraham, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wealthy Volga Bulgar merchant, born a Muslim, who embraced Christ and gave generously to the poor; he was tortured and beheaded by his countrymen for refusing to renounce the faith.
Notes: Venerated chiefly in the Russian tradition; relics translated to Vladimir.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Gerontius the Canonarch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gerontius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves who served as canonarch, leading the chanting in church, and pursued strict ascetic discipline; his relics rest in the Far Caves.
Notes: Reposed in the Far (Theodosian) Caves of Kiev.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Macarius the Confessor of Pelekete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Orphaned young and well-grounded in Scripture, he became abbot of the Pelekete monastery near the Hellespont and suffered exile and hardship for venerating the holy icons during the iconoclast persecution.
Notes: Confessor under iconoclasm; born c. 785, reposed 9th century.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Gerontius and Basilides
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Gerontius and Basilides, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two early Christian martyrs who were beheaded for confessing Christ.
Notes: Grouped as one commemoration per the source. Region not specified by the source — left blank.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: John Shavteli
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian monk, theologian, and hymnographer educated at the Gelati Academy during the reign of Queen Tamar, celebrated for his learning and ascetic life.
Notes: Venerated in the Georgian tradition; closely associated with St Eulogius the Fool-for-Christ. Also commemorated Jun 9 (John of Shavta-Gelati).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Eulogius the Prophet
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Eulogius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian fool-for-Christ and seer associated with Saint John Shavteli, honored for the gift of prophecy.
Notes: Venerated in the Georgian tradition. OCA titles him 'Prophet'; closely linked to St John Shavteli.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Barsanuphius of Optina
Also Known As: Barsanuphius the Elder of Optina; Paul Plikhankov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Barsanuphius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born Paul Plikhankov, a military officer who left a worldly career to enter Optina Monastery, becoming one of its last great elders and a gifted spiritual guide and confessor.
Notes: One of the Optina Elders; reposed 1913. Glorified by the Russian Church (Synaxis of the Optina Elders).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Titus the Wonderworker
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Titus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Studion Monastery near Constantinople who from youth embraced ascetic struggle through fasting and prayer. Beloved by the brethren, he was ordained a priest at their request and was renowned as a wonderworker.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Polycarp of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Polycarp, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Alexandria who, moved by zeal for God, openly denounced the emperor Maximian for his bloody persecution of Christians and was put to death for his confession.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint George Matskevereli of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hierarch of Georgia of noble birth whose memory is preserved in the writings of the tenth-century Georgian hagiographers George Merchule and Basil of Zarzma.
Notes: Lived late 9th to early 10th century.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Niketas the Confessor
Also Known As: Nicetas the Confessor; Niketas of Medikion; Nicetas of Medikion
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Niketas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A 9th-century abbot of the Medikion monastery in Bithynia who suffered persecution and exile as a confessor for the veneration of the holy icons during the second iconoclast period. He reposed in 824.
Notes: Defender of the holy icons during the iconoclast controversy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Holy Virgin Martyr Theodosia of Tyre
Also Known As: Theodosia of Tyre; Theodora of Tyre
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Virgin Martyr Theodosia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Christian woman of about seventeen who, during the persecution under Diocletian, openly encouraged condemned Christians at Caesarea in Palestine. She was seized, tortured, and cast into the sea, receiving the crown of martyrdom in the early 4th century.
Notes: In the Greek tradition her principal feast is observed on Apr 2; the OCA commemorates her on Apr 3. Also commemorated May 29; in the Greek tradition on Apr 2.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martyr Irene
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Irene, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A holy martyr commemorated on this day; details of her life and martyrdom are not preserved in the available source.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA lists the name without a biography.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Illyricus of Mount Myrsinon
Also Known As: Illyricus of Mount Myrsinon; Illyricus the Wonderworker
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Illyricus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic and wonderworker who pursued the monastic struggle on Mount Myrsinon in the Peloponnesus. His dates of birth and repose are unknown.
Notes: Honest stub; flourished on Mount Myrsinon in the Peloponnesus (Greece).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Elpidephorus, Dius, Bithonius, and Galycus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Elpidephorus, Dius, Bithonius, and Galycus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Four holy martyrs who suffered together for Christ: Elpidephorus was beheaded, Dius stoned, Bithonius drowned, and Galycus given to wild beasts.
Notes: Named cluster commemorated as one; individual names listed in Also Known As per grouping rule. Era/century not preserved in source.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Joseph the Hymnographer
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in Sicily and forced to flee barbarian invasions, he became a monk and the most prolific hymnographer of the Church, composing many canons for the saints; he is called the sweet-voiced nightingale of the Church.
Notes: Endured captivity by pirates and exile.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: George of Maleon
Also Known As: Venerable George of Mount Maleon; George of Maleos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: He refused an arranged marriage, withdrew to Mount Maleon in the Peloponnesos to live in asceticism, gathered disciples, and received the gift of prophecy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Joseph the Much-Ailing
Also Known As: Venerable Joseph the Much-Ailing of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves who, gravely ill, vowed to serve the brotherhood if he recovered, and after his healing faithfully fulfilled his vow.
Notes: Of the Kiev Caves (Far Caves).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Zosimas of Vorbozomsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Zosimas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A fifteenth-century Russian ascetic who founded a monastery dedicated to the Annunciation on an island in Lake Vorbozoma near the White Lake region.
Notes: Nov 7 = discovery of his relics.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Zosimas of Palestine
Also Known As: Venerable Zosimas of Palestine; Zosimas the Anchorite
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Zosimas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An aged priest-monk of a monastery near the Jordan who, while keeping a Lenten retreat in the desert, encountered Saint Mary of Egypt; he later gave her Holy Communion and, returning a year afterward, found her reposed and buried her.
Notes: The elder who communed and buried Saint Mary of Egypt. Distinct from Zosimus, Bishop of Syracuse (a different saint).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Pherbutha of Persia
Also Known As: Virgin-Martyr Pherbutha; Phermoutha; Pherbutha, her sister and servant
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Pherbutha, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Sister of the Hieromartyr Simeon, Bishop of Seleucia, she was a consecrated virgin martyred together with her sister and servant during the persecution of the Christians under the Persian emperor Sapor.
Notes: Commemorated with her sister and servant.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Niketas of Serres
Also Known As: New Martyr Niketas of Pojani and Serres; Niketas the Albanian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Niketas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Russian monastery of Saint Panteleimon on Mount Athos who, longing for martyrdom, went to Serres where he confessed Christ before the Turks and was put to death.
Notes: Martyred 1808 under Ottoman rule.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Isidore of Seville
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Isidore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Orphaned young and from a family of saints, he became Archbishop of Seville in Visigothic Spain, helped lead the conversion of the Visigoths from Arianism to Orthodoxy, and was a renowned scholar and encyclopedist.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint. Often dated to 636.
Works by the Saint: Etymologiae
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Theonas of Thessalonica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theonas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint James of Kastoria, he lived in the monasteries of Mount Athos, founded the Monastery of Saint Anastasia the Deliverer-from-Potions, and later became Archbishop of Thessalonica.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: George Mtsire of Georgia
Also Known As: Saint George Mtsire; George the Younger
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian monk and disciple-biographer associated with the Athonite Georgian tradition; honest stub pending fuller sources.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Plato of the Studion
Also Known As: Venerable Plato the Confessor; Plato of Sakkoudion; Plato of the Studium
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Plato, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot and founder of the Sakkoudion monastery in Bithynia and uncle of Saint Theodore the Studite, he suffered imprisonment and persecution as a confessor for opposing the uncanonical marriage of Emperor Constantine VI.
Notes: Uncle and spiritual father of St Theodore the Studite. Reposed c. 814.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Benjamin (Kononov) of Solovki
Also Known As: New Hieromartyr Benjamin Kononov Archimandrite of Solovki
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Benjamin, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An archimandrite of the Solovki Monastery martyred in 1928 during the Soviet persecution of the Church.
Notes: Among the Synaxis of New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martyrs Agathopodes the Deacon and Theodulus the Reader at Thessalonica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Agathopodes and Theodulus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Clergy of the church at Thessalonica — Agathopodes an aged deacon and Theodulus a young reader — who confessed Christ during the persecution under Diocletian and Maximian and were drowned in the sea for refusing to deny their faith.
Notes: OCA groups the two as one paired commemoration.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Publius of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Publius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A desert ascetic of Egypt during the reign of Julian the Apostate, known for his gift of foresight.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theonas of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theonas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic father of Egypt commemorated on this day; little of his life is recorded.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Simeon of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Simeon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic father of Egypt commemorated on this day; little of his life is recorded.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Phorbinus of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Phorbinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic father of Egypt commemorated on this day; little of his life is recorded.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Mark the Anchorite of Athens
Also Known As: Mark of Athens; Mark the Athenian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Mark, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Athenian who studied philosophy in his youth, then after his parents' death withdrew to a remote mountain in the Egyptian desert region, living many decades as a solitary hermit.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Plato the Confessor of Studion
Also Known As: Plato of Studion; Plato of Sakkudion
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessor Plato, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monastic abbot of Constantinople, uncle of Saint Theodore the Studite, who suffered imprisonment and persecution for upholding the veneration of icons and the canons of the Church.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Theodora of Thessalonica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Theodora, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born on Aegina around 812, she married and bore a child; after her husband's death she became a nun in Thessalonica, where through obedience and prayer she received the gift of working miracles (+892).
Notes: Principal feast is August 29 (translation/repose); also commemorated April 5.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martyr Hypomone
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Hypomone, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An early Christian woman martyr commemorated on this day; a liturgical service in her honor survives in a Vatican codex.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Eutychius of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Eutychius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Constantinople (552-565 and 577-582) who presided over the Fifth Ecumenical Council in 553. He was deposed and exiled for refusing to accept the emperor's heterodox views, and was later restored to his see.
Notes: Born at Theium in Phrygia. Presided over the Second Council of Constantinople (Fifth Ecumenical Council).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Saint Platonis of Nisibis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Platonis, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former deaconess who founded a women's monastery in the desert near Nisibis in Syria, renowned for its strict ascetic rule and daily fasting.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: The 120 Martyrs of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Persia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A company of 120 Christians taken captive and martyred under the Persian Shah Sapor (Shapur II) in the mid-fourth century, put to death by fire for confessing Christ.
Notes: Named cluster commemorated as one. Suffered c. 344-347 under Sapor.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Jeremiah and Archilias
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Jeremiah and Archilias, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two early martyrs, Jeremiah and the presbyter Archilias, mentioned by St Gregory the Dialogist. Little of their lives survives.
Notes: Archilias is a presbyter (priest), hence Hieromartyr; Jeremiah a Martyr. Mentioned by St Gregory Dialogus. Region uncertain (Roman context inferred from Gregory the Dialogist).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Gregory the Byzantine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A native of Constantinople who took up monastic life at the Great Lavra of St Athanasius on Mount Athos, where he became a spiritual guide of St Gregory Palamas.
Notes: Spiritual father of St Gregory Palamas; dated to the 14th century on that basis.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable-Martyr Gennadius of Dionysiou
Also Known As: Gennadios of Dionysiou on Mount Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyr Gennadius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Monastery of Dionysiou on Mount Athos, sent to Constantinople accompanying fellow monks who sought martyrdom for Christ.
Notes: Century uncertain from source; left blank. Of Dionysiou Monastery, Mount Athos.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: St. George the Confessor, Bishop of Mytilene
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monastic from his youth who became Bishop of Mytilene on the island of Lesbos and confessed the veneration of the holy icons, suffering persecution under the iconoclasts.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Daniel, Abbot of Pereslavl-Zalessky
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Daniel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born Demetrius to pious parents in Pereslavl-Zalessky around 1460, he embraced the monastic life and became an abbot in his native city.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Calliopius at Pompeiopolis in Cilicia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Calliopius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in Perge of Pamphylia to the pious widow Theoklia, wife of a senator, he was dedicated to God and suffered martyrdom for Christ at Pompeiopolis in Cilicia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Rufinus the Deacon, Aquilina, and 200 Soldiers at Sinope
Also Known As: Rufinus the Deacon; Aquilina; the Two Hundred Soldiers of Sinope
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Rufinus a deacon, the woman Aquilina, and two hundred soldiers who confessed Christ and were martyred together at Sinope during the persecution under Maximinus Daia around 306.
Notes: Named cluster commemorated as one; Rufinus was a deacon.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Herodion of the Seventy
Also Known As: Herodion; Agabus; Rufus; Asyncritus; Phlegon; Hermes; Apostles of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostles Herodion and those with you, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A group of Apostles of the Seventy commemorated together: Herodion (a kinsman and companion of the Apostle Paul), Agabus the prophet, Rufus, Asyncritus, Phlegon, and Hermes. They preached the Gospel in various lands and several died as martyrs.
Notes: Also commemorated among the Synaxis of the Seventy Apostles (Jan 4).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Niphon, Bishop of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Niphon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves Monastery known for ascetic struggle, he was consecrated Bishop of Novgorod and defended the unity and order of the Church in 12th-century Rus'.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Rufus the Obedient of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Rufus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit of the Kiev Caves distinguished for obedience, labor, and strict fasting. He reposed and was buried in the Far Caves.
Notes: Also commemorated Aug 28 with the Synaxis of the Saints of the Kiev Far Caves.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Pausilippus of Heraclea in Thrace
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Pausilippus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Denounced as a Christian under the Emperor Hadrian, he endured beatings and persecution yet remained steadfast in confessing Christ.
Notes: Suffered at Heraclea in Thrace (region near Constantinople); no exact Thrace region term in the controlled vocabulary.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Celestine, Pope of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Celestine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Pope of Rome (422-432), a learned defender of Orthodoxy who supported the condemnation of Nestorius at the Council of Ephesus and sent missionaries, including Palladius, to Ireland and Britain.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martyr Eupsychius of Caesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Eupsychius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Caesarea in Cappadocia who, during the reign of Julian the Apostate, took part in destroying a pagan temple and was tortured and beheaded for his confession of Christ.
Notes: Distinct from the 2nd-century Martyr Eupsychios of Caesarea (feast Sep 7); this is the 4th-century martyr under Julian the Apostate.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Desan of Persia and Companions
Also Known As: Desan, Bishop of Persia; Mariabus the Presbyter; Abdiesus; the 270 (272) Martyrs of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Desan and Companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop Desan of Persia, the presbyter Mariabus, Abdiesus, and a large company of about 270 captive Christians were imprisoned and put to death under Shah Sapor (Shapur) II for refusing to renounce Christ.
Notes: Listed in the synaxarion as a single commemoration of Bishop Desan together with his companions and the some 270 (or 272) who suffered with them.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable-Martyr Bademus of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyr Bademus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born to a wealthy family in the Persian city of Bithlapata, Bademus renounced his possessions, founded a monastery, and lived as its archimandrite; imprisoned under Sapor II, he was slain for refusing to deny Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hierarch Acacius of Amida
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Acacius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Amida in Mesopotamia in the fifth century who melted down and sold his church's gold and silver vessels to ransom Persian prisoners of war, feeding them and sending them home, an act of charity that moved the Persian king to relent in his persecution of Christians.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: The Martyrs in Africa
Also Known As: The Massylitan Martyrs; Martyrs of Masyla in Africa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Africa, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A company of martyrs of Masyla in North Africa, on whose feast Saint Augustine is recorded to have preached; their names and the precise account of their suffering are not preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; the synaxarion preserves the commemoration but few details. Century approximate.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: New Martyrs Raphael, Nicholas and Irene of Lesbos
Also Known As: Raphael of Lesbos; Nicholas of Lesbos; Irene of Lesbos; Eleni (Susanna) of Lesbos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyrs Raphael, Nicholas and Irene, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Saint Raphael, an abbot, the deacon Nicholas, and the young girl Irene, together with her family, were tortured and slain by the Turks on Bright Tuesday in 1463 on the island of Lesbos; forgotten for nearly five centuries, they were rediscovered in the twentieth century through visions and relics.
Notes: Their principal commemoration falls on Bright Tuesday (movable); the fixed date Apr 9 is also kept. Eleni (Susanna), said to be a cousin of Saint Irene, is commemorated with them.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs James the Presbyter, and Azadanes and Abdiesus the Deacons, of Persia
Also Known As: Jacob the Presbyter; Azadanes; Abdicius; Abdiesus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr James and companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest and two deacons of the Church in Persia who were arrested with Bishop Akepsimas and endured severe tortures for confessing Christ during the persecution under Shapur II, around the year 380.
Notes: Arrested together with Bishop Akepsimas, whose principal feast (with the martyrs Joseph and Aithalas) is Nov 3.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs of the Kvabtakhevi Monastery in Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Kvabtakhevi, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Monastics and faithful of the Kvabtakhevi Monastery in Georgia who were slain when the armies of Tamerlane invaded and devastated the country's churches and monasteries during the reign of King Bagrat V.
Notes: Collective martyrdom commemorated as one feast; a named cluster of monastic martyrs.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Gregory V, Patriarch of Constantinople
Also Known As: Gregory V of Constantinople; Gregorios E
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Ecumenical Patriarch of Constantinople, born in Dimitsana in the Peloponnese in 1746. He was hanged at the gate of the Patriarchate on Pascha 1821 in Ottoman reprisal during the Greek War of Independence, and is venerated as a hieromartyr and ethnomartyr.
Notes: Glorified as a saint by the Church of Greece; venerated as a hieromartyr/ethnomartyr. CANONIZATION NOTE: a modern (1821) Greek martyr — flagged for clergy/source review per guardrails.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; Wikipedia, Gregory V of Constantinople

Name: Hieromartyr Antipas of Pergamum
Also Known As: Antipas of Pergamum; Saint Antipas; Antipas, Bishop of Pergamum
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Antipas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint John the Theologian, Antipas served as bishop of Pergamum during the persecution under Nero and was martyred for refusing to offer sacrifice to idols.
Notes: Mentioned in Revelation 2:13 as Christ's faithful witness. Widely invoked for the healing of toothache and dental ailments.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable James of Zheleznoborov
Also Known As: James of Zheleznoborov; Iakov of Zheleznoborov; James, Abbot of Zheleznoborov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father James, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born into a noble family in the late 14th century, he became a disciple of Saint Sergius of Radonezh and founded a monastic community at Zheleznoborov near Galich in the Kostroma region.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Barsanuphius of Tver
Also Known As: Barsanuphius of Tver; Varsonofy of Tver; Barsanuphius, Bishop of Tver
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Barsanuphius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in 1495 in Serpukhov, he was taken captive by Crimean Tatars in his youth, later became a monk, and served as bishop of Tver.
Notes: Principal feast (with St Gurias of Kazan) is observed Oct 4; also commemorated among the Kazan hierarchs. Oct 4 = uncovering of his relics at Kazan (1595).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Processus and Martinian of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Processus and Martinian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Pagan guards at the Mamertine prison in Rome who were converted through the witness of the Christians held there and were themselves martyred for the faith.
Notes: Also commemorated in the Western calendar on Jul 2.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Pharmuthius the Anchorite of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Pharmuthius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A desert anchorite of fourth-century Egypt who carried food to Saint John, an ascetic dwelling in a well at the same desert.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable John the Disciple of Gregory of Decapolis
Also Known As: John, Disciple of Gregory of Decapolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monastic disciple of Saint Gregory the Decapolite, with whom he shared the ascetic life.
Notes: His fuller commemoration is noted on Apr 18.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Callinicus of Cernica
Also Known As: Callinicus of Cernica; Calinic of Cernica; Callinicus, Bishop of Ramnicu Valcea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Callinicus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in Bucharest in 1787, he was a monk and abbot of Cernica Monastery and later served as bishop of Ramnicu Valcea in Romania.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Basil the Confessor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Parium who firmly defended the veneration of holy icons during the Iconoclast persecution and suffered for refusing to sign an edict against them.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Zeno of Verona
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Zeno, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A native of North Africa who became Bishop of Verona in Italy and shepherded his flock through times of persecution; some accounts honor him as a martyr.
Notes: Martyrdom is attested by some sources and disputed by others; OCA titles him Hieromartyr. Reposed c. 371.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Saint Isaac the Syrian of Spoleto
Also Known As: Isaac of Spoleto; Isaac the Syrian Abbot of Spoleto
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Isaac, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Syrian monk who fled persecution and settled near Spoleto in Italy, where he founded a monastery and was renowned for ascetic labors and miracles.
Notes: Distinct from St Isaac the Syrian of Nineveh (the ascetical writer).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyrs Menas, David, and John of Palestine
Also Known As: Menas of Palestine; David of Palestine; John of Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Monastic Martyrs Menas, David, and John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three Palestinian monks who were shot with arrows by Arab raiders following the fall of Jerusalem, receiving the crown of martyrdom.
Notes: Listed as a single commemoration of three named martyrs.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Anthusa of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Anthusa, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A daughter of the Iconoclast emperor Constantine Copronymus who renounced royal life to become a nun, enduring her father's hostility while remaining steadfast in venerating the holy icons.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Athanasia of Aegina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Athanasia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A pious Greek woman, twice widowed, who became a nun and later abbess of a monastery on the island of Aegina, renowned for her charity, austerity, and miracles after her repose.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Acacius the Younger of Mount Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Acacius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of Mount Athos who lived at the Kavsokalyvia skete near the cell of Saint Maximus, attaining a great old age through a life of strict asceticism and prayer.
Notes: Reposed April 12, 1730; this date is his feast.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martin the Confessor, Pope of Rome
Also Known As: Martin I; Martin the Confessor, Pope of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Martin, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Pope of Rome who convened the Lateran Council of 649 and condemned the Monothelite heresy, rejecting the imperial Ecthesis and Typos. Arrested by order of the emperor, he was tried and exiled to Cherson, where he died in 655 a confessor for the Orthodox faith.
Notes: Byzantine-rite feast is principally Apr 14; the Apr 13 commemoration is listed in the OCA/Western reckoning. The last Pope of Rome venerated as a confessor for the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Hieromartyr Artemon of Laodicea
Also Known As: Artemon of Laodikeia; Artemon the Presbyter
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Artemon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Presbyter of Laodicea in Syria who served as a reader for sixteen years before his ordination. Renowned for his zeal in destroying idols and preaching Christ, he was seized and martyred during the persecution of Diocletian.
Notes: Distinct from Artemon, Bishop of Seleucia (Mar 24).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Crescens of Myra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Crescens, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A man of illustrious family in Myra of Lycia who openly preached against paganism and urged his fellow citizens to turn to Christ. Brought before the prefect, he confessed his faith and was put to death by fire.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Thomais of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Thomais, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Christian woman of Alexandria, raised in piety and married to a fisherman, who was slain for refusing the unchaste advances of her father-in-law. She is venerated as a protector against the assaults of unclean passions.
Notes: Commemorated Apr 13 (and Apr 14 in some calendars). Invoked for help in struggles against carnal temptation.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martyrs Maximus, Quintilian and Dadas
Also Known As: Maximus; Quintilian; Quintillian; Dadas; Martyrs of Ozovia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Maximus, Quintilian and Dadas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three martyrs who suffered at Ozovia in Macedonia during the persecution under the emperors Diocletian and Maximian. Refusing to sacrifice to the idols, they confessed Christ and were beheaded.
Notes: Suffered at Ozovia in Macedonia; no Macedonia region term in the controlled vocabulary, so Greece is used as the nearest. Also commemorated Apr 28 as the Martyrs of Dorostolum in Moesia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martyrs Anthony, John, and Eustathius of Vilnius
Also Known As: Anthony of Vilnius; John of Vilnius; Eustathius of Vilnius; Martyrs of Vilna; Kruglec; Kumets; Nezhilo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Anthony, John, and Eustathius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three kinsmen at the court of the Lithuanian Great Prince Algirdas in Vilnius who embraced Christianity and refused to return to pagan customs. For confessing Christ they were imprisoned and hanged in 1347, becoming the first martyrs of Lithuania.
Notes: One row for the named cluster of three; individual names listed in Also Known As.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Ardalion the Actor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Ardalion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An actor who, while mockingly portraying a Christian on stage during the persecutions of the early fourth century, was moved to declare that he truly was a Christian. He confessed Christ before the crowd and was martyred.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Azades the Eunuch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Azades, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wealthy eunuch in the household of King Shapur II of Persia who was put to death for confessing Christ, together with a thousand other Christians during the great Persian persecution.
Notes: Commemorated with the 1,000 Martyrs of Persia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostles Aristarchus, Pudens, and Trophimus of the Seventy
Also Known As: Aristarchus; Pudens; Pudis; Trophimus; Apostles of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostles Aristarchus, Pudens, and Trophimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three of the Seventy Apostles and companions of the Apostle Paul who labored with him in preaching the Gospel and shared in his sufferings. According to tradition they were beheaded at Rome during the persecution of Nero.
Notes: Named cluster; also commemorated among the Synaxis of the Seventy Apostles (Jan 4), and listed Apr 14 or Apr 15 in some calendars. Aristarchus is additionally commemorated Sep 27.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; orthodoxwiki.org

Name: Martyrs Basilissa and Anastasia of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Basilissa and Anastasia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Roman women converted to Christianity by the Apostles Peter and Paul, they devoted themselves to caring for the bodies of the martyrs during Nero's persecution. For their confession of Christ they were tortured and put to death at Rome.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Sukias and his Sixteen Companions in Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Sukias and companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Court dignitaries of Caucasian Albania who came to faith in Christ through the preaching of Chrysos, a disciple of the Apostle Thaddeus. Withdrawing to Mount Sukaketi as ascetics, they were martyred by fire (ca. 100-130) under the pagan ruler Datianos.
Notes: Named cluster commemorated together; Georgian and Armenian tradition. Numbered at sixteen companions.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; orthochristian.com

Name: Martyr Savva (Sabbas) the Goth of Wallachia
Also Known As: Sabbas the Goth; Savva the Goth
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Savva, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Goth of the Buzau valley in what is now Romania, baptized as a Christian, known for his strict and temperate life. During the persecution of King Athanaric he refused to eat food sacrificed to idols and was drowned in the river Musaeus in 372.
Notes: Principal feast observed April 24 in the Eastern Orthodox calendar (April 12 in the West); OCA also commemorates him April 15.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Saint Ephraim the Great of Atsquri
Also Known As: Eprem of Atsquri; Ephraim of Atsquri
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Ephraim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint Grigol of Khandzta, he was consecrated bishop of Atsquri and administered the diocese for forty years. He labored for the autocephaly of the Georgian Church and was endowed with gifts of prophecy, wonderworking, and healing.
Notes: Principal feast observed April 28 in the Georgian Church; OCA commemorates him April 15.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin-Martyrs Agape, Irene, and Chionia of Illyria
Also Known As: Agape; Irene; Chionia; Agape of Aquileia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Virgin-Martyrs Agape, Irene, and Chionia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three Christian sisters of the Illyrian region who refused to renounce Christ or surrender the holy Scriptures during the persecution of Diocletian. Agape and Chionia were burned alive, and Irene was martyred shortly after.
Notes: One cluster commemoration of the three sisters martyred together (c. 304).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Martyrs Leonidas and his Companions of Corinth
Also Known As: Leonidas; Chariessa; Nike; Galina; Kalista; Nunechia; Basilissa; Theodora; Irene
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Leonidas and companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A company of martyrs who suffered at Corinth in the year 258, cast into the sea for confessing Christ. According to tradition they walked upon the waters singing hymns before receiving their crowns.
Notes: One cluster commemoration: Leonidas with the women martyrs Chariessa, Nike, Galina, Kalista, Nunechia, Basilissa, Theodora, and Irene.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic-Martyr Christopher of Dionysiou
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable-Martyr Christopher, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Monastery of Dionysiou on Mount Athos who suffered martyrdom for Christ. Details of his life are not preserved in the synaxarion.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Niketas
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Niketas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest-martyr commemorated on this day; no further biographical details are preserved in the synaxarion.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Symeon of Persia
Also Known As: Symeon bar Sabba'e; Simeon of Seleucia-Ctesiphon; Symeon the Hieromartyr; Abdechalas; Ananias; Usthazanes; Pusicius; Azad the Eunuch; the Martyrs with him
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Symeon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Symeon, bishop of Seleucia-Ctesiphon and head of the Church in Persia, was put to death together with a great company of clergy and faithful during the persecution of the Persian emperor Shapur II for refusing to deny Christ and worship the sun. A named cluster commemorated as one feast.
Notes: Commemorated together with his companion martyrs, including the priests Abdechalas (Abdella) and Ananias, the eunuch Azad, Usthazanes, and Pusicius, among many others martyred under Shapur II.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org (Shemon bar Sabbae)

Name: Acacius of Melitene
Also Known As: Akakios of Melitene; Acacius, Bishop of Melitene
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Acacius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Acacius, bishop of Melitene in Armenia, was dedicated to God from childhood and ordained by his predecessor Otreius. He took part in the Third Ecumenical Council at Ephesus in 431, standing with St Cyril of Alexandria against the heresy of Nestorius, and reposed in peace.
Notes: Sep 15 = uncovering of his relics.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Agapitus of Rome
Also Known As: Agapetus I; Agapitus, Pope of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Agapitus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Agapitus, Pope of Rome, was a zealous defender of the Orthodox faith who was sent on a mission to Constantinople and contended against heresy there before his repose in 536.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Adrian of Corinth
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Adrian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Adrian, a Christian of Corinth, was imprisoned and put to death for refusing to offer sacrifice to idols during the persecution under the emperor Decius.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Makarios of Corinth
Also Known As: Macarius Notaras of Corinth; Makarios Notaras; Michael Notaras
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Makarios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Makarios Notaras, archbishop of Corinth, was a leader of the Kollyvades movement and, together with St Nikodemos of the Holy Mountain, gathered and edited the Philokalia, the great anthology of Orthodox spiritual writings. He reposed in 1805.
Works by the Saint: The Philokalia (compiler, with St Nikodemos)
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org (Macarius of Corinth)

Name: Zosimas of Solovki
Also Known As: Zosima of Solovki; Zosimas the Abbot of Solovki
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Zosimas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Zosimas, abbot of Solovki, established cenobitic monastic life on the remote Solovki islands in the Russian North and became one of the great luminaries of northern Russian monasticism. He reposed in 1478.
Notes: Principal feast is Aug 8 (his repose); Apr 17 is a secondary commemoration. Likely already present in the database under his principal feast.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org (Zosima of Solovki)

Name: Martyr Victor of Nicomedia and Companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A company of martyrs of Nicomedia who suffered under the emperor Diocletian during the persecution of Christians.
Notes: Commemorated with his companions Zoticus, Acindynus, Zeno, Severian, and Caesarius.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Cosmas, Bishop of Chalcedon, and Auxentius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Cosmas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Cosmas received monastic tonsure in his youth and was later consecrated Bishop of Chalcedon, suffering with his fellow ascetic Auxentius for the veneration of the holy icons during the iconoclast oppression.
Notes: Commemorated together with his fellow ascetic Auxentius.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr John the New of Epirus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New-Martyr John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in Ioannina in Epirus and orphaned young, he kept a trade stall in Constantinople and was martyred for refusing to deny the Orthodox faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Euthymius, Enlightener of Karelia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Euthymius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born in the second half of the fourteenth century, he travelled north around the year 1400 to live as an anchorite and brought the Christian faith to Karelia and Finland.
Notes: Commemorated together with the righteous Anthony and Felix.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Basil Ratishvili of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A thirteenth-century Georgian monastic, uncle of Catholicos Ekvtime III, who labored with the Georgian fathers at the Iveron Monastery on Mount Athos and was endowed with the gift of prophecy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: John of the Ancient Caves
Also Known As: John of the Old Caves; John the Hermit
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of the Lavra of Saint Chariton near Bethlehem who lived in the cave of the Old (Ancient) Lavra and is reckoned among its venerable fathers.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Christopher, Theonas, and Anthony
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Christopher, Theonas, and Anthony, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Soldiers at Rome under Diocletian who, moved by the witness of the Great Martyr George, confessed Christ and were martyred for the faith.
Notes: Listed as one commemoration; individual names kept in Also Known As.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Paphnutius of Jerusalem
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Paphnutius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of the Jerusalem region who endured torture and was beheaded during the pagan persecutions of the early Church.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: George the Confessor, Bishop of Antioch in Pisidia
Also Known As: George of Pisidia; George the Confessor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk made bishop of Antioch in Pisidia who, as a defender of the holy icons, refused to comply with the iconoclast council and was exiled, confessing the faith into old age.
Notes: Iconodule confessor of the iconoclast era; exiled under Constantine V and again resisted iconoclasm under Leo V.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Tryphon, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Tryphon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk renowned for meekness who was made Patriarch of Constantinople (928-931); deceived into resigning when the emperor's young son came of age, he returned to his monastery and reposed as a simple monk in 933.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; orthodoxwiki.org

Name: Nikephoros, Abbot of Catabad
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nikephoros, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wealthy nobleman who renounced his possessions to embrace monastic life and became abbot of the monastery of Catabad.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Agathangelus of Esphigmenou
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Agathangelus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former sailor who became a monk of Esphigmenou Monastery on Mount Athos and was martyred for refusing to embrace Islam.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Simeon of Philotheou
Also Known As: Simeon the Barefoot; Symeon of Philotheou
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Simeon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Tonsured a monk at the age of fifteen at Philotheou Monastery on Mount Athos, he led a life of strict asceticism, going barefoot in his labors.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Theodore Trichinas
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born to wealthy parents in Constantinople, he forsook his comforts for a strict ascetic life in a Thracian monastery, wearing only a rough hair shirt, from which he received his surname.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Alexander of Oshevensk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A fifteenth-century Russian monastic who founded the Dormition Monastery at Oshevensk and labored to spread Christian enlightenment through the Kargopol region.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Gabriel of Bialystok
Also Known As: Gabriel of Zabludow; Gabriel of Zverki
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Gabriel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young child of the Bialystok region (in the historic Grand Duchy of Lithuania) who died and is venerated as a martyr in the Russian, Belarusian, and Polish Orthodox tradition; he was glorified in 1820 and his relics are a focus of local pilgrimage.
Notes: Flagged for clergy/editorial and source review before publication: the historical circumstances of his death are contested; only the fact of his death as a child and his canonical veneration as a martyr are stated here.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Gregory of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Patriarch of Antioch remembered for his pastoral leadership of the Church of Antioch.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Anastasius of Sinai
Also Known As: Anastasius the Sinaite; Abbot of Sinai
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anastasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Raised in piety from his youth, he became a renowned ascetic and abbot on Mount Sinai, esteemed as a teacher and defender of the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Betranion of Tomis
Also Known As: Bretanion; Vetranion; Bishop of Lesser Scythia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Betranion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Tomis in Lesser Scythia who resisted the Arian pressure of the Emperor Valens, defending the Nicene faith and enduring a brief exile before being restored by popular demand.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Theotimus of Tomis
Also Known As: Bishop of Lesser Scythia; Theotimus the Philosopher
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theotimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Tomis in Lesser Scythia, honored as a teacher and held by tradition to have instructed Saints John Cassian and Germanus in the monastic life.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Joseph of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A saint venerated in the Serbian Orthodox tradition; few details of his life are recorded.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Zacchaeus the Publican
Also Known As: Apostle Zacchaeus; Zacchaeus, Bishop of Caesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Zacchaeus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The wealthy chief tax-collector of Jericho who climbed a sycamore tree to see Christ and, receiving Him into his house, repented and made fourfold restitution; tradition counts him among the Seventy and as a bishop of Caesarea.
Notes: Also commemorated among the Seventy Apostles (Jan 4).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Athanasius of Meteora
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Athanasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born Andronikos in Hypate, he founded the Great Meteoron monastery atop the rocks of Meteora in Thessaly and gave it a cenobitic rule patterned on Mount Athos; his disciple and successor Joasaph co-built the community.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Hieromartyr Januarius, Bishop of Benevento, and his Companions
Also Known As: Januarius of Benevento; Gennarius; Proculus the Deacon; Sossius the Deacon; Faustus the Deacon; Desiderius the Reader; Eutychius; Acution; Acutius; Martyrs at Pozzuoli; Martyrs at Puteoli
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Januarius and your companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Januarius, Bishop of Benevento, was martyred with his companions near Pozzuoli around the year 305 during the persecution of Diocletian. His fellow sufferers included the deacons Proculus, Sossius, and Faustus, Desiderius the reader, and the laymen Eutychius and Acution.
Notes: Cluster of seven martyrs commemorated as one. Januarius is widely venerated in the West (San Gennaro of Naples).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Theodore of Perge in Pamphylia, and his Companions
Also Known As: Theodore of Perge; Philippa (his mother); Dioscorus; Socrates; Dionysius; Martyrs of Perge
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Theodore and your companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Theodore of Perge in Pamphylia suffered for Christ during the reign of Antoninus Pius (138-161), together with his mother Philippa and the martyrs Dioscorus, Socrates, and Dionysius. Socrates and Dionysius were soldiers who were converted by witnessing his steadfastness.
Notes: Cluster commemorated as one; principal feast Apr 21, with a secondary commemoration of 'Theodore and those with him' on Apr 19. Vocation 'Soldier' applies to the companions Socrates and Dionysius, not the whole group.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Isaac, Apollos, and Quadratus of Nicomedia
Also Known As: Isaac of Nicomedia; Apollos of Nicomedia; Apollo; Quadratus of Nicomedia; Codratus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Isaac, Apollos, and Quadratus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Isaac, Apollos, and Quadratus were court officials at Nicomedia who, having witnessed the courageous suffering of the Great Martyr George, openly confessed Christ and were martyred under Diocletian.
Notes: Cluster of three commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Maximian, Patriarch of Constantinople
Also Known As: Maximianus of Constantinople; Maximian, Archbishop of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Maximian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Maximian, born in Rome to wealthy and pious parents, lived an ascetic life and was ordained presbyter. He became Patriarch of Constantinople in 431 following the deposition of Nestorius, and governed the Church peaceably until his repose on April 21, 434.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theodore the Sykeote, Bishop of Anastasiopolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of Sykeon in Galatia who became bishop of Anastasiopolis, renowned for his strict monastic life, gift of healing, and casting out of unclean spirits; he reposed in 613.
Notes: Drawn from the OCA synaxarion.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostles Nathanael, Apelles, Luke, and Clement of the Seventy
Also Known As: Apostle Nathanael of the Seventy; Apelles; Luke (Loukios) of the Seventy; Clement of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostles, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Members of the Seventy commemorated together, who labored in the apostolic preaching of the Gospel in the first generation of the Church.
Notes: Apostles of the Seventy commemorated together on Apr 22. The 'Luke' here is Loukios (Lucius) of the Seventy, distinct from the Evangelist Luke (Oct 18, OS-0006). Nathanael is traditionally identified with the Apostle Bartholomew. Grouped as one commemoration; flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Vitalius of Gaza
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Vitalius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Gaza who in old age went to Alexandria, laboring by day and spending his nights visiting fallen women to turn them to repentance through prayer and alms; he bore slander in silence and was vindicated after his repose.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Savva (Trlajic) of Serbia
Also Known As: Sava Trlajic; Bishop of Gornji Karlovac
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Savva, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Serbian bishop of Gornji Karlovac, seized and put to death in 1941 during the wartime persecution; numbered among the Serbian new-martyrs.
Notes: Modern glorification; flagged for clergy/source review. Also listed among the Serbian hieromartyrs on Jun 4 (as Bishop of Upper Karlovac). Also commemorated Jul 4 (as Bishop of Gornji Karlovac).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Platon of Banja Luka
Also Known As: Platon Jovanovic; Platon the New Martyr, Bishop of Banja Luka
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Platon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Serbian bishop of Banja Luka in Bosnia, arrested and martyred in 1941; glorified among the new-martyrs and confessors.
Notes: Modern glorification; flagged for clergy/source review. Also commemorated Jul 4.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Alexandra the Empress
Also Known As: Alexandra of Rome; wife of Diocletian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Alexandra, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Wife of the emperor Diocletian who, moved by the steadfast endurance of the Great Martyr George, openly confessed Christ and was condemned to death, receiving the crown of martyrdom.
Notes: Commemorated together with the Great Martyr George (OS-0012).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Anatolius and Protoleon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Anatolius and Protoleon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Soldiers who, witnessing the courage and miracles of the Great Martyr George, confessed Christ before the emperor and were beheaded.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Lazarus of Bulgaria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Lazarus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Bulgarian shepherd from the region of Gabrovo who suffered martyrdom under Ottoman rule in 1802, refusing to deny Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Holy Martyr Polychronia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Polychronia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Honored in the tradition surrounding the Great Martyr George as his mother, who confessed Christ and died a martyr.
Notes: Known from the tradition of St George's life; flagged for source review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Theocharis and Apostolos of Arta
Also Known As: Theocharis of Arta; Apostolos of Arta
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Theocharis and Apostolos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Local new-martyrs of Arta in Epirus who confessed Christ under Ottoman rule and were put to death.
Notes: Obscure local saints of Arta; flagged for clergy/source review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Savva Stratelates and 70 Soldiers
Also Known As: Savva the General; Sabbas Stratelates; the 70 soldiers with him
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Savva, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian military commander under the emperor Aurelian who, with seventy soldiers who believed through his witness, was tortured and drowned for Christ.
Notes: Distinct from Savva the Goth (OS-1000) and from the Kiev-Caves recluses below.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Savva the Recluse of the Kiev Far Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Savva, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of the Far Caves of Kiev, remembered for his obedience and wonderworking grace; his relics rest in the Caves.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Alexius the Recluse of the Kiev Far Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Alexius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A recluse of the Far Caves of Kiev who lived in strict asceticism; his relics were later uncovered.
Notes: Also commemorated Aug 28 with the Synaxis of the Saints of the Far Caves.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Valentine and Pasikrates
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Valentine and Pasikrates, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two young Christian soldiers at Durostorum in Moesia who openly refused to offer pagan sacrifice and were beheaded for Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Eusebius, Neon, Leontius, Longinus, and Companions at Nicomedia
Also Known As: Eusebius; Neon; Leontius; Longinus; and those with them
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Eusebius, Neon, Leontius, and Longinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A company of believers at Nicomedia who, moved by the martyrdom of the Great Martyr George, confessed Christ and were put to death under Diocletian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Thomas the Fool-for-Christ of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Thomas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk who gathered alms for his brotherhood and took up the hard path of folly for Christ, bearing insult and contempt; he was glorified by God after his repose at Antioch.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Elizabeth the Wonderworker of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Elizabeth, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Dedicated to God from childhood, she became an abbess in Constantinople renowned for fasting, ascetic struggle, and miraculous healings.
Notes: Dating uncertain (5th-6th c.); Era given, century left blank.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Iorest, Metropolitan of Ardeal, Confessor of Romania
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Iorest, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Metropolitan of Transylvania who defended the Orthodox faith amid Calvinist pressure, for which he was deposed and imprisoned; honored as a confessor.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Savva Brancovici, Metropolitan of Ardeal, Confessor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Savva, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Metropolitan of Transylvania, of a Serbian family, who strengthened the Orthodox under heavy pressure and suffered imprisonment; honored as a confessor.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr and Confessor Elijah the Wallachian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Elijah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Wallachian hieromartyr and confessor commemorated on this day; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; the OCA entry gives no biographical detail. Flagged for clergy/source review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Branko Dobrosavljevic the New Martyr
Also Known As: Branko Dobrosavljevic; parish priest of Veljun
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Branko, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian priest martyred in 1941 during the wartime persecution; numbered among the new-martyrs.
Notes: The OCA Apr 24 list shows him under two phrasings ('Branko the New Martyr' and 'Branko, parish priest of Veljun/Veljusa'); recorded here as one person. Modern glorification; flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Joseph the Confessor of Maramures
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop in Maramures who defended the Orthodox Christians of the region against pressure from the Latin authorities; honored as a confessor.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Holy Apostle and Evangelist Mark
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle and Evangelist Mark, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Seventy and the writer of the second Gospel; a companion of the apostles Peter and Paul and nephew of Barnabas, he founded and led the Church of Alexandria, where he was martyred.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Basil of Poiana Marului
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monastic elder of Poiana Marului in Romania, a teacher of the prayer of the heart and spiritual guide in the hesychast renewal associated with St Paisius Velichkovsky.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Sylvester, Abbot of Obnora
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Sylvester, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk in the tradition of St Sergius of Radonezh who withdrew into the wilderness of the Obnora River, where he gathered a brotherhood; a contemporary of St Alexis of Moscow.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Basil, Bishop of Amasea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Amasea in Pontus, put to death under the emperor Licinius; he is remembered together with the maiden Glaphyra whom he had sheltered.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Stephen, Bishop of Perm
Also Known As: Stephen the Enlightener of Perm; Apostle to the Zyrians
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A missionary bishop to the Permian (Zyrian) people, for whom he devised an alphabet and translated the divine services, establishing the Church among them; he reposed in 1396.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Virgin Martyr Glaphyra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Glaphyra, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A maidservant of the imperial household who fled the emperor Licinius's advances to preserve her chastity, taking refuge near Amasea; she is honored as a righteous virgin.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Joannicius of Devich
Also Known As: Joannicius of Devic; Ioannikije of Devic
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Joannicius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic who founded the monastery of Devic in Kosovo, where his relics became a source of healing for the sick.
Notes: Principal feast of his repose is Dec 2; also commemorated Apr 26.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: 6,000 Martyrs of the David-Gareji Monastery
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Venerable Martyrs of David-Gareji, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The monks of the David-Gareji lavra in Georgia, slain in 1616 during the invasion of Shah Abbas I when, on the night of Pascha, they refused to renounce Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Simeon, Kinsman of the Lord, Bishop of Jerusalem
Also Known As: Simeon of Jerusalem; son of Cleopas; second Bishop of Jerusalem
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Simeon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A kinsman of the Lord and son of Cleopas who succeeded the Apostle James as second bishop of Jerusalem; he was crucified in extreme old age under the emperor Trajan.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Stephen, Abbot of the Kiev Far Caves and Bishop of Vladimir-in-Volhynia
Also Known As: Stephen of the Caves; Stephen of Vladimir in Volhynia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple and successor of St Theodosius as abbot of the Kiev Caves, who founded the Blachernae monastery and later served as bishop of Vladimir in Volhynia; he reposed in 1094.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Eulogius the Hospitable of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Eulogius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A poor stonecutter of the Thebaid renowned for receiving and feeding strangers; the desert fathers recount how sudden wealth tested, and nearly overcame, his hospitality.
Notes: Dating approximate.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostles Jason and Sosipater, the Virgin Kerkyra, and Companions
Also Known As: Jason; Sosipater; Kerkyra; Cercyra; the martyrs of Corfu
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostles Jason and Sosipater, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Disciples of the Apostle Paul, of the Seventy, who preached on Corfu, where the king's daughter Kerkyra and others believed and were crowned with martyrdom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Cyril, Bishop of Turov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Cyril, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned bishop of Turov in the lands of Rus', celebrated for his sermons, prayers, and liturgical hymns; he withdrew for a time as a recluse before his episcopate.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Nine Martyrs at Cyzicus
Also Known As: Theognes; Rufus; Antipater; Theostichus; Artemas; Magnus; Theodotus; Thaumasius; Philemon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Nine Martyrs of Cyzicus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Nine Christians of differing ages and walks of life who, in a time of persecution at Cyzicus, openly confessed Christ together and were martyred for refusing to sacrifice to idols.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Memnon the Wonderworker
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Memnon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A desert ascetic who became abbot of a monastery, remembered for his spiritual guidance and for the gift of wonderworking.
Notes: Dating uncertain; Era given, century left blank.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Diodorus and Rhodopianus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Diodorus and Rhodopianus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two deacons who spread the faith at Aphrodisias in Caria and were stoned to death during the persecution under Diocletian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Basil, Bishop of Zakholmsk
Also Known As: Basil of Ostrog; Basil of Zahumlje; Wonderworker of Ostrog
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian bishop who labored in Herzegovina and withdrew to the mountain of Ostrog, where his relics remain a great source of healing for pilgrims of every faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Nectarius of Optina
Also Known As: Nectarius Tikhonov; Elder Nectarius of Optina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nectarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The last of the great elders of Optina, a hieromonk of deep humility and clairvoyant counsel who guided many through the upheavals of the early twentieth century.
Notes: Among the Optina Elders; modern glorification; flagged for clergy/source review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs of Lazeti
Also Known As: the Laz Martyrs; Martyrs of Lazica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Lazeti, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A company of Orthodox Christians of the Lazeti region who suffered martyrdom for the faith during a time of persecution.
Notes: Regional group; individual names not preserved in the OCA entry. Honest stub; flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Endellion, Recluse of Cornwall
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Endellion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A holy woman of Cornwall in the early British church who lived as a recluse and is venerated as a local saint.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; the OCA entry gives no detailed life. Honest stub.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle James, Brother of Saint John the Theologian
Also Known As: James, son of Zebedee; James the Greater
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle James, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Twelve, a fisherman of Galilee and brother of John the Theologian, called by the Lord among the first; he was the first of the apostles to be martyred, beheaded by Herod Agrippa.
Notes: Son of Zebedee; distinct from James the Brother of the Lord (Oct 23) and James son of Alphaeus (Oct 9).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Donatus, Bishop of Euroea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Donatus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Euroea in Epirus during the reign of Theodosius the Great, remembered for his pastoral care and for many miracles of healing and deliverance.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Maximus of Ephesus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Maximus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian who confessed Christ at Ephesus and was put to the sword for the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Ignatius Brianchaninov, Bishop of the Caucasus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Ignatius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian bishop of the nineteenth century and one of the great spiritual writers of his age, whose works gathered the teaching of the fathers on prayer and the ascetic life for modern readers.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Argyra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Argyra, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young married woman of Proussa who, falsely accused after refusing a Turk's advances, endured long imprisonment and torment rather than deny Christ, and died in prison.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Prophet Jeremiah
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Jeremiah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the great prophets of the Old Testament, of priestly family from Anathoth, who called Judah to repentance before the Babylonian destruction of Jerusalem and suffered much for his witness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Paphnutius, Abbot of Borov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Paphnutius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian monastic founder and abbot of Borovsk known for his strict asceticism and discernment; teacher of St Joseph of Volotsk.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Euthymius of Mount Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Euthymius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Mount Athos, born in the Peloponnese, who after a fall returned in repentance and publicly confessed Christ at Constantinople, receiving the crown of martyrdom in 1814.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Ignatius of Mount Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Ignatius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Mount Athos, born in Bulgaria, who confessed Christ before the Turks at Constantinople and was hanged in 1814.
Notes: Distinct from St Ignatius Brianchaninov (Apr 30).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Acacius of Mount Athos and Seres
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Acacius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Christian of Macedonia who, having denied the faith, repented and became a monk of Mount Athos, then confessed Christ openly and was martyred in 1816.
