Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Macarius, Metropolitan of Kiev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Metropolitan of Kiev who was seized and slain by raiding Tatars in 1497 while visiting his flock, sealing his pastoral service with martyrdom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Bata the Persian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Bata, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Persian monk who was put to death for confessing Christ during the persecution of the Church in Persia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Tamara, Queen of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing Queen Tamara, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Queen of Georgia at the height of its golden age, remembered for her just and Christian rule, her defense and adornment of the Church, and her care for the poor.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Nikephoros of Chios
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nikephoros, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk and teacher of Chios who composed services and lives for the new martyrs and labored to preserve Orthodox learning and piety under Ottoman rule.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Panaretus of Cyprus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Panaretus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hierarch of Cyprus venerated by the Church; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; the OCA entry gives no biographical detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Gerasimus of Boldino
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gerasimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian monastic founder, disciple of St Daniel of Pereyaslavl, who established the monastery at Boldino and several others; known for his ascetic labors.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Isidora
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Isidora, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr commemorated on this day; no details of her life are preserved in the source.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Do NOT conflate with Venerable Isidora the Fool-for-Christ of Tabenna. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Zosimas II of Kumurdo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Zosimas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian monk who became bishop of Kumurdo and served the Church in the late medieval period.
Notes: Honest stub; few details preserved.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Matrona of Moscow
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Mother Matrona, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A blind and bedridden woman of great prayer and spiritual sight who, through the hardest years of the twentieth century in Russia, counseled and comforted the multitudes who came to her and obtained healing for many.
Notes: Modern Russian saint; also widely venerated on her repose.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Athanasius of Lubensk, Patriarch of Constantinople
Also Known As: Athanasius III Patelarios; the Sitting Saint
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Athanasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A native of Crete who twice served as patriarch of Constantinople and reposed on a journey through Ukraine, where he was buried in a seated posture; his incorrupt relics at Lubny made him beloved as a wonderworker.
Notes: Distinct from St Athanasius the Great of Alexandria (OS-0024).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Hesperus and his Family at Attalia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Hesperus, Zoe, and their children, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian household of slaves at Attalia — Hesperus, his wife Zoe, and their sons Cyriacus and Theodulus — who refused to eat of the food offered to idols and were tortured and martyred together under Hadrian.
Notes: Named family group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Boris, Equal of the Apostles, Baptizer of Bulgaria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Equal-to-the-Apostles Boris, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Prince of Bulgaria who received holy Baptism with the name Michael and led his whole people into the Christian faith, establishing the Church among the Bulgarians; in old age he withdrew to a monastery.
Notes: Distinct from the Passion-bearer Boris of Rus' (Boris and Gleb).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Timothy the Reader and his wife Maura
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Timothy and Maura, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A reader and copyist of the sacred books and his wife, newly married, who were arrested together under Diocletian and, after long torments, were crucified side by side for refusing to surrender the Scriptures or deny Christ.
Notes: Named married pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Peter the Wonderworker, Bishop of Argos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk who became bishop of Argos in the Peloponnese, beloved for his mercy to the poor and for feeding his people in time of famine; many miracles were worked through him.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Mamai, Catholicos of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Mamai, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Catholicos of Georgia in the eighth century, remembered as a wise shepherd of the Georgian Church.
Notes: The OCA entry notes information is scarce; possibly venerated as a martyr. Honest stub; flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints Michael and Arsenius of Ulompo
Also Known As: Michael of Ulompo; Arsenius of Ulompo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Michael and Arsenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Georgian monastic saints associated with Ulompo, whose full lives have not been preserved.
Notes: Named pair; biographies not preserved. Honest stub.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Ekoumenios the Wonderworker of Trikala
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Ekoumenios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Trikala in Thessaly remembered as a learned interpreter of Scripture and a wonderworker.
Notes: The OCA entry notes disagreement about his identity and exact dates. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Holy Great Martyr Xenia of Kalamata
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Xenia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian woman of Kalamata in the Peloponnese who endured suffering and death for confessing Christ during the persecutions.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Archpriest Vasily Martysz
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Vasily, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Orthodox archpriest, born in the Chelm region, who served the Church in North America and later in Poland, where he was killed in 1945; he is honored among the new-martyrs.
Notes: Region of origin (Chelm region) has no controlled term; left blank. Modern glorification; flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyr Pelagia of Tarsus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Pelagia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Christian virgin of Tarsus in Cilicia who refused marriage to the emperor's son for the sake of Christ and was martyred under Diocletian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Alphan and his Brothers of Novgorod
Also Known As: the Alfanov Brothers; Niketas; Cyril; Nikephoros; Clement; Isaac
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Five brothers of Novgorod who lived in piety and founded the Sokolnitsky Monastery, where they labored in monastic struggle.
Notes: Named brother-group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Holy Confessor Erasmus, Bishop of Formia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Erasmus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Formia in Campania who confessed Christ through fierce tortures during the persecution of Diocletian; in the West he is invoked as St Elmo, patron of sailors.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Albian, Bishop of Anaea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Albian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Anaea in Asia Minor who refused to offer pagan sacrifice during Diocletian's persecution and was martyred for Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Silvanus, Bishop of Gaza, and 40 Martyrs with him
Also Known As: Silvanus of Gaza; the 40 martyrs with him
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Silvanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former soldier who became bishop of Gaza and, after long confinement at the mines, was beheaded together with forty companions during the persecution of Diocletian.
Notes: Named group; also commemorated Oct 14 in some calendars.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Nikephoros of Mount Athos
Also Known As: Nikephoros the Hesychast; Nicephorus of Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nikephoros, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Italian who embraced Orthodoxy and the monastic life on Mount Athos, where he taught the watchfulness and prayer of the heart and guided disciples in the hesychast tradition.
Notes: Distinct from St Nikephoros of Chios (May 1).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Monica
Also Known As: Monica of Tagaste; mother of Augustine
Life Experience: Marriage; Difficult Marriage; Grief / Bereavement; Parenting
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Mother Monica, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A devout Christian mother of Tagaste in North Africa who bore with a difficult marriage and prayed unceasingly, with many tears, for the conversion of her son Augustine, which she lived to see.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; intercessor for the conversion of children.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Great Martyr Irene
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Irene, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born Penelope, the daughter of a pagan ruler, she came to faith in Christ and endured many torments and deliverances, by which great numbers were converted; she is honored as a great martyr.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Ephraim of Nea Makri
Also Known As: Ephraim the New; Ephraim the Great Martyr
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Ephraim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the mountain of Nea Makri near Athens who was seized by Turkish raiders and martyred after long torture in 1426; in our own time he has become a beloved and swiftly-answering wonderworker.
Notes: Veneration grew widely in the twentieth century after the discovery of his relics.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Barlaam of Serpukhov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Barlaam, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian monastic of Serpukhov who labored in asceticism; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; the OCA entry gives no detail.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Job the Long-Suffering
Life Experience: Illness; Grief / Bereavement; Loss of a Child; Wealth
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Job, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The blameless man of the land of Uz who, stripped of his wealth, children, and health, refused to curse God and held fast in patience through his trials, becoming for all ages the very image of long-suffering faith.
Notes: Region 'land of Uz' approximated; mapped to Mesopotamia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Micah of Radonezh
Also Known As: Micah, disciple of Sergius of Radonezh
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Micah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the first disciples of St Sergius of Radonezh, who shared his master's cell and was found worthy to witness the visitation of the Mother of God to St Sergius; he is remembered for meekness and purity of heart.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Barbarus the Soldier and Companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Barbarus and his companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Christian soldiers in the Morea, serving under Julian the Apostate, who refused to deny Christ and were put to death.
Notes: Named group; distinct from Barbarus the former robber commemorated the same day.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Barbarus the Myrrh-gusher in Thessaly
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Barbarus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A robber of Thessaly who, pierced to the heart with repentance for his crimes, lived out his days in extreme penance in the wilderness and reposed in holiness; myrrh flowed from his relics.
Notes: Distinct from the soldier Barbarus commemorated the same day.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Seraphim of Lebadeia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Seraphim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of Boeotia who struggled on Mount Dobou near Lebadeia and was glorified by God with the gift of wonderworking.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Alexis Toth, Confessor and Defender of Orthodoxy in America
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessor Alexis, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Carpatho-Russian priest in North America who, meeting hostility as an Eastern-rite Catholic, returned with many of his people to the Orthodox Church, becoming the great gatherer of Slavic immigrants into Orthodoxy in America; he reposed in 1909.
Notes: Region of origin (Carpatho-Rus'/Slovakia) has no controlled term; left blank. Modern North American saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Acacius the Centurion at Byzantium
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Acacius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A centurion of Cappadocia who confessed Christ during the persecution under Maximian, endured many torments, and was beheaded at Byzantium.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Nilus, Abbot of Sora
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nilus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian monk who, after time on Mount Athos, founded a skete on the Sora River and taught the inner life of watchfulness and the prayer of the heart; a chief teacher of non-acquisitiveness among the Russian fathers.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John Zedazeni and his Twelve Disciples
Also Known As: John of Zedazeni; the Thirteen Syrian Fathers; Abibus; Anthony; David; Zenon; Thaddeus; Jesse; Joseph; Isidore; Michael; Pyrrhus; Stephen; Shio
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The Syrian father John and his twelve disciples, the Thirteen Syrian (Assyrian) Fathers, who came at the prompting of the Mother of God to strengthen the faith in Georgia, founding monasteries and spreading the monastic life throughout the land.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one. Several are also venerated individually, including Shio of Mgvime (OS-0624).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyr Pachomius of Mount Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Pachomius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A man of Little Russia taken captive and enslaved, who was pressured to deny Christ; escaping to Mount Athos and becoming a monk, he returned to confess the faith openly and was martyred.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Akaki Asistavi of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Father Akaki, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A saint of the Georgian Church commemorated on this day; no details of his life are preserved in the source.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail and his rank/era are uncertain. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Arsenius the Great
Also Known As: Arsenius of Scetis; Arsenius the Roman
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Arsenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned Roman, tutor in the imperial household, who fled the world to the desert of Scetis in Egypt, where he became a model of silence and stillness, weeping continually and counseling, 'Flee, be silent, be still.'
Notes: Distinct from Arsenius the Lover-of-Labor of the Kiev Caves.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Arsenius the Lover-of-Labor of the Kiev Far Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Arsenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Far Caves of Kiev who never sat idle, laboring and praying without ceasing and eating only after sunset; he was glorified for his humility and toil.
Notes: Also commemorated Aug 28 with the Saints of the Far Caves. Distinct from Arsenius the Great.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Pimen the Ascetic of the Kiev Far Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Pimen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Far Caves of Kiev remembered especially for his rigorous fasting and ascetic discipline.
Notes: Also commemorated Aug 28 with the Saints of the Far Caves; dating uncertain. Also commemorated Aug 7 (Pimen the Faster of the Far Caves).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Prophet Isaiah
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Isaiah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The great prophet of Judah, of royal lineage, who in vision beheld the glory of God and foretold with surpassing clearness the coming of the Messiah and the Virgin birth; by tradition he was sawn asunder in his old age.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Christopher of Lycia, with Callinika and Aquilina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Christopher, Callinika, and Aquilina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr of fearsome appearance who suffered for Christ under Decius about the year 250; the two women Callinika and Aquilina, sent to seduce him, were converted by his witness and were martyred with him.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Joseph of Optina
Also Known As: John Litovkin; Elder Joseph of Optina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St Ambrose of Optina and himself one of the Optina elders, who carried on the eldership with deep humility and gentle, discerning counsel; he reposed in 1911.
Notes: Among the Optina Elders; flagged for review (modern).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Simon the Zealot
Also Known As: Simon the Canaanite; Simon of Cana
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Simon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Twelve, called the Zealot, traditionally remembered as the bridegroom of the wedding at Cana who, beholding the Lord's first sign, left all to follow Him and later preached the Gospel to many nations.
Notes: Distinct from Simon Peter and from the minor Apostles of the Seventy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Simon, Bishop of Vladimir and Suzdal
Also Known As: Simon of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Simon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves who became bishop of Vladimir and Suzdal and, with his letters to Polycarp, helped give shape to the Kiev Caves Paterikon.
Notes: Distinct from Apostle Simon the Zealot.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Philadelphus, Cyprian, and Companions in Sicily
Also Known As: Philadelphus; Cyprian; Alphius; Onesimus; Erasmus; and 14 companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A company of Christians connected with Sicily who confessed Christ together and were martyred during the persecutions.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Hesychius of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Hesychius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A high-ranking official of Antioch who, when Christians were stripped of their rank, chose to lay down his office rather than deny Christ, and was drowned for the faith under Maximian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Isidora the Fool of Tabenna
Also Known As: Isidora of Tabenna; Isidora the Fool-for-Christ
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Isidora, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A nun of the convent of Tabenna who feigned madness, taking the lowest and filthiest labors and bearing the scorn of the sisters, while secretly surpassing them all in holiness; when revealed by a holy elder, she fled human praise.
Notes: The first fool-for-Christ among women. Distinct from the Martyr Isidora (May 1, OS-1130).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Thais of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Thais, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wealthy orphan of Egypt who spent her inheritance receiving and serving the poor, monks, and pilgrims, and at last gave herself wholly to Christ.
Notes: Distinct from St Thais the former harlot (Oct 8). Oct 8 = her commemoration as Thais the Penitent.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Comgall of Bangor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Comgall, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A father of early Irish monasticism who founded the great monastery of Bangor, whose strict rule formed many missionaries, among them St Columban.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Mocius the Presbyter
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Mocius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Amphipolis in Macedonia who preached against idolatry and, after enduring fierce torments unharmed, was beheaded under Diocletian; the city of Constantinople later honored him greatly.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Joseph, Metropolitan of Astrakhan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Metropolitan of Astrakhan who shepherded his flock during the troubles of the Razin uprising and was seized and cast to his death from the city tower in 1671.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Nikodemos, Archbishop of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Nikodemos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former abbot of Hilandar on Athos who became archbishop of Serbia, known for his church-building and for translating the typikon into Serbian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Rostislav, Prince of Great Moravia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing Prince Rostislav, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The prince of Great Moravia who sought teachers of the faith in their own tongue from Constantinople, thus opening the door for the mission of Saints Cyril and Methodius among the Slavs.
Notes: Region (Great Moravia) has no controlled term; mapped to Germany (Central Europe).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Dioscorus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Dioscorus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A new martyr commemorated on this day; no details of his life are preserved in the source.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Argyrus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Argyrus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A new martyr commemorated on this day; no details of his life are preserved in the source.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Christopher called Christesia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Christopher, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian of Egrisi who, after the loss of his family, gave himself to the monastic struggle and the love of the divine services.
Notes: Dating uncertain; OCA gives partial detail.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Holy Monastic Martyrs Olympia and Euphrosyne
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Monastic Martyrs Olympia and Euphrosyne, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two monastic women of the convent at Therme on Lesbos who were put to death for Christ.
Notes: Named pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Epiphanius, Bishop of Cyprus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Epiphanius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned monk of Palestine who became bishop of Salamis in Cyprus, a tireless defender of the apostolic faith and author of great works against the heresies of his age; renowned for almsgiving and wonders.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Germanus, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Germanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Constantinople who withstood the emperor Leo the Isaurian in defense of the holy icons and, rather than betray the faith, laid down his office and ended his days in exile; a confessor of Orthodoxy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Dionysius, Archimandrite of the Trinity-Saint Sergius Lavra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Dionysius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archimandrite of the Trinity-Saint Sergius Lavra during the Time of Troubles, who rallied the defense and relief of the suffering and labored, with St Hermogenes, to deliver Russia; he bore unjust imprisonment with patience.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Sabinus, Archbishop of Cyprus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Sabinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St Epiphanius who embraced the monastic life and succeeded to the hierarchy in Cyprus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Polybius, Bishop in Cyprus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Polybius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple and companion of St Epiphanius who recorded much of his teacher's life and served as a bishop in Cyprus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr John of Vlachia
Also Known As: John of Wallachia; John the Romanian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Romanian taken captive who, resisting the lust and threats of his captor and refusing to deny Christ, was put to death at Constantinople.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Pancratius of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Pancratius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A youth orphaned and brought to Rome, who confessed Christ at about fourteen years of age and was beheaded under Diocletian.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyr Glyceria at Heraclea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Glyceria, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A maiden of noble family at Heraclea in Thrace who openly confessed Christ before the idols, endured many torments by the help of God, and received the crown of martyrdom under Antoninus.
Notes: Region (Heraclea in Thrace) mapped to Constantinople, the nearest controlled term.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Laodicius the Prison-Keeper
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Laodicius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The keeper of the prison who, beholding the divine help given to St Glyceria, himself believed in Christ and was beheaded.
Notes: Commemorated with St Glyceria.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Virgin Glyceria of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Virgin Glyceria, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hidden righteous maiden of Novgorod whose incorrupt relics were uncovered in 1572.
Notes: Distinct from the Virgin Martyr Glyceria of Heraclea.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Alexander of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Roman soldier who refused to take part in pagan sacrifice and, after long sufferings on the road of his confession, was beheaded under Maximian.
Notes: Distinct from St Alexander, Archbishop of Jerusalem (May 16).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Pausicacus, Bishop of Synnada
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Pausicacus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Synnada in Phrygia, raised in piety and ascetic struggle, who was given the gift of healing the sick and casting out demons.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints George the Confessor and Irene with their Children
Also Known As: George of Constantinople; Irene of Constantinople; their children
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessors George and Irene, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A husband and wife of Constantinople who, with their children, refused to give up the veneration of the holy icons under the emperor Theophilus and shared together in exile and suffering for the faith.
Notes: A lay family of confessors; distinct from the Great Martyr George.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Euthymius the New and the Georgian Fathers of Iveron
Also Known As: Euthymius the Athonite; John the Iberian; George the Hagiorite; Gabriel of Iveron
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers of Iveron, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The Georgian fathers who founded and adorned the Iveron Monastery on Mount Athos — John the Iberian and his son Euthymius the Athonite, and George the Hagiorite — great translators who rendered the Scriptures and the writings of the fathers into Georgian, with Gabriel who received the Portaitissa icon from the sea.
Notes: Named group of the Iveron founders commemorated together. Sts John (the Iberian) and Gabriel of Iveron, named among these fathers, are also commemorated individually on Jul 12.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Holy Monastic Martyrs of Iveron Monastery slain by the Latins
Also Known As: the Iveron Martyrs; John; George; Gabriel of Iveron
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Iveron, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Georgian monks of the Iveron Monastery on Mount Athos who, refusing to accept union with Rome, were put to death — some drowned, some hanged — during the Latin pressure of the thirteenth century.
Notes: Named group; OCA also lists John, George, and Gabriel of Iveron as monastic-martyrs, gathered here as one commemoration.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Isidore and Myrope of Chios
Also Known As: Isidore of Chios; Myrope of Chios
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Isidore and Myrope, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Isidore, a sailor of the fleet, was tortured and beheaded on Chios for confessing Christ under Decius; the pious Myrope, who secretly took up and buried his body, was herself seized and gave her life for Christ.
Notes: Commemorated together.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Isidore the Fool-for-Christ of Rostov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Isidore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A man of German and wealthy birth who, embracing Orthodoxy, forsook all and lived as a homeless fool-for-Christ at Rostov, dwelling in a hut of branches and hiding great gifts of prayer and wonderworking.
Notes: Distinct from the Martyr Isidore of Chios (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Maximus the Merchant
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Maximus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A devout merchant who used his trade to draw others to Christ and, openly confessing the faith, was stoned to death under Decius.
Notes: Distinct from Martyr Maximus of Ephesus (Apr 30).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Leontius, Patriarch of Jerusalem
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Leontius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Phrygia who became patriarch of Jerusalem, shining in asceticism, almsgiving, and the gift of wonders.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Raiko of Bulgaria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Raiko, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Bulgarian new martyr who suffered under Ottoman rule; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Therapontus of Cyprus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Therapontus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Cyprus who suffered martyrdom for Christ; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review. Also commemorated May 25 as Hieromartyr Therapon, Bishop of Cyprus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Isaiah the Wonderworker, Bishop of Rostov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Isaiah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves who became bishop of Rostov and labored to enlighten the still-pagan land, building churches and strengthening the faithful.
Notes: Distinct from St Isaiah of the Kiev Near Caves (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Tsarevich Demetrius of Uglich
Also Known As: Demetrius of Uglich; Dmitry of Moscow
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Passion-bearer Demetrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The young son of Tsar Ivan the Terrible, who met a violent death at Uglich at the age of eight; venerated as an innocent passion-bearer, his relics were later found incorrupt and worked many healings.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Isaiah, Wonderworker of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Isaiah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Near Caves of Kiev, given to labor, silence, and ascetic struggle.
Notes: Distinct from St Isaiah, Bishop of Rostov (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Pachomius, Abbot of Nerekhta
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Pachomius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest's son who embraced the monastic life and founded a monastery at Nerekhta near Vladimir.
Notes: Distinct from St Pachomius the Great (same day, OS-0028).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Silvanus of Nerekhta
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Silvanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monastic saint of Nerekhta; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Euphrosynus, Abbot of Pskov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Euphrosynus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian monastic founder who withdrew to the wilderness near Pskov and gathered a brotherhood, becoming a guide of many in the spiritual life.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Achilles, Bishop of Larissa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Achilles, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Larissa in Thessaly who took part in the First Ecumenical Council and contended boldly against the Arian heresy; he was glorified with the gift of wonders.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Theodore the Sanctified
Also Known As: Theodore, disciple of Pachomius the Great
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The beloved disciple and successor of St Pachomius the Great, the first to be ordained priest in the cenobitic brotherhood, who guided the monasteries of the Thebaid after his teacher's repose.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Cassian, Abbot of Komel
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Cassian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St Cornelius of Komel who guided the Komel monastery as abbot.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Laurence, Abbot of Komel
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Laurence, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St Cornelius of Komel who served as igumen of the monastery, continuing his teacher's work.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Alexander, Archbishop of Jerusalem
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Clement of Alexandria who, already a confessor in chains, was called to be bishop of Jerusalem, where he gathered a great library; he died in prison at Caesarea during the persecution of Decius.
Notes: Distinct from Martyr Alexander of Rome (May 13).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Vitus, Modestus, and Crescentia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Vitus, Modestus, and Crescentia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The boy Vitus of Sicily, raised in the faith by his tutor Modestus and his nurse Crescentia; the three confessed Christ together and were martyred under Diocletian.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saints; named group commemorated as one. Also commemorated Jun 15.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Child Musa of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Musa, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Roman girl, remembered by St Gregory the Dialogist, to whom the Mother of God appeared and called her to a life of purity; she reposed shortly after, as foretold.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint George, Bishop of Mytilene
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Mytilene on Lesbos during the iconoclast troubles, who shepherded his people in mercy and reposed about the year 842.
Notes: REVIEW: distinct from St George the Confessor of Mytilene at Apr 7 (OS-0957) — OCA commemorates a George of Mytilene on May 16; confirm they are different bishops of that name.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Monastic Martyrs of the Lavra of Saint Savva
Also Known As: the 44 Fathers of St Savva Lavra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of the Lavra of St Savva, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Forty-four monks of the Lavra of St Sabbas in the Judean desert who were slain during a raid upon the monastery about the year 614.
Notes: Named group; distinct from the Holy Fathers slain at St Sabbas commemorated Mar 20 (OS-0851, 8th c.).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theodore of Vrsac
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian hierarch commemorated on this day; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Vukasin of Klepci
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Vukasin, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian layman of Klepci in Herzegovina who, facing a tormentor in the camps of the Second World War, met each cruelty with the quiet words 'Just do your work, child,' and so received the martyr's crown.
Notes: Modern glorification; well-known in Serbian commemoration. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Nicholas of Metsovo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Nicholas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young man of Metsovo in Epirus who, having fallen away under pressure, repented and openly confessed Christ, and was burned to death for the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Brendan the Navigator
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Brendan, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Irish abbot and founder of Clonfert, famed for his long sea voyages in search of the Promised Land of the Saints, a father of Irish monasticism and patron of travelers by sea.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Andronicus of the Seventy and Junia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostles Andronicus and Junia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Kinsfolk and fellow-laborers of the Apostle Paul, named by him in the Epistle to the Romans as 'of note among the apostles'; they preached Christ among the nations and shared his imprisonment.
Notes: Of the Seventy; not of the Twelve. Also commemorated Jul 30 with the Apostles of the Seventy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Euphrosyne, Grand Princess of Moscow
Also Known As: Eudokia of Moscow; Euphrosyne of Moscow
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Euphrosyne, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The pious wife of Grand Prince Demetrios Donskoy who, widowed, gave herself to almsgiving, fasting, and the building of churches, and at the last received the monastic tonsure as Euphrosyne.
Notes: May 17 marks her tonsure; also commemorated Jul 7. Wife of St Demetrios Donskoy (May 19).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Solochon, Pamphamer, and Pamphalon at Chalcedon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Solochon, Pamphamer, and Pamphalon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Egyptian soldiers of the imperial army who refused to offer pagan sacrifice and were tortured to death at Chalcedon under Maximian.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Stephen, Archbishop of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A son of the emperor Basil the Macedonian who, drawn to the Church rather than the court, became patriarch of Constantinople and shepherded the faithful with gentleness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Dodo of the David-Gareji Monastery
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Dodo, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A companion of St David of Gareji and one of the Thirteen Syrian Fathers' spiritual heirs, who founded a community of caves in the Gareji wilderness and lived in deep poverty and stillness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Great Martyr Nicholas of Sofia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Nicholas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young cobbler of Ioannina who, settling in Sofia, withstood every pressure to embrace Islam and was stoned and burned for confessing Christ in 1555.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Athanasius the New of Christianopolis
Also Known As: Athanasius the New; Archbishop of Christianopolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Athanasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hierarch and wonderworker of Christianopolis; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Theodotus of Ancyra and the Seven Virgin-Martyrs
Also Known As: Theodotus; Tecusa; Phaine; Claudia; Matrona; Julia; Alexandra; Euphrasia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Theodotus and the holy Virgins, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An innkeeper of Ancyra who sheltered and supported the persecuted faithful and recovered the bodies of seven aged virgins drowned for Christ; for this he too was seized and beheaded.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one. Nov 6 = the Seven Virgins of Ancyra; Nov 7 = Theodotus the Innkeeper.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Peter, Dionysius, Andrew, Paul, and Christina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A company of Christians of the region of Lampsacus and Troas who, after various torments, were put to death under Decius for refusing to worship idols.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Simeon, Isaac, and Bachtisius of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Simeon, Isaac, and Bachtisius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Persian Christians who refused to forsake Christ for the worship of fire and were martyred in the persecution of the Church in Persia.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Heraclius, Paulinus, and Benedimus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Heraclius, Paulinus, and Benedimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Teachers of the faith at Athens who preached boldly against idolatry and, with those they had brought to Christ, were given over to martyrdom.
Notes: Named group; dating uncertain.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs David and Tarichan of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs David and Tarichan, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two young brothers of noble Georgian family who, holding fast to Christ, were hunted down and slain by a pagan kinsman.
Notes: Named pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Patrick, Bishop of Prusa, and Companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Patrick and Companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patrick, bishop of Prusa in Bithynia, with the priests Acacius, Menander, and Polyainus, who confessed the one God before the governor, were preserved unhurt amid boiling waters, and at last were beheaded.
Notes: Named group; dating uncertain.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Cornelius, Abbot of Komel
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Cornelius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A noble-born monk who withdrew to the forest of Komel and there founded a monastery under a strict rule, becoming the teacher of many; among his disciples are Sts Cassian and Laurence of Komel.
Notes: Distinct from Cornelius of Paleostrov (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Cornelius, Abbot of Paleostrov
Also Known As: Cornelius of Paleostrov; Cornelius of Olonets
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Cornelius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The founder of monastic life on Pali Island in Lake Onega, where disciples gathered around him in the northern wilderness.
Notes: Distinct from Cornelius of Komel (same day). Aug 21 = his Paleostrov (Olonets) commemoration.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing John, Prince of Uglich
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing Prince John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A devout prince held in close imprisonment for many years by his uncle; bearing his sufferings without bitterness, he received the monastic tonsure as Ignatius shortly before his repose.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Sergius of Shukhtom
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Sergius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A schema-monk of the Shukhtom hermitage remembered for his ascetic struggle.
Notes: OCA gives limited detail.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John, Bishop of the Goths in the Crimea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of the Gothic Christians of the Crimea who upheld the veneration of the holy icons against the iconoclasts and suffered imprisonment and exile for the faith.
Notes: Region (Crimea) mapped to Scythia, the nearest controlled term. Also commemorated Jun 26.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Demetrios Donskoy, Grand Prince of Moscow
Also Known As: Dmitry Donskoy; Demetrius of the Don
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing Prince Demetrios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Grand Prince of Moscow who, blessed by St Sergius of Radonezh, led the Russian princes to victory over the Tatars at Kulikovo, and ruled in piety and the building up of the Church.
Notes: Husband of St Euphrosyne (Eudokia) of Moscow (May 17).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Caluf of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Caluf, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Egyptian Christian of the Thebaid who confessed Christ before the authorities and, after enduring torture, received the crown of martyrdom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Theotime
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Theotime, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian woman beheaded for the faith about the year 311; little more of her life is preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives very little detail.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Unmercenary Thallelaios and Martyrs Alexander and Asterios
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Unmercenary Thallelaios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Christian physician who healed the sick without payment and, confessing Christ at Aegae in Cilicia, was martyred together with Alexander and Asterios.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Dovmont, Prince of Pskov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing Prince Dovmont, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Lithuanian prince who, fleeing strife, came to Pskov, embraced Orthodoxy with the name Timothy, and for many years defended the city in faith and justice, never losing a battle and building churches in thanksgiving.
Notes: Region of origin (Lithuania) mapped to Baltics.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Asclas of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Asclas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Great Hermopolis in Egypt who confessed Christ under Diocletian and, after grievous tortures, was drowned in the Nile.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Zabulon and Sosanna, Parents of Saint Nino
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Zabulon and Sosanna, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A noble and devout couple of Cappadocia, the father and mother of St Nino the Enlightener of Georgia; they raised their daughter in piety and gave themselves to God's service.
Notes: Their glorification is commemorated on this day. Parents of St Nina/Nino (OS-0039).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Stephen of Piperi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian monastic of Piperi; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Lydia of Philippi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Lydia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A seller of purple from Thyatira who, hearing the Apostle Paul at Philippi, became the first recorded convert to Christ in Europe and opened her home to the apostles.
Notes: First convert of St Paul in Europe.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Constantine, Prince of Murom, and his Children Michael and Theodore
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing Prince Constantine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prince who brought the Christian faith to the still-pagan city of Murom, bearing with hostility and the loss of his son Michael, until the people were won to Christ; he and his sons are honored as wonderworkers of Murom.
Notes: Named family group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Cassian the Greek of Uglich
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Cassian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Greek nobleman who came to Russia in the retinue of a princess, and there forsook the world to become a monk, founding a monastery near Uglich.
Notes: Oct 2 = his Uglich commemoration.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Basiliscus, Bishop of Comana
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Basiliscus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A nephew of the Great Martyr Theodore the Recruit who, having confessed Christ with his kinsmen, was kept in prison and at last martyred near Comana in Pontus, where a church rose over his relics.
Notes: Also named in the group commemoration of Eutropius, Cleonicus, and Basiliscus on Mar 3 (OS-0762).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John-Vladimir, Prince of Duklja
Also Known As: John Vladimir; Jovan Vladimir; Prince of Bulgaria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prince John-Vladimir, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A pious prince of Duklja in the Serbian lands who governed his people in peace and faith and was treacherously beheaded by a kinsman; he is venerated as a martyr and wonderworker, the earliest of the Serbian saints.
Notes: OCA titles him 'Prince of Bulgaria'; commonly held in the Serbian/Duklja tradition.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyr Paul of the Lavra of Mount Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Paul, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Great Lavra of Mount Athos who suffered martyrdom for Christ; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed James of Borovichi
Also Known As: James of Borovichi; Wonderworker of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed James, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young man of hidden holiness whose coffin came up the river to Borovichi against the current upon a block of ice; received with reverence, his relics became a fountain of healing and the protection of the town.
Notes: Oct 23 = translation of his relics.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Melchizedek, King of Salem
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Melchizedek, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The king and priest of Salem who brought forth bread and wine and blessed Abraham; without recorded genealogy, he is honored as a type of the eternal priesthood of Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Michael the Confessor, Bishop of Synnada
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Michael, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Synnada in Phrygia, an able defender of the holy icons who was sent on embassies for the Church and, refusing to yield to the iconoclast Leo the Armenian, ended his days in exile.
Notes: Distinct from Sts Pausicacus and Agapitus of Synnada.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Euphrosyne, Abbess of Polotsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Euphrosyne, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A princess of Polotsk who refused marriage to take the veil, copying books with her own hand, founding convents and a school, and adorning the Church; she reposed on pilgrimage in Jerusalem.
Notes: Region (Polotsk, in present Belarus) mapped to Rus' / Russia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Paisius, Abbot of Galich
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Paisius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian abbot of Galich; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyr Michael the Black-Robed
Also Known As: Michael of Edessa; Michael of St Savva
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Michael, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young man of Edessa who gave away his inheritance, became a monk of the Lavra of St Savva, and on a journey to the Holy Places was put to death for refusing to deny Christ or accept the favor of the ruler.
Notes: Also commemorated Jul 29.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Holy Myrrh-bearer Mary, the Wife of Cleopas
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Myrrh-bearer Mary, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A daughter of Joseph the Betrothed and childhood companion of the Theotokos, given in the Gospel as the wife of Cleopas, who followed the Lord and was among the women at the Cross and the empty tomb.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Leontius, Bishop of Rostov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Leontius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop sent to the still-pagan land of Rostov, who, driven out and threatened, won the people at last by gentleness and the teaching of the children, and sealed his apostolic labor with a martyr's death; his relics were uncovered incorrupt in 1164.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Serapion the Sindonite
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Serapion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wandering ascetic of Egypt, recounted in the Lausiac History, who owned nothing but the single linen cloth (sindon) that he wore and gave even that away to the cold and the poor; it is told that he sold himself into slavery to win his masters to Christ, then gave the price away.
Notes: Distinct from St Serapion, Bishop of Thmuis (Mar 21, OS-0860), with whom he is sometimes conflated.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Saint Demetre I (Damiane), King of Georgia
Also Known As: Demetre I of Georgia; Damiane; Demetrius the Hymnographer
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing King Demetrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Son of St David the Builder and king of Georgia who, after a turbulent reign, laid down his crown to embrace the monastic life under the name Damiane; he is honored as a hymnographer, by tradition the author of the beloved hymn to the Mother of God, 'Thou art a Vineyard' (Shen khar venakhi).
Notes: Demetre I, the monk-hymnographer (reposed 1156). Distinct from Demetre II the Self-Sacrificing / Tavdadebuli, commemorated Mar 12 (OS-0815). Previously conflated with that row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints; en.wikipedia.org

Name: Venerable Simeon Stylites the Younger of the Wonderful Mountain
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Simeon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of Antioch who from childhood mounted the pillar and gave his life to prayer and fasting on the Wonderful Mountain, receiving great gifts of healing and prophecy; son of St Martha (OS-0094).
Notes: Distinct from St Simeon Stylites the Elder (Sep 1).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Niketas the Stylite of Pereyaslavl
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Niketas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A tax-collector of Pereyaslavl who, smitten with horror at his own greed and cruelty, fled to the monastery in deep repentance and bound himself in chains upon a pillar, where he labored until martyred by robbers who took his fetters for silver.
Notes: Distinct from St Niketas, Bishop of Chalcedon (May 28).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Meletius Stratelates and Companions
Also Known As: Meletius the General; Stephen; John; Serapion the Egyptian; Callinicus; Theodore; Faustus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Meletius and Companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian general of Galatia who, with a great multitude of soldiers and their wives and children — even a converted sorcerer among them — confessed Christ and was put to death under Antoninus.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Vincent of Lerins
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Vincent, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the island monastery of Lerins who, in his Commonitorium, gave the Church the abiding rule for discerning the true faith: that which has been believed everywhere, always, and by all.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Works by the Saint: The Commonitorium
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Urban, Pope of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Urban, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A third-century bishop of Rome venerated as a hieromartyr; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; honest stub, OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Carpus of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Carpus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Seventy, a fellow-laborer of the Apostle Paul who mentions him in his second letter to Timothy; he served as bishop of Verria in Macedonia and preached the Gospel widely.
Notes: Of the Seventy; not of the Twelve.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Alphaeus of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Alphaeus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An apostle of the Seventy from Capernaum, honored in tradition as the father of the Apostles James (son of Alphaeus) and Matthew.
Notes: Of the Seventy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Abercius and Helen
Also Known As: Abercius; Helen; children of Apostle Alphaeus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Abercius and Helen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Children of the Apostle Alphaeus who confessed Christ and were martyred — Abercius bound naked among bees, and Helen stoned to death.
Notes: Named sibling pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Confessor John Psykhaitis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Psykhaita monastery in Constantinople who contended boldly for the holy icons under the iconoclast emperor Leo the Armenian and suffered for the confession of the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Augustine, Archbishop of Canterbury
Also Known As: Augustine of Canterbury; Evangelizer of England
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Augustine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Rome whom St Gregory the Dialogist sent with forty brethren to preach to the English; landing in Kent in 597, he baptized King Ethelbert and his people and became the first archbishop of Canterbury.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; not Augustine of Hippo.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Therapon, Bishop of Sardis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Therapon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Sardis in Lydia who brought many of the pagan Greeks to Christ and baptized them, and after grievous scourgings received the crown of martyrdom.
Notes: Distinct from Therapon of Cyprus (May 25) and the Russian Therapons of this day.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Therapon, Abbot of White Lake
Also Known As: Therapon of White Lake; Wonderworker of Luzhetsk; Ferapont
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Therapon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A companion of St Cyril of White Lake who withdrew into the northern forests and founded the Ferapontov monastery, and later, at Mozhaisk, the monastery of Luzhetsk; a wonderworker of the Russian north.
Notes: Distinct from Therapon of Sardis and Therapon of Monza.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Therapon, Abbot of Monza
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Therapon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic connected with Kostroma and the river Monza, of hidden origin, who labored in holiness and was venerated locally as a wonderworker.
Notes: OCA says his early life is unknown; possibly a fool-for-Christ. Distinct from the other Therapons of this day.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyr Theodora and Martyr Didymus the Soldier
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Theodora and Didymus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: When the virgin Theodora was condemned to a house of shame at Alexandria for confessing Christ, the soldier Didymus entered in disguise and changed clothes with her that she might escape; discovered, the two were beheaded together under Diocletian.
Notes: Named pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Basil of Khakhuli, Son of King Bagrat III
Also Known As: Basil of Georgia; Basil of Khakhuli
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A son of King Bagrat III of Georgia who forsook the royal court for the monastery of Khakhuli, where he labored in learning and the spiritual life and enriched the Church of Georgia.
Notes: Not St Basil the Great.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Michael of Parekhi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Michael, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian monk of Shavsheti who built a cell and chapel at Parekhi and gave himself to ascetic labor in the wilderness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Matthew of Zaransk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Matthew, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A man of Vyatka who, after years in trade, entered the monastic life and was known for his ascetic devotion.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Niketas, Bishop of Chalcedon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Niketas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Chalcedon renowned for his mercy and care of the poor, who upheld the veneration of the holy icons and suffered exile for the confession of the faith.
Notes: Distinct from St Niketas the Stylite of Pereyaslavl (May 24).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Ignatius the Wonderworker, Bishop of Rostov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Ignatius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Rostov who shepherded the Church there for twenty-six years through difficult times and was glorified after his repose in 1288 with the gift of wonders.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Eutychius, Bishop of Melitene
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Eutychius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A fellow-laborer of the holy apostles who served as bishop of Melitene and sealed his preaching of Christ with a martyr's death.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Heliconis of Thessalonica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Heliconis, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian woman of Thessalonica who went to Corinth and preached boldly against the worship of idols, and after many torments was beheaded for Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Helladius, Bishop in the East
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Helladius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of the East who endured imprisonment and beating and was given over to fire for confessing Christ; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives limited detail.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Germanus, Bishop of Paris
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Germanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk who became bishop of Paris, renowned for his almsgiving, his care of captives and the poor, and his labors to soften the violence of the Frankish kings; many miracles were worked through him.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Sophronius the Bulgarian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Sophronius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Bulgarian hieromonk who, fleeing the Turkish assault upon his homeland, withdrew to Wallachia and there lived out his days in ascetic struggle.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Demetrius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Demetrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A new martyr commemorated on this day; no details of his life are preserved in the source.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint William of Gellone
Also Known As: William of Aquitaine; William of Orange
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father William, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Frankish noble and warrior, a kinsman of Charlemagne, who in later life forsook arms and rank to become a humble monk at the monastery he had founded at Gellone.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; OCA gives limited detail.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed John of Ustiug the Fool-for-Christ
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of Ustiug who from childhood kept strict fast, eating nothing on Wednesdays and Fridays, and took up the hard path of folly for Christ, sleeping on hot coals and hiding his prayer beneath the appearance of madness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyr Theodosia the Nun of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Theodosia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A nun of Constantinople who, when the soldiers came to take down the icon of Christ over the Chalke gate, led the women who pulled down the ladder in its defense, and for this confession of the holy images was put to death.
Notes: Distinct from St Theodosia of Tyre (OS-0926, also May 29).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyred Fathers and Mothers of Atchara
Also Known As: Martyrs of Adjara; the Atchara Martyrs
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Atchara, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The Orthodox Christians of the Atchara region of Georgia who, under long pressure to abandon the faith of their fathers, chose death rather than denial.
Notes: Regional group; individual names not preserved. Honest stub; flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Hermas of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Hermas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Seventy, greeted by the Apostle Paul in the Epistle to the Romans, who served as bishop of Philippopolis in Thrace and reposed in peace after much suffering from the pagans.
Notes: Of the Seventy; distinct from Apostle Hermes of the Seventy (OS-0796, Mar 8).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Hermias at Comana
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Hermias, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An aged Christian soldier of Comana in Cappadocia who confessed Christ before the governor, was preserved through fearful torments, and at last was beheaded under Antoninus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Philosophus at Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Philosophus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Christian of Alexandria who, bound and tempted to sin that he might be made to fall, overcame the snare by his own resolve, and after torture was beheaded under Decius.
Notes: Distinct from the New Hieromartyr Philosophos of St Petersburg (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Philosophos of St Petersburg and his Sons
Also Known As: Philosophos Ornatsky; Boris Ornatsky; Nicholas Ornatsky
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Philosophos and your sons, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A beloved archpriest of St Petersburg, a tireless preacher and worker of charity, who openly resisted the Bolshevik persecution of the Church and was shot in 1918 together with his two sons, Boris and Nicholas.
Notes: Named family group commemorated as one; modern glorification; flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Philotheos, Metropolitan of Tobolsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Philotheos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned hierarch who, as metropolitan of Tobolsk, gave himself to the enlightenment of the peoples of Siberia, baptizing many thousands and building churches across the vast land.
Notes: Also commemorated Jun 10 with the Synaxis of the Saints of Siberia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Apollo of the Egyptian Thebaid
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Apollo, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of the Thebaid who founded a great monastery near Hermopolis and guided some five hundred monks in joy and brotherly love, working miracles and bringing peace in famine and strife.
Notes: REVIEW: possibly the same as Venerable Apollonius the Ascetic of Egypt (Mar 31, OS-0910); confirm whether to merge.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Justin the Philosopher and Companions
Also Known As: Justin Martyr; Chariton; Charito; Euelpistus; Hierax; Peon; Liberian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Justin and Companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A philosopher of Samaria who, having sought truth in every school, found it in Christ and became the first great apologist, defending the faith before emperors and teaching openly at Rome; he and his companions were scourged and beheaded for refusing to sacrifice to idols.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Works by the Saint: First Apology; Second Apology; Dialogue with Trypho
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Dionysius, Abbot of Glushitsa
Also Known As: Dionysius of Glushitsa; Dionysius of Vologda
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Dionysius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A northern ascetic in the tradition of St Cyril of White Lake who founded monasteries on the Glushitsa river, painted icons with his own hand, and was tireless in almsgiving and care of the poor.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Agapitus the Unmercenary Physician of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Agapitus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves and disciple of St Anthony who tended the sick without payment, healing them by prayer and the simple herbs he ate, and who himself healed a prince of Chernigov from afar.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Shio, David, Gabriel, and Paul of Akhalkalaki
Also Known As: Shio the New; David; Gabriel; Paul
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Akhalkalaki, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Four monks of the David-Gareji wilderness who, after lives of monastic labor and obedience, were slain in a raid upon their community.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Nikephoros the Confessor, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Nikephoros, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A court official who forsook the world for the monastic life and was raised to the throne of Constantinople; a staunch defender of the holy icons, he was driven from his see by the iconoclast emperor and died after long years in exile.
Notes: Principal feast Jun 2; the translation of his relics is commemorated Mar 13.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr John the New of Suceava
Also Known As: John the New of Sochi; John of Trebizond
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A devout Orthodox merchant of Trebizond who, betrayed by a envious shipmaster and falsely accused, withstood every torment at Belgrade on the Dnieper rather than deny Christ; his relics now rest at Suceava.
Notes: OCA titles him 'of Sochi'; widely known as John the New of Suceava.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Erasmus of Ochrid
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Erasmus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monastic saint of Ochrid; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Lucillian and Companions at Byzantium
Also Known As: Lucillian; Claudius; Hypatius; Paul; Dionysius; Paula
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Lucillian and Companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A pagan priest of Byzantium who came to faith in Christ in his old age and, with four young men and the maiden Paula who served the imprisoned faithful, was martyred under Aurelian.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one; distinct from Hieromartyr Lucian of Beauvais (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Lucian, Bishop of Beauvais, and Companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Lucian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A missionary bishop sent to preach in Gaul who, with his companions, was seized and beheaded near Beauvais for the confession of Christ.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; distinct from Martyr Lucillian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Kevin of Glendalough
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Kevin, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Irish ascetic who withdrew to a cave by the lakes of Glendalough and there, amid the wild creatures who befriended him, gathered a great monastery that became a beacon of prayer and learning.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Metrophanes, First Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Metrophanes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Byzantium in the days of Constantine the Great, honored as the first patriarch of the imperial city, who in great age blessed the gathering of the First Ecumenical Council.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Martha and Mary, the Sisters of Lazarus
Also Known As: Martha of Bethany; Mary of Bethany
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Martha and Mary, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The sisters of Lazarus of Bethany, friends of the Lord who received Him in their home — Martha in her serving and Mary at His feet — and who beheld the raising of their brother from the dead.
Notes: Named sisters commemorated together.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Methodius, Abbot of Peshnosha
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Methodius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St Sergius of Radonezh who withdrew into a forest beyond the river and, at his teacher's counsel, founded the Peshnosha monastery, laboring at its building with his own hands.
