Notes: Principal feast Jun 14 (repose, 1392).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Frontasius, Severinus, Severian, and Silanus of Gaul
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Missionaries sent to preach in southern Gaul who were seized for refusing the worship of idols and beheaded for Christ.
Notes: Named group; pre-schism Western saints.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Concordius of Spoleto
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Concordius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A subdeacon of Rome, son of a priest, who gave himself to prayer and almsgiving and was tortured and beheaded near Spoleto for refusing to sacrifice to idols.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Astius, Bishop of Dyrrachium
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Astius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Dyrrachium who was scourged and crucified for confessing Christ under the emperor Trajan.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Zosima, Bishop of Babylon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Zosima, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Cilicia who struggled in the deserts of Sinai and Lebanon before being called to serve as bishop of Babylon.
Notes: Dating uncertain.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Sophia of Ainos
Life Experience: Marriage; Grief / Bereavement; Loss of a Child
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Sophia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A devout wife and mother of Ainos in Thrace who, after the loss of her husband and children, gave the remainder of her life to fasting, prayer, and the care of the poor.
Notes: Dating uncertain.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Joannicius of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Joannicius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian hierarch numbered among the new-martyrs; few details are given in the source.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr George of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian new-martyr; few details are given in the source.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Distinct from the Great Martyr George. Flagged for review. Also listed Jul 4.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs of Niculitsel
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Niculitsel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Martyrs of the Lower Danube whose relics were found together at Niculitsel, in the land of ancient Scythia Minor, where Christians suffered exile and death for refusing the worship of idols.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Andronicus, Archbishop of Perm
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Andronicus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Perm who boldly denounced the Bolshevik decrees against the Church and was seized and martyred in 1918.
Notes: Modern glorification; flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Joanikije, Metropolitan of Montenegro
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Joanikije, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Metropolitan of Montenegro and the Coast, numbered among the Serbian new-martyrs of the Second World War period.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Region (Montenegro) mapped to Serbia. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Dorotheus, Bishop of Tyre
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Dorotheus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned bishop of Tyre who endured the persecution of Diocletian and returned to shepherd his flock in peace, but in great old age was seized and martyred under Julian the Apostate.
Notes: Distinct from Venerable Abba Dorotheus of Gaza (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Constantine, Metropolitan of Kiev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Constantine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A metropolitan of Kiev amid the strife that followed the fall of Prince Igor, who in humility asked that after death his body be cast out, judging himself unworthy.
Notes: OCA gives limited detail.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theodore Yaroslavich
Also Known As: Theodore of Novgorod; brother of Alexander Nevsky
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing Prince Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The elder brother of St Alexander Nevsky, a pious prince of Novgorod who reposed in his youth, on the very eve of his wedding.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Marcian, Nicander, and Companions of Egypt
Also Known As: Marcian; Nicander; Hyperechius; Apollonius; Leonidas; Arius; Gorgias; Pambo; Selenia; Irene
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A company of Egyptian Christians, among them two women, who confessed Christ under Maximian and were tortured and put to death for the faith.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Theodore the Hermit of the Jordan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk and hermit of the Jordan wilderness, glorified by God with the gift of wonders; once at sea the salt water turned sweet at his prayer.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Anubius the Confessor and Anchorite of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anubius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Egyptian who confessed Christ under torture in the persecution and afterward withdrew to the desert, where with a few brethren he lived a life of such concord that they were of one mind and one soul.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Abba Dorotheus of Gaza
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Dorotheus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of the great elders Barsanuphius and John at the monastery of Abba Seridus near Gaza, whose gentle teachings on humility, the cutting off of one's own will, and the building up of the soul have guided monastics and laity ever since.
Notes: Distinct from Hieromartyr Dorotheus of Tyre (same day).
Works by the Saint: Spiritual Instructions
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Peter of Korisha
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian ascetic who from childhood loved prayer and, forsaking the world, struggled in a cave near Korisha, overcoming fierce temptations to become a beacon of holiness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Bessarion the Wonderworker of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Bessarion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A desert father of Egypt who kept nothing for himself and lived under the open sky in unceasing prayer, of whom it was said that he guarded to the end the grace of his baptism; many wonders were worked through him.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Hilarion the New, Abbot of the Dalmatian Monastery
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hilarion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Tonsured from boyhood, he became abbot of the Dalmatian monastery in Constantinople and, refusing to give up the holy icons, endured beatings and imprisonment under the iconoclast emperors.
Notes: Distinct from St Hilarion the New of Pelecete (Mar 28, OS-0887).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Jonah, Bishop of Perm
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Jonah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A successor of St Stephen of Perm who completed the conversion of Great Perm in 1462, baptizing its princes and people and establishing the Church among them.
Notes: Distinct from Jonah, Abbot of Klimetzk (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Jonah, Abbot of Klimetzk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Jonah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A merchant's son saved from drowning in a storm on Lake Onega, who in thanksgiving forsook the world and founded the Klimetzk Trinity monastery upon the place of his deliverance.
Notes: Distinct from Jonah, Bishop of Perm (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyrs Archelais, Thekla, and Susanna at Salerno
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Archelais, Thekla, and Susanna, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three consecrated virgins who fled a convent near Rome during the persecution of Diocletian, and being seized at Salerno endured torments and were beheaded for Christ.
Notes: Named group; pre-schism Western saints.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Gelasius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Gelasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian who gave away all his goods to the poor and visited the imprisoned faithful, and himself confessed Christ and was martyred; few further details are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives limited detail.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Theodotus, Bishop of Ancyra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Theodotus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Ancyra in Galatia who sheltered and strengthened the faithful through the persecution of Diocletian and was himself martyred for Christ.
Notes: Distinct from Martyr Theodotus the Innkeeper of Ancyra (May 18, OS-1199).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Marcellinus, Pope of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Marcellinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Rome who under torture faltered and offered incense, but, pierced with repentance, confessed Christ openly before the persecutors and was beheaded.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Sisinius the Deacon of Rome and Companions
Also Known As: Sisinius; Cyriacus; Smaragdus; Largus; Apronian; Saturninus; Crescentian; Papias; Maurus; Priscilla; Lucy; Artemia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon of Rome and the company of clergy, laymen, and women who suffered with him during the persecution of Diocletian.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one; pre-schism Western saints. A separate commemoration from Pope Marcellinus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Kyriake, Kaleria, and Mary of Caesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Kyriake, Kaleria, and Mary, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three Christian women of Caesarea in Palestine who forsook the pagan world for a life of prayer and were martyred under Diocletian.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Zenaida of Caesarea
Also Known As: Zenais; Zenaida of Caesarea in Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Zenaida, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian woman of Caesarea remembered for the grace of wonderworking and for her martyrdom.
Notes: OCA says very little is preserved. Honest stub.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Daniel of Sketis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Daniel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An abba of Sketis, twice carried off captive by barbarians and twice delivered, who guided many in the desert and whose sayings preserved the wisdom of the fathers.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Potamiaena of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Potamiaena, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian slave-woman of Alexandria who, guarding her purity and her faith, was tormented and killed with boiling pitch under Maximian; the soldier Basilides who pitied her was soon converted and martyred himself.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theodore, First Bishop of Rostov and Suzdal
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Greek-born bishop sent in the days of St Vladimir to the still-pagan land of Rostov, where he labored amid much opposition to plant the Gospel.
Notes: Distinct from the Great Martyr Theodore Stratelates (OS-0615).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Ephraim, Patriarch of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Ephraim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A military commander who, after a great earthquake at Antioch, was raised to its patriarchal throne, and shepherded the Church with compassion and zeal for the true faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Zosimus of Phoenicia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Zosimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk near Tyre in Phoenicia, given to fasting and prayer, who was granted the gift of seeing things afar off and discerning the hearts of those who came to him.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Theodore of Kvelta
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A village priest of Kvelta in Georgia who, when invaders demanded he guide them to the king, led them astray into the mountains to spare his people, and was put to death for it.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Cyril, Abbot of White Lake
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Cyril, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Moscow-born monk who, at the bidding of the Mother of God, withdrew to the shores of White Lake and there founded a great monastery, a beacon of the northern Thebaid; renowned for his strict rule and gentleness.
Notes: Not Cyril of Alexandria (same day, OS-0476).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Alexander, Abbot of Kushta
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk schooled in the strict discipline of the northern monasteries who withdrew to the river Kushta and founded a community in the Vologda wilderness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Five Nuns Martyred in Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Five consecrated virgins who were beheaded for Christ during the persecution of the Church under Shapur II of Persia.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Columba of Iona
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Columba, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Irish prince and monk who, leaving his homeland, founded the monastery of Iona off the coast of Scotland, from which he and his disciples brought the Gospel to the Picts and Scots.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints David of Gareji and Lucian
Also Known As: David of Gareji; Lucian of Gareji
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers David and Lucian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: David, one of the Thirteen Syrian Fathers, and his disciple Lucian, who settled in the desert of Gareji in Georgia and there founded the great lavra that bears his name.
Notes: Named pair. David is one of the Thirteen Syrian Fathers (cf. OS-1128); his companion Dodo is OS-1196.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Timothy, Bishop of Prusa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Timothy, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Prusa, given the gift of wonders, who turned many from idols to Christ and, after imprisonment, was beheaded under Julian the Apostate.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Silvanus the Schemamonk of the Kiev Far Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Silvanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A schemamonk of the Far Caves of Kiev, of such prayer that once robbers who entered the monastery garden were held fast, unable to move, until he released them.
Notes: Also commemorated Aug 28 with the Saints of the Far Caves. Also commemorated Jul 10.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John Maximovitch, Metropolitan of Tobolsk
Also Known As: John of Tobolsk; Wonderworker of Siberia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned and gentle hierarch, metropolitan of Tobolsk, who gave himself to the care of his far-flung flock and the mission to the peoples of Siberia, and composed many spiritual writings.
Notes: Not St John Maximovitch of Shanghai and San Francisco (his kinsman, OS-0050).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Alexander and Virgin Martyr Antonina at Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Alexander and Antonina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The maiden Antonina, who refused to become a priestess of Artemis and confessed Christ, and the soldier Alexander who, taking pity, changed clothes with her to set her free; both were tortured and martyred.
Notes: This Antonina is distinct from Martyr Antonina of Nicea (OS-0749, Mar 1; Jun 13).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Kanides the Monk of Cappadocia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Kanides, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Cappadocian of pious parents who from earliest youth gave himself to monastic struggle, living in a cave in continual prayer and fasting.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Theophanes and Saint Pansemne of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Theophanes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young widower of Antioch who took up the ascetic life and by his prayer and gentle words drew the fallen woman Pansemne to repentance and a holy life.
Notes: Named pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Bassianus, Bishop of Lodi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Bassianus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Lodi in Lombardy, a friend and fellow-laborer of St Ambrose of Milan, who defended the Nicene faith and shepherded his people with love.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Neaniskos the Martyr of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Neaniskos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Alexandria denounced in the persecution of Diocletian who, granted wisdom to confound his judges, endured torture for seven days and was beheaded for Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Bartholomew of the Twelve
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Bartholomew, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Twelve, of Cana in Galilee, who after Pentecost preached the Gospel through Syria, Asia Minor, Armenia, and the Indies, and was flayed and crucified for Christ.
Notes: Of the Twelve; identified in the Gospel with Nathanael. Distinct from the Apostle Nathanael of the Seventy (OS-1040). Aug 25 = return of his relics to Lipari.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Barnabas of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Barnabas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Levite of Cyprus, fellow-laborer of the Apostle Paul and son of consolation, who preached the Gospel widely and was martyred on his native island; founder of the Church of Cyprus.
Notes: Of the Seventy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Barnabas, Abbot of Vetluga
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Barnabas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest who withdrew to the wild banks of the Vetluga river and lived there in solitude for twenty-eight years before disciples gathered to him.
Notes: Not the Apostle Barnabas.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Metrophanes and the Martyrs of China
Also Known As: Metrophanes Chang; the Chinese Martyrs of the Boxer Rising
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of China, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Metrophanes, the first Chinese Orthodox priest, with his family and some two hundred and twenty-two of the Orthodox faithful of Peking, who were killed for Christ during the Boxer Rising of 1900.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one; not St Metrophanes of Constantinople (Jun 4, OS-1279).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Luke the Confessor, Archbishop of Simferopol
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Luke, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A renowned surgeon who became a bishop, and who through eleven years of imprisonment and exile under the Soviets never ceased either to operate upon the sick or to preach Christ; a confessor and wonderworker, and a heavenly patron of physicians.
Notes: St Luke (Voyno-Yasenetsky) of Crimea; reposed 1961. Major modern saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Onuphrius the Great
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Onuphrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit who for sixty years dwelt utterly alone in the inner desert of Egypt, fed by an angel and clothed only in his own hair and leaves, whose life was made known by St Paphnutius shortly before his repose.
Notes: Distinct from Onuphrius of Malsk (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Peter of Mount Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A soldier taken captive and delivered through the prayers of St Nicholas, who in thanksgiving became a monk and dwelt as a hermit on the Holy Mountain of Athos for fifty-three years, the first of its hermits.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Arsenius, Abbot of Konevits
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Arsenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Novgorodian coppersmith who became a monk and, after time on Mount Athos, founded the monastery on the island of Konevits in Lake Ladoga.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Onuphrius, Abbot of Malsk
Also Known As: Onuphrius of Malsk; Onuphrius of Pskov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Onuphrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The founder of the Nativity monastery at Malsk near Pskov, where he was buried and venerated.
Notes: Distinct from Onuphrius the Great (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Bassian and Jonah of Pertominsk
Also Known As: Bassian of Pertomsk; Jonah of Pertomsk; of Solovki
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Bassian and Jonah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two monks of Solovki, disciples of Igumen Philip, who perished in a storm on the White Sea and were buried at Pertominsk, where their relics were later found.
Notes: Named pair; OCA gives little detail. Their relic-finding is noted Jun 5.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Onouphrios and Auxentios of Vologda
Also Known As: Onuphrius; Auxentius; founders of the Pertsev Hermitage
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Onouphrios and Auxentios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two monastic fathers who founded a hermitage with a church of the Holy Trinity in the forests near Vologda.
Notes: Named pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Stephen of Komel
Also Known As: Stephen of Ozersk; Stephen of Vologda
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An abbot connected with the Ozersk monastery in the Komel region; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable John, Andrew, Heraclemon, and Theophilus of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Four hermits of the Egyptian desert, met in the tradition surrounding Sts Onuphrius and Paphnutius, who shared one life of stillness and prayer.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John (Tornike) of Mount Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian commander who forsook worldly glory for the monastic life on Athos, and who, called back briefly to lead the imperial army to victory, gave the spoils to build up the Iveron monastery.
Notes: Of the Iveron community (cf. OS-1207 group, May 13).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Aquilina of Byblos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Aquilina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A girl of Byblos in Phoenicia, scarcely twelve years old, who boldly confessed Christ and even won others to the faith, and after grievous torments received the martyr's crown under Diocletian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Triphyllius, Bishop of Leucosia
Also Known As: Triphyllius of Nicosia; Triphyllius of Cyprus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Triphyllius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned and eloquent man, trained in law at Beirut, who became a disciple of the simple St Spyridon and, putting worldly wisdom beneath the wisdom of Christ, served as bishop of Leucosia in Cyprus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Andronikos, Disciple of Saint Sergius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Andronikos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St Sergius of Radonezh who, at the request of St Alexis of Moscow, became the first abbot of the Savior monastery on the Yauza, later home to the iconographer St Andrei Rublev.
Notes: Not the Apostle Andronicus of the Seventy (OS-1192).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Savva, Abbot of Moscow
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Savva, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk who succeeded St Andronikos as abbot of the Savior monastery in Moscow, continuing his teacher's work.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints Anna and her Son John of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Anna, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A widow of Constantinople who, taking the name Euthymianus, lived in a monastery in the guise of a man, and with her son John shared a hidden life of ascetic struggle.
Notes: Named mother and son commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable James the Penitent
Also Known As: James who fell and was restored
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father James, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of great gifts who, lifted up by pride, fell through delusion into grievous sin, but coming to himself shut himself in a tomb in fierce penitence until God restored him and granted him again the grace of wonders.
Notes: OCA title preserved for identification.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Antipater, Bishop of Bostra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Antipater, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Bostra in Arabia, esteemed among the fathers for his learning and especially for his writings against the errors of Apollinarius.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Anthimus of Iberia, Metropolitan of Wallachia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Anthimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian taken captive in youth who became one of the great lights of the Romanian Church — metropolitan of Wallachia, master printer, engraver, and preacher, who gave the people the services in their own tongue and was drowned by the Turks for the faith.
Notes: Anthimus the Iberian (Antim Ivireanul). Sep 14 = his repose (1716).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: The Ten Thousand Martyrs
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A great multitude of Christians who were beheaded for Christ; their names, time, and place are not preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no names, date, or region. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Prophet Elisha
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Elisha, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The disciple and successor of the Prophet Elijah, upon whom a double portion of his master's spirit rested; he prophesied in Israel for many years and worked great wonders, cleansing the leper and raising the dead.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Methodius, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Methodius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Sicilian-born monk who suffered long imprisonment for the holy icons and, after the triumph of Orthodoxy, was raised to the throne of Constantinople, where he restored the icons to the churches.
Notes: Not Methodius of Patara (Jun 20) nor Methodius of Peshnosha.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Mstislav (George), Prince of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing Prince Mstislav, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prince of Novgorod venerated as a saint; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Elisha of Suma
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Elisha, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Solovki monastery who labored in humble obediences, weaving nets; after his repose miracles were worked at his grave.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Niphon of Mount Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Niphon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The son of a priest who gave himself to stillness on the Holy Mountain, struggling many years as a hermit and priest in unceasing prayer.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Prophet Amos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Amos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The third of the Minor Prophets, a herdsman of Tekoah in Judah called by God to prophesy in Israel, crying out for justice and mercy and against the idolatry of his day.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Martyrs Cassian and Gregory, Abbots of Avnezh
Also Known As: Cassian of Avnezh; Gregory of Avnezh
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Cassian and Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two ascetic abbots of the Avnezh monastery in the Vologda lands who were slain during a Tatar raid in 1392.
Notes: Named pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Doulas of Cilicia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Doulas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Cilicia seized under Maximian who, after grievous torments, gave up his life for Christ.
Notes: Distinct from St Doulas the Passion-Bearer of Egypt (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Doulas the Passion-Bearer of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Doulas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk who for twenty years bore the contempt, mockery, and false accusations of his brethren with unfailing meekness and obedience, and was vindicated by God.
Notes: Distinct from Martyr Doulas of Cilicia (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Jerome of Stridon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Jerome, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A scholar of Dalmatia who, after the schools of Rome, withdrew to the desert and then to Bethlehem, where he gave his vast learning to the translation and exposition of the Scriptures, rendering them into Latin for the whole Western Church.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint. Region of origin (Stridon, Dalmatia) has no controlled term.
Works by the Saint: Commentaries on the Holy Scriptures
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Prince Lazar, Great Martyr of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Lazar, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The prince who gathered the Serbian people to defend their faith and land against the Ottoman host, and who, choosing the kingdom of heaven over the kingdom of earth, fell with his nobles at the Battle of Kosovo in 1389.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Ephraim the Bulgarian, Patriarch of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Ephraim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Bulgarian-born Athonite ascetic who was twice called against his will to the patriarchal throne of Serbia, and who twice withdrew again into the silence he loved.
Notes: Not Ephraim, Patriarch of Antioch (Jun 8).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Augustine, Bishop of Hippo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Augustine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A restless seeker of North Africa who, after long wandering through error and passion, was brought to Christ through the prayers of his mother Monica and the preaching of St Ambrose, and became bishop of Hippo and a great teacher of grace and the inner life.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; not Augustine of Canterbury (OS-1243).
Works by the Saint: The Confessions; The City of God
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Michael, First Metropolitan of Kiev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Michael, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Remembered as the first metropolitan of Kiev after the Baptism of Rus', who labored to plant the Church among the newly-baptized people and built the first churches of the city.
Notes: Based on certain Russian sources, per OCA. Sep 30 = his repose; Jun 15 = translation of his relics.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theophanes of the Roslavl Forests
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theophanes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former Cossack soldier who entered the monastic life and withdrew to the solitude of the Roslavl forests, a place of refuge for many seekers of stillness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostles Fortunatus, Achaicus, and Stephanas of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostles, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Of the Seventy, fellow-laborers of the Apostle Paul whom he names with thanksgiving in his first letter to the Corinthians as those who refreshed his spirit.
Notes: Of the Seventy; Stephanas is not Stephen the Protomartyr.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Tikhon, Bishop of Amathus in Cyprus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Tikhon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Amathus in Cyprus, raised in piety from childhood, who turned many from idols to Christ and was glorified with the gift of wonders.
Notes: The three Tikhons of this day are distinct persons.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Tikhon of Kaluga
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Tikhon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk who left Moscow for the wilderness near Maloyaroslavl and dwelt in the hollow of a great oak, where disciples gathered and a monastery arose.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Tikhon of Luchov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Tikhon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A man of the Lithuanian lands who, refusing union with Rome, came to Russia, gave away all he had, and entered the monastic life, founding a community at Lukhov near Kostroma.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Tigrius the Presbyter and Eutropius the Reader of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Tigrius and Eutropius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A gentle priest and a pure-hearted reader, both clergy devoted to St John Chrysostom, who suffered in the troubles that followed his unjust exile.
Notes: Named pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Kaikhosro the Georgian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Father Kaikhosro, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian saint remembered through the records and frescoes of the Holy Cross monastery in Jerusalem; few details are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives limited detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Moses of Optina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Moses, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The abbot who gathered and built up Optina in its years of flowering, sheltering the great elders and making the monastery a haven of eldership and charity.
Notes: Among the Optina Elders.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Manuel, Sabel, and Ismael of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Manuel, Sabel, and Ismael, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three Persian brothers of noble birth, raised in the faith by their Christian mother, who came to the Romans as envoys and, confessing Christ, were put to death by Julian the Apostate.
Notes: Named brother group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Nectan of Hartland
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Nectan, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A son of the holy family of Brychan of Wales who crossed to Devon and lived as a hermit, and was beheaded by robbers; he is venerated as a martyr.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Shalva of Akhaltsikhe
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Shalva, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian noble and famed commander under Queen Tamar who, taken captive by the Muslims, refused every honor offered him to deny Christ and was tortured to death.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Botolph of Iken
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Botolph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An English monastic founder who established a monastery at Iken; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; OCA gives no detail. Honest stub.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Hervius of Plouvien
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hervius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A blind Breton ascetic and singer, beloved in Brittany; few details of his life are preserved on the page.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; OCA gives no detail. Honest stub.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Ananias the Iconographer
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Ananias, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Antoniev monastery at Novgorod, gifted in the painting of icons, who in twenty-six years never once left the monastery, serving God in humility and his art.
Notes: Also commemorated Jun 29.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Leontius, Hypatius, and Theodulus at Tripoli
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Leontius, Hypatius, and Theodulus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Roman military commander at Tripoli in Phoenicia and the two soldiers sent to arrest him, who were instead converted by his witness and a vision, and all suffered together for Christ.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Leontius the Canonarch of the Kiev Far Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Leontius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young monk of the Far Caves of Kiev, of a fine voice, who served as canonarch in the church singing and reposed in his youth in holiness.
Notes: Not Martyr Leontius of Tripoli (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Leontius the Hagiorite
Also Known As: Leontius of Mount Athos; Leontius the Clairvoyant
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Leontius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Dionysiou monastery on Mount Athos who in sixty years never once left its walls, and was granted the gift of foreseeing things to come.
Notes: Not Leontius of the Kiev Caves (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Jude the Brother of the Lord
Also Known As: Jude; Thaddeus; Lebbaeus; Judas not Iscariot
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Jude, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A kinsman of the Lord, son of Joseph the Betrothed, and one of the Twelve, who wrote the catholic epistle bearing his name and preached the Gospel through Judea, Syria, and Mesopotamia, suffering martyrdom near Mount Ararat.
Notes: Of the Twelve; not Judas Iscariot. Identified with the Apostle Thaddeus.
Works by the Saint: The Epistle of Jude
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Barlaam of Shenkursk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Barlaam, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Novgorod boyar and civic leader of the Vaga region who in later life forsook the world for the monastic habit and founded a monastery on his own lands.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Zosimus the Soldier at Antioch in Pisidia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Zosimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A soldier who, drawn by longing to Christ, laid down his belt of office to confess the faith openly and was martyred under Trajan.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Paisius the Great
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Paisius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A great ascetic of the desert of Egypt, given from childhood to the Church, who attained to such heights of prayer and love that Christ Himself appeared to him; a father whose intercession is sought for the departed.
Notes: Not Paisius of Hilandar (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John the Solitary of Jerusalem
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A solitary of the Holy Land who passed his days in fasting and prayer in a cave near Jerusalem.
Notes: OCA gives limited detail.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Paisius of Hilandar
Also Known As: Paisius of Chilandari; Paisius of Bulgaria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Paisius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Athonite monk of Bulgarian birth who, grieved at the forgetfulness of his people, wrote the Slavo-Bulgarian History to awaken in them the memory of their saints and tsars, kindling the renewal of Bulgarian Orthodox life.
Notes: Not Paisius the Great (same day).
Works by the Saint: Slavo-Bulgarian History
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Job, Patriarch of Moscow and All Russia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Job, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk from his youth who became the first patriarch of Moscow and, in the Time of Troubles, refused to bless the pretender Dimitri, for which he was driven from his throne and died in exile, a confessor of the truth.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Methodius, Bishop of Patara
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Methodius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Patara in Lycia, learned and humble, who wrote in defense of the faith — most famously the Banquet of the Ten Virgins — and was martyred for Christ.
Notes: Not Methodius of Constantinople (Jun 14) nor of Peshnosha.
Works by the Saint: The Banquet of the Ten Virgins; On the Resurrection
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Prince Gleb Andreevich
Also Known As: Gleb of Vladimir; George in baptism
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing Prince Gleb, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A son of St Andrew Bogoliubsky who from the age of twelve gave himself to prayer and the reading of the divine books, and reposed in purity at nineteen; his relics were found incorrupt.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Aristocles, Demetrian, and Athanasius of Cyprus
Also Known As: Aristocles the Presbyter; Demetrian the Deacon; Athanasius the Reader
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest, a deacon, and a reader of Cyprus who confessed Christ and were martyred under Maximian.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Leucius the Confessor, Bishop of Brindisi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Leucius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Alexandrian who became the first bishop of Brindisi in Italy, laboring to turn the people from idols to Christ.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; dating traditions vary.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Callistus, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Callistus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Athos and disciple of St Gregory of Sinai who twice served as patriarch of Constantinople, a defender of the hesychast fathers and writer on the life of prayer.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Menas, Bishop of Polotsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Menas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves who was consecrated bishop of Polotsk and shepherded his flock in holiness.
Notes: Not the Great Martyr Menas.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Nicholas Cabasilas
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Nicholas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned layman of Thessalonica, kinsman of bishops and friend of emperors, whose writings on the Divine Liturgy and the life in Christ unfold the mystery of the sacraments with surpassing depth and beauty.
Works by the Saint: The Life in Christ; A Commentary on the Divine Liturgy
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Nahum of Ochrid
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nahum, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple and fellow-laborer of Sts Cyril and Methodius who, with St Clement, taught and enlightened the Slavs in Bulgaria and at Ochrid, founding schools and translating the sacred books.
Notes: A disciple of Sts Cyril and Methodius.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Inna, Pinna, and Rimma
Also Known As: Inna; Pinna; Rimma; disciples of the Apostle Andrew
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Inna, Pinna, and Rimma, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Disciples of the Apostle Andrew who preached Christ among the peoples of Scythia by the Danube, and were bound to stakes in the frozen river and so gave up their lives for the faith.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Julian of Tarsus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Julian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Christian of Tarsus, raised in piety by his mother, who through a year of torments held fast to Christ and was at last sewn into a sack with serpents and cast into the sea.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Terence, Bishop of Iconium
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Terence, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A first bishop of Iconium in Lycaonia, ordained by the apostles, who was tortured and beheaded for the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints Julius the Presbyter and Julian the Deacon of Novara
Also Known As: Julius of Novara; Julian of Novara
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Fathers Julius and Julian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two brothers, a priest and a deacon, who labored to overthrow the idols and build churches throughout the region of Novara in northern Italy.
Notes: Named brother pair; pre-schism Western saints.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Anastasia of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Anastasia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The wife of Stephen Nemanja and mother of St Sava, who with her husband forsook the throne for the monastic life, taking the name Anastasia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Niketas of Nisyros
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Niketas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young man of the island of Nisyros who, having been drawn into Islam, returned to Christ in repentance and confessed Him openly, receiving the crown of martyrdom under the Turks.
Notes: Not Niketas the Stylite or Niketas of Chalcedon.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Eusebius, Bishop of Samosata
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Eusebius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A staunch defender of the Nicene faith who was repeatedly driven from his see and exiled by the Arians, and at last was struck on the head with a tile and killed as he traveled to strengthen the faithful.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Zeno and Zenas of Philadelphia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Zeno and Zenas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Zeno, a wealthy Christian who freed his slaves and gave his goods to the poor, and his servant Zenas who would not leave him, who were tortured and martyred together for Christ.
Notes: Named pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Galacteon, Juliana, and Saturninus of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Christians of Constantinople who confessed Christ — Galacteon drowned in the sea, and Juliana with her son Saturninus given to the fire.
Notes: Named group; OCA gives brief detail.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Alban, Protomartyr of Britain
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Alban, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A citizen of Verulamium in Britain who sheltered a fleeing priest, was converted by him, and gave himself up in the priest's place; the first to shed his blood for Christ in the British Isles.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; protomartyr of Britain.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Niketas of Remesiana
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Niketas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Remesiana who preached the Gospel to the peoples of the Balkans; few details are preserved on the page.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA titles him 'Hieromartyr,' though he is generally remembered as a missionary bishop. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Niketas the Dacian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Niketas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr of the Dacian lands; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Distinct from Niketas of Remesiana. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Gregory, Metropolitan of Wallachia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned metropolitan of Wallachia, a translator of the fathers, glorified by the Romanian Orthodox Church.
Notes: Glorified 2005.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Agrippina of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Agrippina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian maiden of Rome who consecrated her virginity to Christ and was scourged and tormented to death under Valerian; her relics, brought to Sicily, worked many healings.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Artemius of Verkola
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Artemius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A pious peasant boy of Verkola on the Dvina who at twelve years was struck dead by a thunderclap; left in the forest, his body was found years later incorrupt and became a source of healing.
Notes: Also commemorated Oct 20.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Eustochius and Companions of Ancyra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Eustochius, a pagan priest converted by the courage of the Christian martyrs, and his companions, who confessed Christ and were put to death under Maximian.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Niketas of Thebes and his Companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Niketas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Thebes in Boeotia whose holiness drew disciples — Daniel, Theodore, and Gregory — who shared his ascetic life.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Etheldreda of Ely
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Etheldreda, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Anglo-Saxon queen of East Anglia who, preserving her virginity through two marriages, at last received the veil and founded the monastery of Ely, ruling it as abbess in holiness.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Youths John and Jacob of Meniugi
Also Known As: John of Meniugi; Jacob of Meniugi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Youths John and Jacob, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two young brothers of Meniugi near Novgorod who reposed in childhood and were venerated after their relics were found incorrupt.
Notes: Named sibling pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Seven Martyred Brothers of Georgia
Also Known As: Orentius; Pharnacius; Eros; Firmus; Firminus; Cyriacus; Longinus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Seven soldier brothers who confessed Christ in the army of Diocletian and were exiled and put to death, one after another, for the faith.
Notes: Named brother group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Athanasius Parios
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Athanasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A theologian and teacher of the Greeks, a leading voice of the Kollyvades fathers, who labored through learning and writing to renew the Orthodox life of his people under Ottoman rule.
Notes: Not Athanasius the Great.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Barlaam of Khutyn
Also Known As: Barlaam of Novgorod; Barlaam of Khutin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Barlaam, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A man of Novgorod who forsook wealth for the monastic life and founded the monastery of Khutyn on the Volkhov, a great wonderworker of the Russian north.
Notes: Principal feast Nov 6; also commemorated Jun 24 and Feb 10.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyr Febronia of Nisibis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Febronia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A nun raised from childhood in a convent at Nisibis, of rare beauty and deeper holiness, who endured frightful tortures rather than deny Christ or her virginity, and was beheaded under Diocletian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints Peter and Fevronia of Murom
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Wonderworkers Peter and Fevronia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prince of Murom and the wise peasant maiden who healed and married him, who ruled together in love and faith and at the last took monastic vows as David and Euphrosyne, reposing on the same day; patrons of Christian marriage.
Notes: Named married pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Dionysios, Builder of the Prodromou Monastery of Mount Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Dionysios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Kastoria who labored on the Holy Mountain and founded the monastery of the Honorable Forerunner (Dionysiou) on Athos.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Dometius of Dionysiou
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Dometius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Dionysiou monastery on Mount Athos; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyr Procopius of Iveron
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Procopius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Iveron monastery on Mount Athos honored as a monastic martyr; no details are preserved on the page.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Distinct from Procopius of Varna. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Procopius of Varna
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Procopius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A new martyr of Varna; no details of his life are preserved on the page.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Nikon the Confessor of Optina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nikon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The last of the elders to hold out at Optina as the monastery was closed, who continued to shepherd the faithful in secret and died of consumption in the northern exile of the Soviet camps, a confessor of Christ.
Notes: Among the Optina Elders; modern confessor.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr George of Attalia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A new martyr of Attalia; no details of his life are preserved on the page.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Not the Great Martyr George. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Moloug of Lismore
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Father Moloug, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Celtic monastic founder and missionary who labored at Lismore and among the Picts; few details are preserved on the page.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; OCA gives no detail.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theoleptos, Metropolitan of Philadelphia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theoleptos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk and metropolitan of Philadelphia, a teacher of the prayer of stillness whose writings on the inner life formed many, among them St Gregory Palamas.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint David of Thessaloniki
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father David, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of Thessalonica who dwelt for three years in the branches of an almond tree in unceasing prayer, and came down at God's command to serve the city, glorified with the gift of wonders.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Dionysius, Archbishop of Suzdal
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Dionysius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves who founded a cave monastery on the Volga near Nizhny Novgorod and later, as archbishop of Suzdal, labored for the unity and good order of the Russian Church.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr David of Saint Anne's Skete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr David, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of St Anne's Skete on Mount Athos, born at Aivali in Asia Minor, who suffered martyrdom for Christ under the Turks.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Sampson the Hospitable of Constantinople
Also Known As: Sampson the Hospitable; the Host of strangers
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Father Sampson, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wealthy Roman skilled in medicine who gave away his inheritance, healed the sick freely, and was ordained priest at Constantinople, where he founded a great hospital and home for strangers that bore his name.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Joanna the Myrrh-bearer
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Myrrh-bearer Joanna, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The wife of Chuza, the steward of Herod, who ministered to the Lord of her substance, followed Him to the Cross, and came among the women to the tomb; by tradition she recovered and honored the head of the Forerunner.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Serapion of Kozhe Lake
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Serapion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Tatar prince taken in war and baptized with the name Sergius, who embraced the monastic life and withdrew to the wilds of Kozhe Lake, where a monastery grew around him.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Severus the Presbyter of Interocrea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Father Severus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Interocrea in central Italy, recounted by St Gregory the Dialogist, known for his virtue and pastoral compassion.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable George of Mount Athos
Also Known As: George the Athonite; George the Hagiorite
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian raised in the monastic life from childhood who became a great light of the Iveron monastery on Athos, completing the translation of the Scriptures and the service-books into the Georgian tongue.
Notes: George the Hagiorite of Iveron; not the Great Martyr George.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Martin of Turov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Martin, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A humble cook who served the bishops of Turov and, in old age and sickness, was comforted by Sts Boris and Gleb and lived out his days in the monastic life.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Kirion II, Catholicos-Patriarch of All Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Kirion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned hierarch and scholar who labored for the restoration of the autocephaly of the Georgian Church and, elected its Catholicos-Patriarch, was found slain in 1918.
Notes: Already commemorated Mar 12 (OS-0816); see reconciliation.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Sergius and Herman, Wonderworkers of Valaam
Also Known As: Sergius of Valaam; Herman of Valaam
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Sergius and Herman, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The founders of the monastery on the island of Valaam in Lake Ladoga, who brought the monastic life and the light of Christ to the pagan peoples of the northern frontier.
Notes: Named pair commemorated as one. Sep 11 = translation of their relics.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Paul the Physician of Corinth
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Paul, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A physician who became a monk and, falsely slandered, was vindicated by a miracle; he healed the sick and labored in humility and prayer.
Notes: Dating uncertain.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Basil Sitnikov the Deacon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon of the Perm region killed in the early Soviet persecution of the Church.
Notes: OCA gives partial detail; modern glorification. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Holy Martyr Pappias
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Pappias, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian who, refusing to sacrifice to idols in the persecution of Diocletian and Maximian, was martyred for Christ.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives limited detail.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Confessor Sergius of Paphlagonia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Sergius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of noble birth from Paphlagonia who stood for the veneration of the holy icons and suffered for the confession of the faith under the iconoclasts.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Peter, Prince of Ordinsk
Also Known As: Peter of the Horde; Peter Tsarevich of Rostov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing Prince Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prince of the Golden Horde who, hearing the preaching of St Cyril of Rostov, forsook his people and his gods to be baptized, settled at Rostov, and at the end of his days received the monastic habit.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Gelasius of Rimet
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gelasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An abbot of the Rimet monastery in Transylvania, a man of strict fasting and solitude who was granted the grace of working miracles.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Holy Queen Dinar
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing Queen Dinar, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian queen of the Caucasus remembered in the chronicles for the defense and strengthening of the Orthodox faith among her people.
Notes: OCA notes scholarly dispute about her historical identification. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Potitus at Naples
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Potitus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A boy who came to Christ against the will of his pagan father and, working wonders even in his youth, confessed the faith and was martyred under Antoninus Pius.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Peter of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A patrician and military officer who, taken captive and freed, gave up rank and the world to become a monk, ending his days in ascetic struggle.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Angelina of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Angelina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The devout wife of St Stephen Brankovic who, widowed and driven into exile, raised holy sons and ended her days in the monastic life; the mother of a household of Serbian saints.
Notes: Also commemorated Jul 30.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Leontius of Radauti
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Leontius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Moldavian monk and priest who founded a monastery near Radauti and later served as bishop, glorified for his holiness and wonders.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Photius, Metropolitan of Kiev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Photius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Greek of Monemvasia who from his youth embraced the monastic life and was called to be metropolitan of Kiev and All Russia, shepherding the Church through years of division and strife.
Notes: Not St Photius the Great of Constantinople (OS-0603).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Juvenal, Patriarch of Jerusalem
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Juvenal, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Jerusalem through the stormy years of the Christological controversies, who stood for the Orthodox faith at the Councils and saw his see raised to a patriarchate.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Stephen the Great of Moldavia
Also Known As: Stephen III of Moldavia; Stephen the Great and Holy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing Prince Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Prince of Moldavia who for nearly half a century defended his land and the faith against the Turks, and who raised a church or monastery after each of his battles; a spiritual son of St Daniel the Hesychast.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Hyacinth of Caesarea and Companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Hyacinth, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young chamberlain at the court of Trajan who, found at prayer, refused to taste the meat offered to idols and was shut up in prison, where he chose to starve rather than eat of it, and so died for Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Anatolius of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anatolius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Near Caves of Kiev; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; distinct from Anatolius the Recluse of the Far Caves (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Anatolius the Recluse of the Kiev Far Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anatolius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A recluse of the Far Caves of Kiev who struggled in silence and prayer; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; distinct from Anatolius of the Near Caves (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Basil, Bishop of Ryazan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Murom and Ryazan who, falsely slandered, committed himself to God and was borne miraculously upon his mantle across the waters to Ryazan, where he shepherded his flock; a wonderworker of the Russian land.
Notes: Also commemorated Jun 10; not Basil the Great.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Princes Basil and Constantine of Yaroslavl
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing Princes Basil and Constantine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Brother princes of Yaroslavl who bore the hardships of the Mongol yoke with faith and ruled their people in piety.
Notes: Named brother pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable John and Longinus, Wonderworkers of Yarenga
Also Known As: John of Yarenga; Longinus of Yarenga
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers John and Longinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two monks of Solovki who perished together in a storm on the White Sea, and whose relics were carried to Yarenga, where many wonders were worked.
Notes: Named pair commemorated together; also Oct 16. OCA gives little detail.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed John of Moscow the Fool-for-Christ
Also Known As: John the Big Cap; John of Moscow
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A laborer of Vologda, called 'the Big Cap' for the heavy iron he wore, who took up the fool's path for Christ in Moscow, going barefoot in all weathers and speaking the truth boldly to the great.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Nikodemos, Abbot of Kozhe Lake
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nikodemos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A peasant's son who entered the monastic life and withdrew into the wilderness of Kozhe Lake, where he lived as a hermit and was later abbot.
Notes: Not Nikodemos the Hagiorite.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Mark and Mocius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Mark and Mocius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two Christians who refused to sacrifice to idols under Maximian and were beheaded for Christ.
Notes: Named pair commemorated as one. Honest stub.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Anatolius, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Anatolius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned cleric of Alexandria who became patriarch of Constantinople and labored for the Orthodox confession in the days of the Council of Chalcedon.
Notes: Not the Anatolii of the Kiev Caves (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyr Gerasimus the New of Carpenision
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Gerasimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young man who, having fallen away under pressure, repented and became a monk, then returned to confess Christ openly and was hanged for the faith under the Turks.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint George the God-Bearer of the Black Mountain
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A recluse of the Black Mountain near Antioch, a God-bearing elder renowned for his ascetic struggle and spiritual gifts.
Notes: Not the Great Martyr George.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Andrew, Archbishop of Crete
Also Known As: Andrew of Crete; Andrew of Jerusalem
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Andrew, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Jerusalem who became archbishop of Crete, the greatest of the hymnographers of the Church, whose Great Canon of repentance is sung in the days of the Fast.
Notes: Not Andrew Rublev.
Works by the Saint: The Great Canon
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Andrew Rublev the Iconographer
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Andrew, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk and disciple in the spiritual line of St Sergius of Radonezh, the greatest of Russian iconographers, whose icon of the Holy Trinity opens a window into the divine beauty itself.
Notes: Not Andrew, Archbishop of Crete.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Theodotus and Theodotia at Caesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Theodotus and Theodotia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two Christians of Caesarea in Cappadocia who refused to sacrifice to idols and were put to death under Trajan.
Notes: Named pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Theodore, Bishop of Cyrene, and Companions
Also Known As: Theodore of Cyrene; Cyprilla; Aroa; Lucia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Theodore and Companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Cyrene in Libya, skilled in copying the sacred books, who was betrayed by his own son and martyred under Diocletian together with faithful women who confessed Christ.
Notes: Named group; includes women martyrs.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Tikhon, Vasily, and Nikon, Monks of Solovki
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three monks of the Solovki monastery commemorated together; few details of their lives are preserved.
Notes: Named group; OCA gives no detail. Honest stub.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Prince Andrew Bogoliubsky
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing Prince Andrew, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The Grand Prince who made Vladimir the heart of the Russian land, a great builder of churches and lover of the Mother of God, who was treacherously murdered in his palace by conspiring boyars and is honored as a passion-bearer.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Cosmas and Damian of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Unmercenaries Cosmas and Damian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Brother physicians at Rome who healed the sick freely for the love of Christ, and were stoned to death by their own former teacher, who envied the grace given them.
Notes: The Roman pair (martyrs). Distinct from Cosmas and Damian of Asia (OS-0036, Nov 1) and of Arabia (Oct 17).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Cosmas and Damian of Arabia
Also Known As: the Unmercenary physicians of Arabia; of Cilicia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Unmercenaries Cosmas and Damian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Brother physicians of Arabia who healed both soul and body without payment and, confessing Christ, were tortured and beheaded with their companions at Aegae in Cilicia under Diocletian.
Notes: The Arabian pair (martyrs). Distinct from Cosmas and Damian of Asia (OS-0036, Nov 1) and of Rome (Jul 1). Region of origin (Arabia) has no controlled term; mapped to Asia Minor, the place of their martyrdom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Luarsab II, King of Kartli
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Luarsab, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A king of Kartli who withstood the pressure of Shah Abbas of Persia to renounce Christ; held long in captivity at Shiraz, he was strangled for the faith about the year 1622.
Notes: Martyred under Shah Abbas of Persia (17th c.). Commemorated jointly with St Archil II on Jan 28 and individually on Jun 21. Previously joined with Archil II in OS-0557.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Athanasius of Athos and his Six Disciples
Also Known As: Athanasius the Athonite; founder of the Great Lavra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Athanasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The founder of the Great Lavra on Mount Athos and father of its cenobitic life, who against his own love of stillness gathered and ordered the monastic brotherhood of the Holy Mountain; he reposed when the dome of his church fell, with six disciples beside him.
Notes: Named with his six disciples.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Anna at Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Anna, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr of Rome; no details of her life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Cyrilla of Cyrene
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Cyrilla, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian widow of Cyrene in Libya who suffered martyrdom for confessing Christ.
Notes: Possibly the Cyprilla numbered among the companions of Hieromartyr Theodore of Cyrene (Jul 4, OS-1471). Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Lampados of Irenopolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Lampados, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hierarch of Irenopolis in Isauria, remembered for his wisdom, patience, and pastoral virtue.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Cyprian of Mount Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Cyprian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Mount Athos honored as a hieromartyr; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Sisoes the Great
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Sisoes, pray to God for us.
