Brief Life: A great father of the Egyptian desert who struggled sixty years in the cave once hallowed by St Anthony, and who, dying with his face shining like the sun, still prayed for a little more time to repent.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Sisoes the Schemamonk of the Kiev Far Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Sisoes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A schemamonk of the Far Caves of Kiev, remembered among the holy fathers whose relics rest there.
Notes: Not Sisoes the Great.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Marinus, Martha, and their Children at Rome
Also Known As: Marinus; Martha; Audifax; Habakkuk; and companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Marinus, Martha, and your children, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A noble Christian family of Persia — the parents Marinus and Martha with their sons Audifax and Habakkuk — who came to Rome to venerate the tombs of the apostles, served the imprisoned faithful, and were martyred with many others under Claudius.
Notes: Named family group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Isaurius the Deacon and Companions at Apollonia
Also Known As: Isaurius; Innocent; Felix; Hermias; Basil; Peregrinus; Rufus; Ruphinus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon of Athens and his companions who preached Christ at Apollonia in Macedonia and were beheaded under the emperor Numerian.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Confessor Quintus of Phrygia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessor Quintus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Phrygia, given to almsgiving and the healing of the sick by prayer, who confessed Christ under torture and, surviving his tormentors, lived out his days in holiness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Lucy the Virgin and Companions at Rome
Also Known As: Lucy; Lucia; Rexius; Antoninus; and companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Lucy and Companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A virgin of Campania consecrated to Christ who, with a company of those converted through her, suffered martyrdom at Rome.
Notes: Named group; pre-schism Western saints. Not St Lucia of Syracuse (Dec 13).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Thomas of Mount Maleos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Thomas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A military commander who, leaving worldly honor and his rank, took up the monastic life and withdrew to Mount Maleos, where he struggled in stillness and was given the gift of wonders.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Acacius of Sinai
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Acacius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young monk who for nine years bore without complaint the harshness and blows of a cruel elder, and after his death answered from the tomb that he yet lived — a wonder of holy obedience recounted in The Ladder.
Notes: Not Acacius the Centurion.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Peregrinus and Companions of Dyrrachium
Also Known As: Peregrinus; Lucian; Pompeius; Hesychius; Papius; Saturninus; Germanus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Seven Christians of Italy who, fleeing persecution, came to Dyrrachium, and beholding the martyrdom of Bishop Astius confessed Christ themselves and were drowned in the sea under Trajan.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one; cf. Hieromartyr Astius of Dyrrachium (Jun 4, OS-1283).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Evangelicus, Bishop of Tomis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Evangelicus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Honored as the first bishop of Tomis on the Black Sea, a successor of the preaching of the Apostle Andrew among the peoples of the lower Danube, who sealed his episcopate with martyrdom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Great Martyr Kyriake of Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Kyriake, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A maiden born on the Lord's Day and named for it, who refused both marriage and the worship of idols, and after fire and beasts could not harm her was beheaded for Christ under Diocletian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Prosper of Aquitaine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Prosper, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned layman of Aquitaine, a disciple of the teaching of St Augustine, who contended in his writings for the truth of grace against the errors of his age.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Great Martyr Procopius of Caesarea
Also Known As: Neanius; Procopius of Caesarea in Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Procopius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A military officer of Jerusalem, in the world Neanius, who being sent to persecute the Christians was met by Christ on the road as once was Saul, and turning to the faith broke his idols, suffered fearful torments, and was beheaded under Diocletian.
Notes: Not Procopius the Fool of Ustya (same day) nor Procopius of Varna.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Procopius the Fool-for-Christ of Ustya
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Procopius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A fool-for-Christ of the Vologda land whose incorrupt relics at Ustya became a source of healing; little of his origin and life is recorded.
Notes: OCA says no account of his origin is known. Not the Great Martyr Procopius (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyrs Epictetus and Astion at Halmyris
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Epictetus and Astion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The priest-monk Epictetus and his young disciple Astion, who came from Bithynia to Halmyris in Scythia Minor and, after working wonders and enduring long torture, were beheaded for Christ under Diocletian.
Notes: Named pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Theophilus the Myrrh-gusher of Macedonia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theophilus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned ascetic of Macedonia who, having labored in the monastic life, was glorified after his repose with the flowing of fragrant myrrh from his relics.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Royal Martyr Mirdat, King of Kartli
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Mirdat, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A king of Kartli who loved Christ and his people, taken captive by the Persians and put to death for the faith; remembered as the first martyred king of Georgia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Pancratius, Bishop of Taormina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Pancratius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A man of Antioch, baptized in the days of the apostles, who was sent by the Apostle Peter to preach in Sicily and became the first bishop of Taormina, where he was martyred.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; not Martyr Pancratius of Rome (OS-1158).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Patermuthius, Coprius, and Alexander
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patermuthius, a robber-chief who repented and became a monk, his disciple Coprius, and the soldier Alexander converted by their witness, who together confessed Christ and were martyred under Julian the Apostate.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theodore, Bishop of Edessa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An orphan of Edessa who gave away his inheritance, became a monk of the Lavra of St Sabbas, and was called to be bishop of his native city, where he labored for the Orthodox faith.
Notes: Also commemorated Jul 19.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Dionysios the Rhetorician and his Disciple Metrophanes
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Dionysios and Metrophanes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned monk and his disciple who struggled together in the Little Skete of St Anne on Mount Athos.
Notes: Named teacher-and-disciple pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: The 45 Holy Martyrs at Nicopolis in Armenia
Also Known As: Leontius; Maurice; Daniel; Anthony; Alexander; Sisinius; and companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Forty-five Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Forty-five Christians of Nicopolis in Armenia who openly confessed Christ before the governor in the persecution of Licinius, and after fearful tortures were burned and their bones cast into the river.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Apollonius of Sardis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Apollonius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Sardis in Lydia who refused to swear by the name of the emperor and, after torture, was crucified for Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Bianor and Silvanus of Pisidia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Bianor and Silvanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bianor, who confessed Christ steadfastly under torture, and Silvanus, converted by beholding his constancy, who were martyred together in Pisidia.
Notes: Named pair commemorated as one; not Silvanus of the Kiev Caves.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: The 10,000 Martyred Fathers of the Deserts of Scete
Also Known As: the Martyred Fathers of Nitria and Scete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyred Fathers, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A great multitude of monks of the Nitrian desert who were slain amid the strife over the teaching of Origen in the days of Patriarch Theophilus of Alexandria.
Notes: Named large monastic group; individual names not preserved.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyr Nectarius of Saint Anne's Skete
Also Known As: Nectarius of St Anne's Skete on Mount Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Nectarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of St Anne's Skete on Mount Athos honored as a martyr; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Joseph and Companions of Damascus
Also Known As: Joseph of Damascus; the Martyrs of Damascus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Damascus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Orthodox Christians of Damascus, with the priest Joseph among them, who were slain for the faith in the massacre of 1860.
Notes: OCA gives little detail; honest stub. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Great Martyr Euphemia the All-Praised
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Euphemia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A maiden of Chalcedon who confessed Christ and was given to wild beasts and fire under Diocletian; in later days, at the Fourth Ecumenical Council, the Orthodox confession of faith was laid in her tomb and found upon her breast, while the heretics' scroll lay at her feet.
Notes: Principal martyrdom feast is Sep 16; Jul 11 commemorates the miracle of her relics at Chalcedon.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Kindeos the Presbyter of Pamphylia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Kindeos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Talmenia in Pamphylia who preached Christ boldly during the persecution of Diocletian and was burned for the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Monastic Martyr Nikodemos of Albania
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Nikodemos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A married Christian of Elbasan who fell away to Islam, but coming to himself in bitter repentance became a monk on Mount Athos, returned to confess Christ openly before those he had denied, and was beheaded for the faith.
Notes: Region of origin (Albania) has no controlled term. OCA also lists him Jul 10.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Arcadius of Novotorsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Arcadius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A youth of Vyazma who embraced poverty and the path of folly for Christ, living by alms and prayer in silence and meekness.
Notes: Aug 14 = placement of his relics (1798).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Proclus and Hilary of Ancyra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Proclus and Hilary, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two kinsmen of the village of Kallippi near Ancyra who confessed Christ before the governor and were tortured and slain under Trajan.
Notes: Named pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Michael Maleinos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Michael, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Cappadocian nobleman, kinsman of emperors, who forsook the court for the monastic life and founded a community on Mount Kyminas; the spiritual father of St Athanasius of Athos.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Theodore and John, the Varangians of Kiev
Also Known As: Theodore the Varangian; John his son
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Theodore and John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Varangian Christian and his son in Kiev who refused to give the youth up for pagan sacrifice and were killed by the heathen — the first martyrs of the Russian land, before the Baptism of Rus'.
Notes: Named father-and-son pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Arsenius of Novgorod the Fool-for-Christ
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Arsenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A fool-for-Christ of Novgorod; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Simon, Abbot of Volomsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Simon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A peasant's son who, after long pilgrimage and monastic struggle, founded a monastery in the Volomsk wilderness, where he was slain by robbers.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Golinduc of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Golinduc, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The wife of a Persian chief magician who, shown the torments of the wicked and the glory of the saints in a vision, came to Christ and was baptized Mary, and bore long imprisonment and suffering for the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Veronica, the Woman with the Issue of Blood
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Veronica, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The woman of the Gospel who, suffering a flow of blood for twelve years, touched the hem of the Lord's garment in faith and was made whole; remembered in the Church by the name Veronica.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Serapion the New of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Serapion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Alexandria who confessed Christ before the authorities and was martyred under the emperor Severus.
Notes: Distinct from Serapion of Thmuis, Serapion the Sindonite, and the Martyr Serapion of Jul 13.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Stephen of Saint Savva Monastery
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A nephew of St John of Damascus who entered the Lavra of St Sabbas as a child of ten and lived out his life there in asceticism and the writing of sacred hymns.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Julian, Bishop of Le Mans
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Julian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A missionary bishop, by tradition sent in apostolic times, who preached the Gospel in Gaul and is honored as the first bishop of Le Mans.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; dating traditions vary.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Serapion of Severus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Serapion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian brought before the governor Achilles who confessed Christ and was martyred under the emperor Severus.
Notes: Honest stub; not Serapion the New of Alexandria (Jul 12).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Marcian of Iconium
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Marcian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Christian of Iconium who turned many to Christ by his preaching and, after refusing to deny the faith, received the martyr's crown.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Just of Penwith
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Father Just, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A saint of Cornwall in the early British church; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; OCA gives no detail. Honest stub.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Abbess Sarah of Sketis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Sarah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A desert mother who struggled sixty years by the river without once looking upon it, and whose sayings of fearless humility are treasured among the words of the fathers.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Aquila of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Aquila, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Jewish Christian of Pontus, a tentmaker who with his wife Priscilla labored beside the Apostle Paul, opened his house as a church, and helped instruct the eloquent Apollos more perfectly in the way of the Lord.
Notes: Of the Seventy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Stephen, Abbot of Makhrishche
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk formed in the Kiev Caves who came north and founded the Makhrishche monastery near Moscow, a friend and fellow-laborer of St Sergius of Radonezh.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Justus the Soldier at Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Justus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Roman soldier to whom the Life-giving Cross appeared in a vision, who gave away his goods to the poor and openly confessed Christ, and was martyred for the faith.
Notes: Not Saint Just of Penwith (Jul 13).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Hellius of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hellius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Egyptian raised in a monastery from childhood who withdrew into the desert, where by long struggle he received the gifts of abstinence and of taming the wild beasts.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Onesimus the Wonderworker of Magnesia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Onesimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk born at Caesarea who founded a monastery at Magnesia and was glorified by God with the gift of wonders.
Notes: Not the Apostle Onesimus of the Seventy (OS-0662).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Nikodemos the Hagiorite
Also Known As: Nikodemos of the Holy Mountain; Nicholas Kallivourtzis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nikodemos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Mount Athos of immense learning and prayer who gathered, edited, and gave to the Church a treasury of her own wisdom — the Philokalia and a host of ascetical, canonical, and liturgical works.
Notes: Not Nikodemos of Kozhe Lake (Jul 3).
Works by the Saint: The Philokalia (editor); Unseen Warfare; The Rudder
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Quiricus and Julitta
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Quiricus and Julitta, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian widow of Iconium and her son Quiricus, but three years old, who fleeing persecution were seized at Tarsus; when the child cried out that he too was a Christian, both were put to death together.
Notes: Named mother-and-son pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Aboudimos of Tenedos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Aboudimos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of the island of Tenedos who refused to take part in pagan worship and was beheaded for Christ under Diocletian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Athenogenes and his Ten Disciples
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Athenogenes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop who with his ten disciples confessed Christ at Sebastea and was martyred; to him is ascribed by tradition the ancient evening hymn 'O Gladsome Light.'
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Paul, Chionia, and Alevtina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Egyptian Christians — a man and two sisters — who were taken to Caesarea in Palestine and there confessed Christ and were martyred under Maximian.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Antiochus the Physician of Sebaste
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Antiochus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian physician of Sebastea, brother of the martyr Platon, who healed the sick freely and, confessing Christ, endured boiling and torture before he was beheaded; milk flowed from his wound.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyr Julia of Carthage
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Julia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian maiden of Carthage carried off into slavery, who kept her faith and purity in her servitude and, refusing to join in a pagan sacrifice on Corsica, was crucified for Christ.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr John of Trnovo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A new martyr of Trnovo; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Irenarchus, Abbot of Solovki
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Irenarchus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An abbot of the Solovki monastery who guided the brotherhood in prayer and labor through troubled years.
Notes: Not Irenarchus the Recluse of Rostov (OS-0441).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Royal Passion-bearers of Russia
Also Known As: Tsar Nicholas II; Tsarina Alexandra; Tsarevich Alexei; Grand Duchesses Olga, Tatiana, Maria, Anastasia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Royal Passion-bearers, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The last emperor of Russia, Nicholas, with his wife Alexandra and their children Alexei, Olga, Tatiana, Maria, and Anastasia, who bore imprisonment and humiliation with Christian meekness and forgiveness and were shot together at Ekaterinburg in 1918.
Notes: Named family group commemorated as one; modern glorification.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Leonid of Ustnedumsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Leonid, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A farmer who, bidden in a dream, took up the monastic life in old age and founded a monastery in the northern wilderness at Ustnedumsk, draining the marshes with his own hands.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Emilian of Silistria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Emilian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Slav of Durostorum on the Danube who, in zeal for Christ, shattered the idols in the pagan temple under Julian the Apostate, and gave himself up to be burned rather than let another suffer in his place.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Hyacinthus of Amastris
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Hyacinthus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A child of Amastris of wondrous early faith, who for confessing Christ and overturning the worship of a sacred tree was tormented and died a martyr.
Notes: Not Hyacinth of Caesarea (Jul 3, OS-1487).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable John the Long-Suffering of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves who waged so fierce a war against the lusts of the flesh that he buried himself to the breast in the earth through a whole Lent, and by long endurance overcame the passions and received peace.
Notes: Patron of those who struggle against fleshly temptation.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Pambo the Recluse of the Kiev Far Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Pambo, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves taken captive by the Tatars who endured their torments rather than deny Christ, and being miraculously returned to his monastery, lived out his days as a recluse.
Notes: Not Pambo the Hermit of Egypt (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Pambo the Hermit of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Pambo, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A great father of the Nitrian desert, of such gravity and silence that he weighed every word, and whose face shone like Moses' so that the brethren could not gaze upon it.
Notes: Not Pambo of the Kiev Caves (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Grand Duchess Elizabeth
Also Known As: Elizabeth Feodorovna; the New Martyr Elizabeth
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Elizabeth, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A German princess who freely embraced Orthodoxy, and after her husband's murder gave away all she had to found the Convent of Martha and Mary, serving the sick and poor of Moscow; cast alive into a mine-shaft in 1918, she was heard singing hymns until she died.
Notes: Region of origin Germany (Hesse). Sister of Tsarina Alexandra. Not St Elizabeth the Wonderworker of Constantinople (OS-1055).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Nun-martyr Barbara of Alapayevsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Barbara, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A nun and faithful companion of Grand Duchess Elizabeth who would not leave her, and was cast with her into the mine-shaft at Alapayevsk and died with her in 1918.
Notes: Not the Great Martyr Barbara (OS-0016).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Kozman of David-Gareji
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Kozman, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hieromonk of the David-Gareji monastic wilderness in Georgia who ended his life as a martyr; few details are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives few details. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Macrina, Sister of Saint Basil the Great
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Macrina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The eldest of the holy children of a Cappadocian family that gave the Church St Basil the Great and St Gregory of Nyssa, who turned her mother's house into a convent and was the teacher and guide of her brothers in the way of perfection.
Notes: Granddaughter of St Macrina the Elder.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Dius, Abbot of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Dius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of Antioch renowned for fasting and prayer, who founded a monastery at Constantinople and was glorified with the gift of wonders.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Prince Roman of Ryazan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Prince Roman, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prince of Ryazan who defended his people and the faith under the Mongol yoke, and being slandered to the Horde was hideously tortured to death in 1270 for refusing to deny Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Paisius of the Kiev Far Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Paisius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Far Caves of Kiev; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives limited detail.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Stephen, King of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing King Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A son of the holy Prince Lazar who, as despot of Serbia, governed his people wisely under the shadow of the Turk, adorned the Church, cared for the poor, and was himself a writer of spiritual works.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Princess Militsa of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Militsa, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The wife of holy Prince Lazar who, widowed after Kosovo, held the Serbian land together for her young sons and at last took the veil as the nun Euphrosyne, ending her days in prayer and good works.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Abramius of Galich
Also Known As: Abraham of Chukhloma; disciple of Sergius of Radonezh
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Abramius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St Sergius of Radonezh who withdrew to the wild shores of Chukhloma Lake near Galich and there founded monasteries that drew many to the monastic life.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Martyr Ilia Chavchavadze of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Martyr Ilia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A writer and leader of his people, called the uncrowned king and the father of the nation, who in word and deed defended the Georgian Church and people, and was murdered for it in 1907.
Notes: Modern Georgian saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Martyr Maria Skobtsova of Paris
Also Known As: Mother Maria of Paris; Elizaveta Pilenko
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Maria, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian poet and emigre who became a nun in Paris, opening her house to the hungry and homeless, and who in the war sheltered and rescued Jews, for which she was taken to Ravensbruck and gave her life in the gas chamber in place of another.
Notes: Modern saint; region of origin (Riga) mapped to Baltics.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Priest-martyr Demetrius Klepinin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Demetrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Orthodox priest in Paris who, working beside Mother Maria, gave baptismal certificates to Jews to save them, and was arrested and died in the camp of Buchenwald.
Notes: Modern saint; OCA gives few details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Priest-martyr Alexei Medvedkov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Alexei, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian priest who served a poor emigre parish at Ugine in France with great humility and was glorified after his incorrupt relics were found.
Notes: Modern saint; OCA gives few details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Salome the Georgian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Salome, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian nun of a convent in Jerusalem who, seized for confessing Christ, suffered martyrdom for the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Aaron the High Priest
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Aaron, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The brother of Moses and Miriam, appointed by God as the first high priest of Israel, who served at the altar and bore the names of the tribes before the Lord as the people came up out of Egypt.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Prophet Ezekiel
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Ezekiel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Israel carried into the Babylonian captivity, who by the river Chebar beheld the vision of the chariot of God and the valley of dry bones, and called the exiles to repentance and to hope in the restoration to come.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Simeon of Emessa, Fool-for-Christ, and his Companion John
Also Known As: Simeon the Holy Fool; John of Emessa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Simeon and Venerable John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two friends from youth who together forsook the world for the desert by the Jordan; after long ascetic struggle Simeon went into the city of Emessa to take up the hidden path of folly for Christ, while John remained in the wilderness.
Notes: Named pair commemorated as one. Simeon is a chief of the fools-for-Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Onuphrius the Silent and Onesimos the Recluse of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Onuphrius and Onesimos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two ascetics of the Kiev Caves, the one given to unbroken silence and the other to the life of the recluse, who struggled in stillness and prayer.
Notes: Named pair; not Onuphrius the Great or the Apostle Onesimus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Cornelius of Pereyaslavl
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Cornelius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A merchant's son who left his home for the monastic life and dwelt in silence and humble obedience at a monastery of Pereyaslavl, hiding his struggles from all.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Markella of Chios
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Markella, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A maiden of Chios who, fleeing the unnatural lust of her own father, would sooner die than sin, and was hunted down and slain by him; she is venerated as a virgin-martyr and a protector of those in danger.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Trophimus and 14 Others in Lycia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Trophimus, Theophilus, and thirteen others of Lycia who confessed Christ before the governor and, after the breaking of their limbs and other torments, were beheaded under Diocletian.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Apollinaris, Bishop of Ravenna
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Apollinaris, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A man of Antioch, a disciple of the Apostle Peter who set him as the first bishop of Ravenna, where through many sufferings he preached Christ and at last was martyred.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Vitalius, Bishop of Ravenna
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Vitalius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Ravenna venerated as a martyr; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; OCA gives no detail. Honest stub.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Anna of Leukadio
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Anna, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A noblewoman of Constantinople who, to escape an unwanted marriage, fled in the guise of a man under the name Euphemianos and lived out her life in ascetic struggle.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Great Martyr Christina of Tyre
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Christina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The daughter of the governor of Tyre, shut up by her father in a tower with idols of gold and silver; coming to know the true God, she broke the idols and gave the pieces to the poor, and through fearful torments at the hands of her father and two governors held fast to Christ unto death.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Polycarp, Archimandrite of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Polycarp, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An archimandrite of the Kiev Caves who guided the brotherhood in repentance and virtue in the days of the great Paterikon.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Hilarion of Tvali
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hilarion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An abbot of the Khakhuli monastery in Georgia, esteemed for his virtue, learning, and preaching of the word of God.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Athenagoras of Athens
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Athenagoras, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian philosopher of Athens who in the days of Marcus Aurelius addressed to the emperors a reasoned defense of the Christians against the slanders of the age, and wrote on the resurrection of the dead.
Works by the Saint: A Plea for the Christians; On the Resurrection of the Dead
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Olympias the Deaconess of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Mother Olympias, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wealthy widow of Constantinople who, made a deaconess, poured out her whole great fortune upon the Church and the poor, and stood faithfully by St John Chrysostom in his exile, sharing in his sufferings.
Notes: Served the Church as a deaconess.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Makarios, Abbot of Zheltovod and Unzha
Also Known As: Macarius of Unzha; Makarios of Zheltovod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Makarios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk from his twelfth year who founded a monastery at Yellow Water Lake; carried off captive when the Tatars destroyed it, he was set free for his holiness and founded another community at Unzha, leading his people through the wilderness with the mercy of God.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Jacob Netsvetov, Enlightener of Alaska
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Father Jacob, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of mixed Russian and Aleut birth, the first native-born clergyman of his people, who labored for decades among the peoples of Alaska, translating the Scriptures and services into their tongues and bringing many to Christ.
Notes: Saint of Alaska.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Hermolaus and Martyrs Hermippus and Hermocrates
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Hermolaus and Companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three priests who survived the burning of the church of Nicomedia and lived in hiding; Hermolaus brought the youth Pantoleon (St Panteleimon) to Christ, and all were martyred under Maximian.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Moses the Hungarian of the Kiev Caves
Also Known As: Moses of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Moses, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A servant of the holy Prince Boris, taken captive to Poland, who withstood the lust and threats of a noblewoman who would have made him her husband, suffering for his chastity; freed at last, he became a monk of the Kiev Caves.
Notes: A helper against fleshly temptation. Not Moses the Wonderworker of the Kiev Caves (Jul 28).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Paraskevi of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Paraskevi, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A maiden of Rome who, orphaned, gave away her goods and preached Christ openly, and after fierce torments was beheaded; she is invoked for the healing of the eyes.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Gerontios, Founder of the Skete of Saint Anna
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gerontios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Athonite monk who, withdrawing from a monastery exposed to raids, founded the Skete of St Anna on the Holy Mountain.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Savva III, Archbishop of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Savva, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An archbishop of Serbia in the early fourteenth century, numbered among the holy Serbian hierarchs.
Notes: Also commemorated Aug 30. OCA gives brief detail.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Holy Virgin Martyr Oraiozele
Also Known As: Oraiozele; disciple of the Apostle Andrew
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Oraiozele, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A woman of Byzantium converted and baptized by the Apostle Andrew, who gave her life for Christ.
Notes: Disciple of the Apostle Andrew.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Nicholas Kochanov the Fool-for-Christ of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Nicholas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A man of a noble Novgorod family who, fleeing the praise of men, took up the path of folly for Christ, and by his feigned quarrels with another holy fool rebuked the strife of the warring sides of the city.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Anthousa the Confessor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Anthousa, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An abbess of Mantinea in Paphlagonia who, holding fast to the veneration of the holy icons, was tortured under Constantine Copronymus and confessed the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Clement of Ochrid and his Companions
Also Known As: Clement; Nahum; Savva; Gorazd; Angelar; the Five Holy Confessors
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Father Clement and Companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Clement, bishop of Ochrid, and his fellow-disciples of Sts Cyril and Methodios — Nahum, Savva, Gorazd, and Angelar — who, driven from Moravia, carried the Slavonic letters and the Gospel to the Bulgarians, founding schools and enlightening the Slavic lands.
Notes: Named group; with Sts Cyril and Methodios they are honored as the Seven. Nahum is also commemorated Jun 20 (OS-1397).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostles and Deacons Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, and Parmenas of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostles, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Four of the seven deacons chosen by the apostles in Jerusalem to serve the tables, numbered also among the Seventy, who preached the Gospel and ended their lives in the apostolic labor.
Notes: Of the Seventy and of the first seven deacons (Acts 6).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Pitirim, Bishop of Tambov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Pitirim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Tambov who labored to build up the church life of his diocese, founding monasteries and a convent and shepherding his people in prayer.
Notes: Not St Pitirim, Bishop of Perm (OS-0558).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Moses the Wonderworker of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Moses, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves given to psalmody, prostrations, and the wearing of chains, who was glorified with the gift of wonders.
Notes: Not Moses the Hungarian of the Kiev Caves (Jul 26).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Julian of Dalmatia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Julian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Christian of Dalmatia who confessed Christ in Campania and was put to death under Antoninus.
Notes: Not Julian of Tarsus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Eustathius of Apamea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Eustathius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian soldier who openly confessed Christ and was tortured and martyred for the faith.
Notes: Not Eustathius of Mtskheta (Jul 29).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Paul, Founder of Xeropotamou Monastery
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Paul, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A son of the emperor Michael who forsook the purple for the monastic habit and founded the monasteries of Xeropotamou and of St Paul on Mount Athos.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Acacius of Apamea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Acacius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian who endured torture at the hands of several governors rather than sacrifice to idols, and was martyred for Christ.
Notes: Not Acacius the Centurion or Acacius of Sinai.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Melitina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Melitina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr commemorated on this day; few details of her life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review. Possibly also commemorated Oct 29 (verify).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Irene of Chrysovalantou
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Irene, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Cappadocian noblewoman who, instead of an imperial marriage, became a nun and abbess of the Chrysovalantou convent in Constantinople, attaining great heights of prayer and the gift of foresight.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable George the Builder of Iveron
Also Known As: George the Builder; George of Iveron
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian monk who served as abbot of the Iveron monastery on Mount Athos and labored in its building up.
Notes: Distinct from George the Hagiorite of Iveron (Jun 27, OS-1438).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Callinicus of Gangra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Callinicus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Cilicia who went about turning the heathen from their idols, and being seized was made to run in iron shoes until he was burned for Christ at Gangra.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Constantine and Cosmas, Abbots of Kosinsk
Also Known As: Constantine of Kosinsk; Cosmas of Kosinsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Constantine and Cosmas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Disciples in the tradition of Sts Barlaam of Khutyn and Anthony of Dymsk who founded a monastery on a wilderness peninsula at Kosinsk near Staraya Russa.
Notes: Named pair commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyr Seraphima of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Seraphima, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian virgin of Antioch who lived at Rome in the household of the noblewoman Sabina, whom she won to Christ, and was martyred under Hadrian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Theodota and her Three Sons
Also Known As: Theodota of Bithynia and her sons
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Theodota and your children, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian widow of Bithynia who raised her three sons in the faith and, refusing to deny Christ, was martyred together with them under Diocletian.
Notes: Named family group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Eustathius of Mtskheta
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Eustathius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Persian by birth and a fire-worshipper who, settling in Mtskheta in Georgia, came to faith in Christ and was baptized, and rather than return to the religion of his fathers was beheaded for the faith.
Notes: Not Eustathius of Apamea (Jul 28).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Bessarion, Bishop of Smolyan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Bessarion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Smolyan venerated as a martyr; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Child Schemamonk Bogolep of Chernyi Yar
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Bogolep, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A nobleman's young son who, falling sick, asked for and received the great schema, and reposing soon after at the age of seven was venerated as the child-saint Bogolep.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Roman of Kirzhach
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Roman, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of St Sergius of Radonezh whom Sergius left as abbot of the Annunciation monastery he had founded on the Kirzhach river.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Emperor Theodosius the Younger
Short Prayer (Intercession): Right-believing Emperor Theodosius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An emperor raised in piety from childhood, gentle and given to prayer and fasting, in whose reign the Third Ecumenical Council was gathered and the great walls of Constantinople were built.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostles Silas, Silvanus, Crescens, and Epenetus of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostles, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Apostles of the Seventy and fellow-laborers of Paul and Peter — Silas, who shared Paul's chains at Philippi and became bishop of Corinth; Silvanus, bishop of Thessalonica; Crescens, who preached in Galatia and Gaul; and Epenetus, the firstfruits of Achaia, bishop of Carthage.
Notes: Of the Seventy; commemorated together with the Apostle Andronicus (OS-1192).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr John the Soldier
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy John the Soldier, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A soldier under Julian the Apostate who, sent to hunt down Christians, secretly warned and sheltered them instead, and after imprisonment lived out his days in holiness; he is invoked for the finding of lost things and lost people.
Notes: Patron for the recovery of lost or stolen things and of runaways.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Polychronius, Bishop of Babylon, and Companions
Also Known As: Polychronius; Parmenius; Abdon; Sennen; and companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Polychronius and Companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Babylon with his clergy and companions who confessed Christ before the Persian and Roman authorities and were martyred in the persecution of Decius.
Notes: Named group; the Luke among them is not the Evangelist.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Valentine, Bishop of Interamna, and Companions
Also Known As: Valentine of Terni; Proculus; Ephebus; Apollonius; Abundius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Valentine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Interamna, given the gift of healing, who brought many to Christ — among them the household of a scholar whose lame son he raised up — and was beheaded with his disciples at Rome.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; named group.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Anatole II of Optina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anatole, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An elder of Optina, a cell-attendant and disciple of St Ambrose, who in the years of the Church's affliction received the multitudes who came to him with consoling and discerning counsel.
Notes: Among the Optina Elders; not Anatole I of Optina (OS-0539).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Tsotne Dadiani the Confessor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessor Tsotne, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian prince who, when his fellow nobles were seized and tortured by the Mongols for plotting to free their land, came of his own will and stripped himself to share their torments, and by this love so astonished the Mongols that they let them all go.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Eudocimus of Cappadocia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Eudocimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Cappadocian noble and imperial official who lived in purity, chastity, and secret almsgiving, doing good to all and reposing in holiness in the flower of his youth; his relics were found incorrupt.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Julitta at Caesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Julitta, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian woman of Caesarea who, defrauded of her property and offered its return if only she would deny Christ, chose to lose all rather than her Lord, and was burned for the faith.
Notes: Not Julitta the mother of Quiricus (OS-1533, Jul 15).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Germanus, Bishop of Auxerre
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Germanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former lawyer and governor who became bishop of Auxerre and gave away all to the poor; twice he crossed to Britain to confound the Pelagian heresy, and led the Britons to a bloodless victory with the cry of Alleluia.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; not Germanus of Paris (OS-1262).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Benjamin, Metropolitan of Petrograd, and Companions
Also Known As: Benjamin Kazansky; Archimandrite Sergius; George Novitsky; John Kovsharov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Benjamin and Companions, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The beloved metropolitan of Petrograd who, condemned on false charges in a show trial during the seizure of the church treasures, met death with forgiveness and peace, and was shot with the archimandrite Sergius and two laymen in 1922.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one; modern glorification.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Arsenius, Bishop of Ninotsminda
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Arsenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian monk, translator, calligrapher, and theologian who labored at the Iveron monastery on Athos and later served as bishop of Ninotsminda.
Notes: Not Arsenius the Great.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Joseph of Arimathea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A rich man of Arimathea, a member of the council and a secret disciple of the Lord, who took courage to ask Pilate for the body of Jesus, and wrapped it in fine linen and laid it in his own new tomb.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: The Seven Holy Maccabee Martyrs
Also Known As: Abim; Antonius; Gurias; Eleazar; Eusebonus; Alimus; Marcellus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Maccabee Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Seven brothers who, with their mother and their teacher, chose torture and death under Antiochus rather than taste forbidden food and forsake the Law of their fathers, the first martyrs for the true God before the coming of Christ.
Notes: Named brother group; commemorated with their mother Solomonia and their teacher Eleazar.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Solomonia, Mother of the Maccabees
Life Experience: Loss of a Child; Grief / Bereavement; Torture
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Solomonia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The mother of the seven Maccabee brothers, who watched each of her sons die rather than deny God and urged them on to the contest, and at the last gave up her own life, the very pattern of a martyr-mother.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Eleazar, Teacher of the Maccabees
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Eleazar, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An aged scribe and teacher of the Law who, refusing even to feign the eating of forbidden food lest he lead the young astray, accepted death under torture, leaving a noble example to all.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: The Nine Martyrs of Perge in Pamphylia
Also Known As: Leontios; Attos; Alexander; Kindeos; Mnesitheos; Kyriakos; Mineon; Katounos; Eukles
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Nine Martyrs of Perge, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Nine Christians of Perge, most of them farmers and one a carpenter, who confessed Christ before the governor and were martyred under Diocletian.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Nikodemos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Nikodemos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Pharisee and ruler of the Jews who came to the Lord by night, defended Him before the council, and with Joseph of Arimathea took down His body and buried it with myrrh and aloes.
Notes: His relics were found in 415 together with those of Stephen, Gamaliel, and Abibas.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Gamaliel
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Gamaliel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Pharisee and doctor of the Law, the teacher of the Apostle Paul, who counseled moderation toward the apostles before the council and is honored as a secret believer in Christ.
Notes: His relics were found in 415 with those of Stephen, Nikodemos, and his son Abibas.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Abibas
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Abibas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The son of Gamaliel, baptized in his youth, whose relics were found together with those of Stephen, Gamaliel, and Nikodemos.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Basil of Moscow the Fool-for-Christ
Also Known As: Basil the Blessed; Wonderworker of Moscow
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A holy fool of Moscow who went naked through every winter, foresaw things hidden and to come, and feared not to rebuke even Tsar Ivan the Terrible to his face; the great cathedral of the Protection on Red Square bears his name.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Basil of Kamen the Fool-for-Christ
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Basil, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Savior-Kamen monastery near Vologda who took up the path of folly for Christ; his relics were venerated at the monastery.
Notes: Limited biographical detail.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Stephen, Pope of Rome, and Companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Rome who upheld the unity of the Church against the Novatian schism and was martyred with companions under Valerian.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Photeine of Cyprus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Photeine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wonderworker of Cyprus venerated in local tradition; the details of her life are uncertain.
Notes: Local Cypriot tradition; chronology uncertain. Honest stub.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Dalmatus the Ascetic
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Dalmatus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A soldier under Theodosius who left the army for the monastic life under St Isaac, and became abbot of the monastery at Constantinople that bears his name.
Notes: Commemorated with Sts Isaac (OS-0863) and Faustus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Faustus the Ascetic
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Faustus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The son of St Dalmatus and a monk of the Dalmatian monastery, who succeeded his father as abbot and was renowned for strict fasting.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Anthony the Roman of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anthony, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Orthodox Christian born at Rome who, amid the persecution of the Orthodox in the West, was borne by God upon a great stone across the seas to Novgorod, where he founded a monastery and lived in holiness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Razhden the Protomartyr of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Razhden, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Persian noble who came to Georgia in the days of King Vakhtang Gorgasali, embraced Christ, and was crucified for refusing to return to the worship of fire; honored as the protomartyr of Georgia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Cosmas the Eunuch and Hermit of Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Cosmas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit of the wilderness of Pharan, remembered in the Spiritual Meadow for his fasting and his zeal for the Orthodox faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: The Nine Kherkheulidze Brothers and the Martyrs of Marabda
Also Known As: the Kherkheulidze martyrs; Martyrs of Marabda
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Marabda, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Nine brothers of the Kherkheulidze family, with their mother and sister and a great host of Georgian Christians, who fell in 1625 defending their faith and people against the Persian invasion.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Theodora of Thessaloniki and her Daughter Theopiste
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Theodora, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A woman of Aegina who, widowed at Thessaloniki, entered the monastic life with her young daughter Theopiste, and reposed in holiness in the year 892.
Notes: Named mother and daughter commemorated together. Nominally Aug 29; celebrated Aug 3 because of the Beheading of the Forerunner.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Holy Myrrh-bearer Salome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Myrrh-bearer Salome, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The wife of Zebedee and mother of the Apostles James and John, who followed the Lord, ministered to Him, and came with the other women bearing myrrh to the tomb.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: The Seven Youths of Ephesus
Also Known As: the Seven Sleepers; Maximilian; Iamblicus; Martinian; John; Dionysius; Constantine; Antoninus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Seven Youths of Ephesus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Seven young Christians of Ephesus who, hiding in a cave from the persecution of Decius, fell asleep and were sealed in; after nearly two hundred years they awoke, a living witness to the resurrection of the dead, and then reposed again in the Lord.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one; also commemorated Oct 22. Also commemorated Oct 22.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Eudokia of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Eudokia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian woman of Anatolia, learned in the Scriptures, who was carried off captive in the campaign of Shapur of Persia and was put to death for confessing Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Eleutherius the Cubicularius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Eleutherius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A chamberlain at the court of Maximian who secretly served Christ and, being discovered, was martyred for the faith.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Eusignius of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Eusignius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An old soldier of Antioch who had served in the Roman armies for sixty years and had seen the vision of the Cross with Constantine, who in his old age rebuked Julian the Apostate to his face and was beheaded for Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Job of Ushchelsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Job, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Solovki who founded a community in the Mezen region of the Russian north, and was slain by robbers in 1628.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Antherus, Pope of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Antherus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Rome who suffered martyrdom for Christ in the year 236.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Fabian, Pope of Rome, and Martyr Pontius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Fabian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Rome who fearlessly buried the bodies of the martyrs and ordered the care of the Church, and was himself martyred under Decius, together with his companion Pontius.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saints.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Cantidius, Cantidian, and Sibelius of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three Egyptian Christians who refused to deny Christ; two were stoned and the third was shot through with arrows.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Nonna, Mother of Saint Gregory the Theologian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Righteous Nonna, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A devout Cappadocian wife and mother who won her husband Gregory the Elder to Christ and bore to the Church St Gregory the Theologian, a model of the praying Christian mother.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theoctistus, Bishop of Chernigov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theoctistus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of the Kiev Caves who became abbot and then bishop of Chernigov, shepherding his flock in prayer and good order.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Pontius of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Pontius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Roman of senatorial family who came to Christ, gave away his wealth, and confessed the faith, suffering martyrdom in the persecution.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; OCA lists him separately from the Pontius commemorated with Pope Fabian; possible overlap, kept separate per the source.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Oswald, King and Martyr of Northumbria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy King Oswald, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian king of Northumbria who, returning from exile, set up the Cross and led his people to victory and to the faith, and fell at last in battle against the heathen, calling on God for the souls of his men; venerated as a martyr.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John Jacob the Chozebite
Also Known As: John Jacob of Neamt; John the Romanian; Hozevite
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John Jacob, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Romanian monk who withdrew to the Holy Land and lived as a hermit in the gorge of Chozeba near the Jordan, a man of prayer and spiritual writing; he reposed in 1960 and his relics were found incorrupt.
Notes: Recently glorified Orthodox saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Christos of Preveza
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Christos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr of Preveza; few details of his life are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Habakkuk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Habakkuk, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A new martyr known only from an old manuscript commemoration; no account of his martyrdom survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA notes the record is sparse. Not the Prophet Habakkuk.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Dometius of Persia and his Two Disciples
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Dometius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Persian who came to Christ and became a monk near Nisibis, healing and teaching, who with two disciples was stoned to death in his cave by order of Julian the Apostate.
Notes: Named group; the Dometius long expected at Aug 7. Distinct from Dometius of Philotheou (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Pimen the Much-Ailing of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Pimen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves who was sick from birth to death, and who bore his lifelong infirmity with such thanksgiving that he was healed only on the day he reposed; a helper of those who suffer in sickness.
Notes: Not Pimen the Faster of the Far Caves (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Mercurius, Bishop of Smolensk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Mercurius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of the Kiev Caves who is held to have been bishop of Smolensk in the hard years of the Tatar invasions.
Notes: Distinct from the Martyr Mercurius of Smolensk (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Marinus the Soldier of Caesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Marinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A soldier of Caesarea who, about to be made a centurion, was denounced as a Christian, and choosing the Gospel over the sword was beheaded for Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Asterius the Senator of Caesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Asterius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Roman senator who, taking up the body of the martyr Marinus to bury it with honor, was himself put to death for Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Horus of the Thebaid
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Horus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit of the Egyptian desert who after long years of solitude gathered and guided a brotherhood of monks, and was granted angelic visitations.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyr Potamia the Wonderworker
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Potamia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A virgin-martyr honored as a wonderworker, who died by the sword for Christ; few details are preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA notes she is sometimes wrongly listed as male (Potamius).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Dometius of Philotheou
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Dometius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An elder of the Philotheou monastery on Mount Athos who lived in silence and asceticism.
Notes: Distinct from Martyr Dometius of Persia (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Anthony of Optina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anthony, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An elder of Optina, brother of the abbot Moses, who bore long and painful illness with thanksgiving and guided many in the spiritual life.
Notes: Among the Optina Elders.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theodora of Sihla
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Theodora, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Romanian woman who, after marriage, withdrew to the wilderness of Sihla, where she lived in a cave in extreme austerity, fed in her last days by a raven.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Nicanor, Wonderworker of Mount Calistratus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nicanor, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wonderworker of Mount Calistratus; no details of his life are preserved on the page.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theodosius the New, Healer of Peloponnese
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theodosius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A saint of the Peloponnese venerated as a healer; no details of his life are preserved on the page.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Holy Martyr Mercurius of Smolensk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Mercurius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A soldier who, at the bidding of the Mother of God, went out alone against the host of Batu Khan and delivered Smolensk, laying down his life for the city; venerated as its martyr and protector.
Notes: Distinct from Bishop Mercurius of Smolensk (same day).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Narcissus, Bishop of Jerusalem
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Narcissus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An aged bishop of Jerusalem of holy life who, slandered by the wicked, withdrew into the desert until his accusers were overtaken by judgment, and then returned to his flock and was glorified with the gift of wonders.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Gregory the Iconographer of the Kiev Caves
Also Known As: Gregory of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves and fellow-laborer of St Alypius, an iconographer who painted many wonderworking icons venerated throughout the Russian land.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Myron the Wonderworker, Bishop of Crete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Myron, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A farmer of Crete so generous that he let the poor take freely from his harvest, who was made bishop of the island and worked great wonders, even turning back a river in flood.
Notes: Not the New Martyr Myron of Crete (Mar 20, OS-0855).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Eleutherius and Leonidas of Constantinople and the Infants with them
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Christians who, together with many infants, were given to the fire in a persecution against the Church.
Notes: Named group commemorated as one.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Gregory of Sinai
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Sinai, Athos, and the Balkans who, in an age when the inner prayer was nearly forgotten, kindled it anew, teaching the watchfulness of the heart and the prayer of Jesus to a host of disciples across the Orthodox lands.
Notes: A chief teacher of hesychast prayer.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Euthymius the Elder of the Davit-Gareji Wilderness
Also Known As: Euthymius of St John the Baptist Monastery
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Euthymius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An abbot of the monastery of St John the Baptist in the Davit-Gareji wilderness of Georgia, remembered as a learned and laborious elder.
Notes: Limited detail; Georgian monastic saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Triandaphyllus of Stara Zagora
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Triandaphyllus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A youth of fifteen from Zagora in Thessaly who, pressed to deny Christ, held fast to the faith and was beheaded at Constantinople in 1680.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Zosimas of Tuman
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Zosimas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monastic saint connected with Tuman in Serbia; no details of his life are preserved on the page.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no detail. Flagged for review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: The Ten Ascetics of Egypt and the Two Martyrs of Tyre
Also Known As: ten Egyptian ascetics; two martyrs of Tyre
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Ascetics and Martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Ten ascetics of Egypt who perished at sea and two martyrs of Tyre who were dragged over the ground for Christ, commemorated together.
Notes: Named group; individual names not preserved. Honest stub.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Matthias
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Matthias, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Chosen by lot to take the place of Judas among the Twelve Apostles. He preached Christ and suffered for the faith.
Notes: Numbered among the Twelve, replacing Judas (Acts 1).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Anthony of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Anthony, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Alexandria who was tortured with iron hooks and burned for confessing Christ, exhorting others even in the flames.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Psoi of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Psoi, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A fourth-century disciple of Saint Pachomius the Great who lived ascetically in the desert of Sketis.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Julian of Constantinople and those with him
Also Known As: Julian; Marcian; John; James; Alexius; Demetrius; Photius; Peter; Leontius; Maria the Patrician; Gregory the Protospatharios
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Constantinople, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A group of Christians of Constantinople who suffered in 730 under the iconoclast emperor Leo the Isaurian for defending the holy icons.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Macarius of Oredezhsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint Alexander of Svir who founded a monastery on the Oredezha River near Lake Ladoga; reposed in 1532.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Ignatius (Bazyluk)
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Ignatius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Saint Onuphrios Monastery who suffered in the modern persecution of Orthodox Christians.
Notes: Local modern martyr; canonization/veneration to be confirmed by clergy review.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Euthymios of Rhodes
