Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Euthymios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Metropolitan of Rhodes under Ottoman rule who was arrested and martyred after being accused of conspiracy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr and Archdeacon Laurence of Rome and those with him
Also Known As: Laurence; Pope Sixtus; Felicissimus; Agapitus; Romanus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Laurence, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archdeacon of Rome who distributed the Church's treasure to the poor and was martyred in 258 under Valerian, together with Pope Sixtus, the deacons Felicissimus and Agapitus, and the soldier Romanus.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saints; named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Laurence of Kaluga
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Laurence, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An early-sixteenth-century holy fool near Kaluga who lived in hidden ascetic poverty and was revered as a wonderworker.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr and Archdeacon Euplus of Catania
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Euplus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An archdeacon of Catania in Sicily who preached openly from the Gospel during Diocletian's persecution and was tortured and executed in 304.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Basil and Theodore of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Basil and Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two ascetics of the Kiev Near Caves who were killed in the eleventh century after refusing to reveal a hidden treasure and forsake their monastic integrity.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theodore of Ostrog
Also Known As: Prince Theodore of Ostrog; Theodosius of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prince of Ostrog descended from Saint Vladimir who defended Orthodoxy in Volhynia and later became a monk of the Kiev Caves.
Notes: OCA lists him as 'Theodosius (Prince Theodore of Ostrog) of the Kiev Caves'.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyr Susanna of Rome and those with her
Also Known As: Susanna; Gabinus; Caius; Maximus; Claudius; Praepedigna; Alexander; Cutias
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Virgin Martyr Susanna, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian virgin of Rome, kin to the imperial house, who refused a pagan marriage and suffered martyrdom with members of her household and clergy.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saints; named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Niphon, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Niphon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk who became Patriarch of Constantinople and endured repeated exile, later serving the Church in Wallachia and revered for pastoral holiness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Passarion of Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Passarion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A fifth-century Palestinian ascetic who founded a monastery in Jerusalem and served as vicar-bishop, a contemporary of Saint Euthymius the Great.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Mary Syncletica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Mary, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A woman of high rank healed through the Icon of the Savior Not-Made-by-Hands during the reign of Emperor Tiberius.
Notes: OCA gives limited details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Anicetus and Photius of Nicomedia and those with them
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Anicetus and Photius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Anicetus, a military official of Nicomedia, denounced Diocletian's persecution, and with his nephew Photius and others endured martyrdom.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Alexander, Bishop of Comana
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned ascetic who hid his holiness by selling charcoal, was chosen Bishop of Comana, and later suffered martyrdom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Gerontius and those with him of David Gareji
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of David Gareji, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Monks of the Davit-Gareji Wilderness in Georgia who were killed during raids on the monastery.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Pamphilus and Capiton
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Pamphilus and Capiton, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two martyrs who were beheaded near Constantinople.
Notes: OCA gives only brief details. Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Tikhon of Zadonsk
Also Known As: Tikhon, Bishop of Voronezh; Timothy Sokolov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Tikhon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Voronezh who retired to Zadonsk Monastery and became one of Russia's most beloved spiritual writers, living in prayer, humility, and care for the poor.
Notes: Aug 13 also commemorates the second finding of his relics.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Hippolytus of Rome and those with him
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Hippolytus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prison official of Rome converted through Saint Laurence, who buried Laurence's body and was martyred with members of his household.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saints; named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Holy Empress Irene (Schema-nun Xenia)
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Empress Irene, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Hungarian princess who became Byzantine Empress as wife of John II Komnenos, known for her charity, and who took monastic vows as Xenia before her repose.
Notes: Hungarian by birth.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyr Dositheus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Monastic Martyr Dositheus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monastic martyr commemorated by the Church, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA lists him but supplies no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Prophet Micah
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Micah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Twelve Minor Prophets, from Moresheth in Judah, who preached repentance and foretold Bethlehem as the birthplace of the Messiah.
Notes: The Minor Prophet of Moresheth; distinct from Prophet Micah (son of Imlah) commemorated Jan 5.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Marcellus, Bishop of Apamea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Marcellus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Cypriot-born bishop of Apamea in Syria who opposed pagan worship and was killed while working to destroy a pagan temple in 375.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Nazarius, Metropolitan of Kutaisi-Gaenati, and his companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Nazarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Metropolitan Nazarius and his companions suffered during the anti-Church persecution in Georgia after the Russian Revolution.
Notes: Among the Georgian New Martyrs; named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Makarios the Roman of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Makarios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Born to a wealthy Roman family, he rejected worldly honors, came to the Russian north, and embraced a life of ascetic poverty.
Notes: Macarius the Roman of Novgorod.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Holy Unmercenary Physician Diomedes
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Diomedes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A physician of Tarsus who healed body and soul without charge and preached the Gospel to the sick; though he reposed before his arrest under Diocletian, soldiers still beheaded him.
Notes: Distinct from the Martyr Diomedes commemorated Sep 2.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Cherimon of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Cherimon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Egyptian desert ascetic of Sketis remembered in the Lausiac History and the sayings of the desert fathers.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Anthony of Martkofeli
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anthony, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Thirteen Syrian Fathers who established monastic life in Georgia.
Notes: OCA lists him but gives no life details on this date.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Christopher of Guria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Christopher, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian martyr of Guria whose memory is preserved in Church tradition, though few details survive.
Notes: OCA notes little information exists.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Isaac of Karnu
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Isaac, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr of Karnu in Georgia, commemorated by the Church though no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Joseph of Karnu
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr of Karnu in Georgia, commemorated by the Church though no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Joachim of Osogovo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Joachim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the great Bulgarian hermits, who lived hidden in a mountain cave on Osogovo and drew many to ascetic life.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Roman of Banat
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Roman, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A saint of the Banat region commemorated by the Church, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Eustathius of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Eustathius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian hierarch commemorated by the Church, of whom no detailed life is supplied on this date.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Raphael of Banat
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Raphael, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A saint of the Banat region commemorated by the Church, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Nikodemos of Meteora
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Nikodemos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hieromartyr of Meteora in Thessaly who suffered for Christ in 1551.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Stamatius of Volos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Stamatius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Volos who, falsely accused of accepting Islam, confessed Christ and was beheaded at Constantinople in 1680.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Gerasimus the New Ascetic of Cephalonia
Commonly Asked Intercessions: Mental Health; Deliverance from the Occult
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gerasimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Greek monk who labored on Zakynthos, Mount Athos, and Cephalonia, founding a women's monastery; the patron saint of Cephalonia, often invoked for the mentally afflicted and demon-oppressed.
Notes: Also commemorated Oct 20.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Holy Martyr Constantine Brancoveanu and those with him
Also Known As: Constantine Brâncoveanu; Constantine; Stephen; Radu; Matthew; Ianache
Life Experience: Imprisonment; Loss of a Child; Persecution
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Constantine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Prince of Wallachia martyred at Constantinople in 1714 with his four sons and his counselor Ianache after all refused to renounce Christ under Ottoman pressure.
Notes: Named family group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Myron of Cyzicus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Myron, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A gentle and courageous priest of Achaia who defended his flock during the persecution of Decius and was arrested during worship and martyred in 250.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Alypios the Iconographer of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Alypios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the earliest and most famed iconographers of the Kiev Caves, who learned from Greek masters and painted icons as a monastic labor.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Paul and Juliana of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Paul and Juliana, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A brother and sister of Ptolemais in Phoenicia who suffered under Aurelian after Paul openly confessed Christ and Juliana joined him.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Thyrsus, Leucius, Coronatus, and those with them
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Thyrsus and those with him, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Martyrs who suffered in Bithynian Caesarea and Apollonia under Decius.
Notes: OCA notes Coronatus may be the same as Cornutus commemorated Sep 12. Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Patroclus of Troyes
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Patroclus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Troyes given to prayer, fasting, and charity, who was martyred under Aurelian.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Straton, Philip, Eutychian, and Cyprian of Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Straton and those with him, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Four Christians who rebuked idol-worship at the circus, brought others to Christ, and suffered martyrdom at Nicomedia.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Theodoritus, Enlightener of the Lapps
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theodoritus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Solovki monk who entered the monastery at thirteen and later evangelized the Lapps of the far north.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Leucius of Volokolamsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Leucius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint Paphnutius of Borovsk who founded the Dormition Monastery near Volokolamsk.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Philip of Sukhona
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Philip, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit on Mount Yankov by the Sukhona River, around whose place of ascetic struggle a monastery later grew.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Demetrius of Samarina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Demetrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Greek monk of Samarina in Epirus who endured torture and martyrdom under the rule of Ali Pasha.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Tbeli Abuseridze
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Tbeli, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian noble and scholar of Adjara known for theological, hymnographic, and literary work amid family loss and political turmoil.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Florus and Laurus of Illyria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Florus and Laurus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Brother stone-masons who confessed Christ while building a pagan temple and were martyred by being cast into a well; widely invoked as protectors of horses and livestock.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Hermes, Serapion, and Polyaenus of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Hermes, Serapion, and Polyaenus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three Christians of Rome who suffered for Christ in the early persecutions.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saints; named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Emilian and those with him
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Emilian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop and those with him who suffered for Christ.
Notes: OCA lists this group with few details; era uncertain.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John V, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Constantinople from 669 to 674.
Notes: Brief OCA entry.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint George I, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Constantinople from 678 to 683.
Notes: Brief OCA entry.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Macarius of Pelekete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An abbot of the Pelekete monastery who confessed the veneration of icons and suffered exile during the iconoclast persecution.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable John of Rila
Also Known As: Ivan of Rila; John, Abbot of Rila
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The great ascetic of Bulgaria who withdrew to the Rila wilderness and became the spiritual father of Rila monasticism and patron of the Bulgarian people.
Notes: Principal repose Aug 18; also commemorated Jul 1 and Oct 19 (translations of relics). Oct 19 = translation of his relics to Trnovo (1238).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Sophronius of Saint Anne Skete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Sophronius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An eighteenth-century monk of the Skete of Saint Anne on Mount Athos.
Notes: Brief OCA entry.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints Barnabas and Sophronius of Asia Minor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Barnabas and Sophronius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Athenian uncle and nephew who lived as ascetics on Mount Mela near Trebizond and reposed in 412.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Christopher of Gazara
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Christopher, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monastery superior commemorated by the Church in the seventh century.
Notes: OCA gives few life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: The Four Ascetics and Three Hundred Saints
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy ascetics and martyrs, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Four desert ascetics and three hundred saints who were burned for destroying idols.
Notes: Group commemoration; individual names not supplied by OCA.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Christodoulos the Philosopher
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Christodoulos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian churchman of Sakara in Imereti, known for his knowledge of Scripture, languages, and philosophy placed in service of Christian teaching.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Andrew Stratelates and those with him
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Andrew, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Roman commander in Cilicia who led his soldiers to faith in Christ and was martyred with 2,593 of them under Maximian.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Timothy, Agapius, and Thekla of Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Timothy, Agapius, and Thekla, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three Christians martyred at Caesarea in Palestine in 304; Timothy was known as a teacher of the Scriptures.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Theophanes of Docheiariou
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theophanes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Athonite monk of Docheiariou from Ioannina who became abbot and later founded a monastery, remembered for rescuing his nephew from captivity.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Prophet Samuel
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Samuel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The last of the judges of Israel and a prophet who anointed Saul and David; his birth followed his mother Hannah's prayer for a child.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Philip, Bishop of Heraclea, and those with him
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Philip, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Heraclea in Thrace who suffered under Diocletian with Severus, Memnon, and thirty-seven soldiers.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Heliodorus and Dosa of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Heliodorus and Dosa, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two Persian martyrs who suffered for Christ under Shapur II in 380.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Lucius of Crete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Lucius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian senator who was beheaded on Crete in 310 for confessing Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Theokhares of Neopolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Theokhares, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An orphaned Christian youth taken under Ottoman pressure who refused to deny Christ and was martyred in 1740.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Thaddeus of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Thaddeus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Hebrew of Edessa, one of the Seventy, who preached Christ in Edessa and beyond.
Notes: Apostle of the Seventy; distinct from Jude (Thaddeus) of the Twelve, commemorated Jun 19.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Bassa of Edessa and her sons
Life Experience: Loss of a Child; Grief / Bereavement; Persecution
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Bassa, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian mother who taught the faith to her sons despite her pagan-priest husband, and suffered martyrdom with them under Maximian Galerius.
Notes: Named family group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Abramius of Smolensk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Abramius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A preacher of repentance from a wealthy family who became a monk and archimandrite in Smolensk, enduring false accusation before his vindication.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Ephraim of Smolensk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Ephraim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint Abramius who preserved his teacher's life and continued the Smolensk monastic tradition.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Abramius the Lover-of-Labor of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Abramius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Near Caves remembered for humble labor and ascetic struggle.
Notes: OCA says his dating is uncertain.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Sarmean, Catholicos of Kartli
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Sarmean, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Catholicos of Kartli who led the Georgian Church amid the pressures of Arab rule.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Simeon of Samokov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Simeon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A new martyr of Samokov in Bulgaria, commemorated by the Church though no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Raphael of Sisatovac
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Raphael, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian hieromartyr of Sisatovac commemorated by the Church, of whom few details survive.
Notes: OCA also lists him as Hieromartyr Raphael of Serbia; honest stub.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Schema-nun Martha of Diveyevo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Martha, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A schema-nun of Diveyevo, born in 1810 to a righteous peasant family, glorified among the Diveyevo saints.
Notes: Glorified among the Diveyevo saints.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Agathonicus of Nicomedia and those with him
Also Known As: Agathonicus; Zoticus; Theoprepius; Acindynus; Severian; Zeno
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Agathonicus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Agathonicus and his companions suffered under Maximian after confessing Christ and teaching others the faith.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Athanasios and Venerable Anthousa and those with them
Also Known As: Athanasios of Tarsus; Anthousa of Seleucia; Charisimos; Neophytos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Athanasios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Anthousa, a woman of a wealthy pagan family, sought baptism from Bishop Athanasios and embraced ascetic life; Athanasios and others with her suffered for Christ.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyr Eulalia of Barcelona and Martyr Felix
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Virgin Martyr Eulalia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Christian of Barcelona who openly rebuked the persecution and was tortured and killed at the age of fourteen; Felix is commemorated with her.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saints; named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Bogolep of Uglich
Also Known As: Bogolep, disciple of Paisius of Uglich
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Bogolep, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint Paisius of Uglich, connected in tradition with the finding of a wonderworking Protection icon.
Notes: OCA gives limited details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Isaac I of Optina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Isaac, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An elder of Optina who reposed in 1894, later glorified among the Optina Elders.
Notes: Glorified among the Optina Elders.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Lupus of Thessalonica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Lupus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A faithful servant of Saint Demetrius of Thessalonica who was present at his master's martyrdom and later confessed Christ himself.
Notes: Also commemorated Oct 26 (with St Demetrius).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Irenaeus of Lyons
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Irenaeus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of the apostolic tradition through Saint Polycarp who became Bishop of Lyons and wrote against the heresies of his day; martyred in the early third century.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Works by the Saint: Against Heresies; On the Apostolic Preaching
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints Eutychius and Florentius of Nursia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Eutychius and Florentius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two sixth-century Italian monks of Valcastoria; Eutychius guided others as superior while Florentius was known for simple ascetic holiness.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saints; named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Callinicus, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Callinicus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Constantinople from 693 to 705 who suffered under Emperor Justinian II and died after harsh treatment.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Eutyches
Also Known As: Eutyches, disciple of John the Theologian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Eutyches, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of the Apostles John and Paul, called an apostle for his missionary labors, who suffered as a hieromartyr.
Notes: Not one of the Seventy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Arsenius, Abbot of Komel
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Arsenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A noble-born monk trained at the Trinity-Sergius Lavra who renounced honor to found the Komel monastic community in Vologda, copying books with his own hand.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Tation of Claudiopolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Tation, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Bithynia arrested and tortured under Diocletian, who was martyred after refusing to deny Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyr Syra of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Virgin Martyr Syra, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The daughter of a Zoroastrian priest who became Christian despite her family's opposition and suffered martyrdom in Persia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint George Limniotes, Confessor
Also Known As: George the Confessor of Mount Olympus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessor George, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Mount Olympus who suffered mutilation for venerating the holy icons and died in 718.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Hieromartyr Cosmas of Aitolia, Equal of the Apostles
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Cosmas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk-priest and tireless preacher who traveled widely teaching and founding schools among Orthodox Christians under Ottoman rule, and was executed in 1779.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Martyrius, Archbishop of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Martyrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Novgorod who founded the Transfiguration monastery in 1192.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Serapion the Wonderworker of David Gareji
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Serapion, pray to God for us.

Notes: OCA gives few life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Titus of the Seventy

Notes: Apostle of the Seventy; distinct from the Venerable Tituses (Feb 27, Apr 2).

Name: Confessors Barses and Eulogius, Bishops of Edessa, and Protogenes of Carrhae

Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Menas, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Menas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Constantinople from 536 to 552 under Justinian, known for pastoral service and defense of Orthodoxy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Hieromartyr Vladimir (Moshchansky) of Tver
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Vladimir, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Tver who suffered in the modern persecution of the Russian Church.
Notes: Among the New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia; OCA gives no further life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John the Cappadocian, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Constantinople from 518 to 520, remembered by Saint Photius as a man of virtue.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Epiphanius, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Epiphanius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Constantinople from 520 to 535, who reposed peacefully.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Constantia of Paphos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Constantia, pray to God for us.

Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Adrian of Uglich
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Adrian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An early disciple and cell-attendant of Saint Paisius of Uglich who shared in the labors and visions of the Uglich community.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Joasaph, son of King Abenner
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Joasaph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prince of the Barlaam-and-Joasaph tradition who renounced royal life for Christian asceticism.
Notes: Traditional hagiographic figure; OCA gives no details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Pelagia, Paraskeva, and Maria of Diveyevo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed mothers of Diveyevo, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three blessed women of Diveyevo who embraced the path of holy foolishness for Christ.
Notes: Glorified among the Diveyevo saints; named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Pimen the Great
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Pimen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Egyptian desert father whose sayings became central to monastic wisdom, who lived in strict asceticism with his brothers.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Kuksha and Venerable Pimen of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Kuksha and Venerable Pimen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Kuksha preached among pagan peoples and was martyred, while Pimen the Faster reposed near the same time; both rest among the Kiev Caves saints.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Hosius the Confessor, Bishop of Córdoba
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Hosius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The long-serving Bishop of Córdoba and advisor to Constantine, associated with the First Ecumenical Council, who suffered for Orthodoxy against Arian pressure.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Liberius, Pope of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Liberius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Rome who defended Saint Athanasius and Orthodox teaching against Arian pressure and suffered exile.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Pimen of Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Pimen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A desert monk of the Rouba wilderness remembered in The Spiritual Meadow for spiritual discernment.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Anthusa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Anthusa, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr commemorated by the Church, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Savva of Benephali
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Savva, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A venerable monk commemorated by the Church, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Caesarius, Bishop of Arles
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Caesarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Arles known for his preaching, pastoral reform, and monastic rules, who guided clergy and laity in southern Gaul.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Phanourius the Newly-Revealed
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Great Martyr Phanourius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr known chiefly through the discovery of his icon on Rhodes, widely invoked in popular devotion for the finding of lost things.
Notes: Tradition is uncertain about his background and dates.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Susanna, Queen of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Susanna, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian noblewoman and wife of Prince Varsken who refused to follow his apostasy and suffered imprisonment and abuse for Christ.
Notes: Also commemorated Oct 17.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Savva of Pskov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Savva, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Athonite monk who came to Pskov and founded the Krypetsk Monastery, living in solitude and strict asceticism.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Hezekiah
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Hezekiah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: King of Judah who restored the worship of the Lord and trusted God during the Assyrian threat, as recorded in 2 Kings.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Anastasius of Strumica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Anastasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of the Strumica region, born in 1774, who suffered martyrdom for confessing Christ in 1794.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Arkadios the Wonderworker, Bishop of Arsinoe
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Arkadios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop and wonderworker of Arsinoe in Cyprus in the late third and early fourth centuries.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Alexander, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Constantinople who defended Orthodox teaching against Arianism in the time of Constantine the Great.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Paul the New, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Paul, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Cypriot who became Patriarch of Constantinople during iconoclast rule, later repenting of his weakness under pressure and supporting the veneration of the icons.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Alexander, Abbot of Svir
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A northern Russian monastic founder of great ascetic life and spiritual gifts, who was granted a vision of the Holy Trinity and became a major figure of Russian monasticism.
Notes: Principal repose Aug 30; uncovering of relics commemorated Apr 17.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Christopher of Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Christopher, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Roman monk of the Monastery of Saint Theodosius near Jerusalem, remembered in The Spiritual Meadow.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Fantinus of Calabria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Fantinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Calabrian monk given to monastic life from childhood, known for severe asceticism and wonderworking.
Notes: Pre-schism Italian Orthodox saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Spyridon, Patriarch of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Spyridon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Serbia from 1382 to 1388 who cared for monastic communities during civil and ecclesial unrest.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Macarius, Patriarch of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Serbia from 1557 to 1574 who supported education, printing, and the restoration of church life under Ottoman rule.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Gabriel I, Patriarch of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Gabriel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Serbia who sought aid for his Church under Ottoman pressure and was later martyred.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Gregory the Bishop
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop commemorated by the Church, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Cyprian, Bishop of Carthage
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Cyprian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A well-educated orator who converted, became Bishop of Carthage and a foundational early Christian writer, and was martyred in 258.
Notes: Pre-schism African saint.
Works by the Saint: On the Unity of the Church; On the Lapsed
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Gennadius, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Gennadius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Patriarch of Constantinople from 458, remembered through The Spiritual Meadow and for his pastoral holiness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Aidan of Lindisfarne
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Aidan, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Iona who became missionary bishop of Lindisfarne and re-evangelized Northumbria through humility and pastoral care.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Eanswythe, Abbess of Folkestone
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Eanswythe, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A daughter of King Eadbald of Kent who became a nun and abbess at Folkestone, venerated as an early English monastic woman.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Theodotus and Rufina of Caesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Theodotus and Rufina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Christian parents of Saint Mamas, of patrician rank, who were imprisoned for refusing the imperial decree against the faith; Rufina gave birth to Mamas in prison, where both reposed.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row; distinct from the row for their son, the Martyr Mamas of Caesarea (OS-0120).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Joannicius, first Patriarch of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Joannicius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A native of Prizren who served as Archbishop from 1339 and was elevated as the first Patriarch of Serbia in 1346.
Notes: Distinct from Joannicius of Devich (OS-1067) and Hieromartyr Joannicius of Serbia (OS-1287).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Hieromartyr Gorazd of Prague
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Gorazd, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Orthodox bishop of the Czech lands who took responsibility after the Nazi reprisals following the Heydrich assassination and was executed in 1942.
Notes: Modern martyr of Bohemia and Moravia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Eupsychius of Caesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Eupsychius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Cappadocian Christian who was imprisoned and tortured under Hadrian and eventually beheaded for Christ.
Notes: Distinct from the Martyr Eupsychius of Caesarea commemorated Apr 9 (OS-0966).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Luke, Abbot of Batheos Ryako
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Luke, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk and later abbot of the Deep Stream (Batheos Ryako) Transfiguration Monastery near Triglia, who reposed in peace at the end of the tenth century.
Notes: Not Luke the Evangelist.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Niketas the Hidden of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Niketas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A layman of Constantinople who held a scribal office and practiced hidden asceticism while living in the midst of the city.
Notes: An example of hidden holiness in the world.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Theodora of Alexandria
Life Experience: Repentance from grave sin; Slander / False Accusation
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Theodora, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A married woman who, after falling into sin, repented deeply and entered a men's monastery disguised as a monk, bearing false accusation and hardship with humility.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Demetrius, Euanthia, and Demetrian of Skepsis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Demetrius, Euanthia, and Demetrian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prince-prefect, his wife, and their son, converted through Saint Cornelius the Centurion, who suffered martyrdom together at Skepsis.
Notes: Named family group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Diodorus, Didymus, and Diomedes of Laodicea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Diodorus, Didymus, and Diomedes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three Christians of Laodicea who were flogged to death for confessing Christ.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Ia of Persia and 9,000 Martyrs with her
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Ia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian woman arrested with thousands of Christians under Shapur II who refused apostasy and was martyred with the great company.
Notes: Named numerical group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Autonomus, Bishop in Italy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Autonomus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop from Italy who fled Diocletian's persecution and evangelized in Bithynia, where he was killed by pagans while serving the Liturgy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Bassian of Tiksnensk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Bassian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A peasant tailor who left worldly life to become a monk under Saint Theodosius of Totma and lived in ascetic labor at Tiksnensk.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Julian of Galatia and 40 Martyrs with him
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Julian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest who, hiding with forty Christian companions, was arrested for celebrating the services and suffered martyrdom with them.
Notes: Named group kept as one row; distinct from Hieromartyr Julian of Ancyra commemorated Sep 13.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Theodore of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian layman of Alexandria imprisoned and tortured for confessing Christ.
Notes: Not Theodore, Bishop of Alexandria.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Coronatus, Bishop of Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Coronatus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop who voluntarily confessed Christ during the persecutions of Decius and Valerian and was tortured and martyred.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Athanasius of Serpukhov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Athanasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple in the Russian monastic tradition associated with Serpukhov, drawn from youth to prayer and renunciation.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Cornelius the Centurion
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Cornelius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The first Gentile received into the Church by Saint Peter, as recorded in Acts; later tradition remembers him as a bishop and martyr.
Notes: The centurion of Acts 10.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Chronides of Alexandria and those with him
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Chronides, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A group of Christians of Alexandria who endured fierce torments and death for confessing Christ.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Macrobius and Gordian and those with them
Also Known As: Macrobius; Gordian; Elias; Zoticus; Lucian; Valerian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Macrobius and Gordian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Christian court officials and companions who suffered martyrdom at Tomi after refusing to renounce Christ.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Julian of Galatia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Julian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Ancyra who confessed Christ under Licinius and suffered martyrdom.
Notes: Distinct from Hieromartyr Julian of Galatia and the 40 Martyrs commemorated Sep 12.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Greatmartyr Ketevan, Queen of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Greatmartyr Ketevan, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Queen of Kakheti who was held captive by Shah Abbas of Persia and, refusing to renounce Christ or accept Islam, was tortured to death in 1624.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Peter of Atroe
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic near the Asian Mount Olympus who spent his life in fasting and unceasing prayer.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Hierotheus the Younger of Iviron
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hierotheus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Iviron Monastery on Mount Athos, born in 1686 at Kalamata to wealthy and devout parents.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John of Prislop
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A sixteenth-century monk and hermit of the Prislop Monastery in southwestern Romania.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyr Macarius of Dionysiou
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Monastic Martyr Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monastic martyr of Dionysiou Monastery on Mount Athos, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Greatmartyr Niketas the Goth
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Greatmartyr Niketas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian Goth who confessed Christ during the persecution among his people and was martyred in 372.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Theodotus, Asclepiodotus, and Maximus of Adrianopolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Theodotus, Asclepiodotus, and Maximus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Prominent Christians of Adrianopolis whose example drew many to the faith and who suffered martyrdom under Maximian Galerius.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Porphyrius the Actor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Porphyrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An actor performing a mock baptism to ridicule the faith before Julian the Apostate who was truly converted in the act, confessed Christ, and was martyred.
Notes: An actor by profession.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Philotheus the Presbyter
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Father Philotheus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A married priest and father of Bithynia known for prayer, fasting, charity, and wonderworking.
Notes: A married parish priest.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Joseph, Abbot of Alaverdi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Thirteen Syrian Fathers who came to Georgia with Saint John of Zedazeni, founded monastic life at Alaverdi, and strengthened Christianity in Georgia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Joseph the New of Partos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk born in Dalmatia in 1568 who became Metropolitan of Timisoara, remembered for pastoral care, asceticism, and wonderworking.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Bessarion, Archbishop of Larissa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Bessarion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Larissa in Thessaly who founded the Dusiko monastery.
Notes: OCA gives brief details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Gerasimus of Survia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gerasimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Founder of a Holy Trinity monastery near Makrinitsa in the Zagora region, known as Survia.
Notes: OCA gives brief details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr John of Crete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Cretan farmer working near New Ephesus who confessed Christ and was martyred under Ottoman rule.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Cyprian, Metropolitan of Moscow and All Russia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Cyprian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian Athonite who became Metropolitan of Kiev and All Rus', laboring for Church unity and the literary renewal of Rus'.
Notes: Repose commemoration.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Sebastiana of Heraclea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Sebastiana, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of the Apostle Paul who confessed Christ under Domitian and suffered martyrdom after tortures.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Melitine of Marcianopolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Melitine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian woman of Marcianopolis in Thrace who destroyed pagan idols and converted others before suffering martyrdom under Antoninus Pius.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Victor and Sosthenes of Chalcedon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Victor and Sosthenes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Soldiers ordered to put Saint Euphemia to death who were converted on seeing her divine protection and suffered martyrdom for Christ.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Dorotheus the Hermit of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Dorotheus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Egyptian hermit who labored sixty years in the Skete desert and was remembered by Palladius as a severe ascetic and spiritual elder.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Ludmilla of Bohemia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Ludmilla, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian princess of Bohemia, baptized through the Slavic mission, who helped raise Saint Wenceslaus and was murdered amid political and religious conflict.
Notes: Pre-schism Western/Slavic saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyrs Isaac and Joseph of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyrs Isaac and Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Brothers born into a Muslim family but secretly raised Christian by their Georgian mother, who confessed Christ publicly and suffered martyrdom.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Hieromartyr Gregory of Tver
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest of Tver who suffered in the modern persecution of the Russian Church.
Notes: Among the New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia; OCA gives limited details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Procopius of Sazava
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Procopius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Bohemian priest who spread Christianity and founded the Sazava monastery dedicated to Saint John the Forerunner; reposed in 1053.
Notes: Pre-schism Western/Slavic saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Theodota at Nicea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Theodota, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wealthy Christian woman of Cappadocia denounced for confessing Christ, who suffered martyrdom at Nicaea under Alexander Severus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Agathocleia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Agathocleia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian servant abused for years by her pagan mistress because of her faith, who endured torture and was martyred.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: 156 Martyrs of Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Palestine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A large company of Christians, including the bishops Peleus and Nilus and the presbyter Zeno, who suffered under Maximian Galerius.
Notes: Named numerical group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Eumenius, Bishop of Gortyna
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Eumenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A virtuous man of Crete who gave away his wealth and became Bishop of Gortyna, remembered for charity, miracles, and pastoral holiness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Ariadne of Phrygia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Ariadne, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian slave of Phrygia who refused to join her master's pagan celebration and suffered as a martyr under Hadrian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Sophia, Irene, and Castor of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Sophia, Irene, and Castor, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three martyrs of Alexandria commemorated together by the Church, of whom few details survive.
Notes: Named group kept as one row; OCA gives minimal details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Greatmartyr Bidzini and Martyrs Elizabar and Shalvi of Georgia
Also Known As: Prince Bidzini; Prince Elizabar; Prince Shalvi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Greatmartyr Bidzini and Martyrs Elizabar and Shalvi, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Georgian nobles who resisted Persian oppression and were tortured and killed for refusing to abandon Christ.
Notes: Named group kept as one row; distinct from Shalva of Akhaltsikhe (OS-1377).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Euphrosyne of Suzdal
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Euphrosyne, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A princess who entered monastic life in Suzdal and became known for holiness, with miracles associated with her after her repose.
Notes: Sep 25 = her repose (principal); Sep 18 = uncovering of her relics.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Hilarion of Optina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hilarion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Optina Elders, known for spiritual counsel, humility, and monastic obedience.
Notes: Glorified among the Optina Elders.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Trophimus, Sabbatius, and Dorymedon of Synnada
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Trophimus, Sabbatius, and Dorymedon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three Christians who refused pagan worship under Emperor Probus and suffered martyrdom after torture.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theodore of Smolensk and his sons David and Constantine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prince Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prince of Smolensk and Yaroslavl who lived through the Mongol-era turmoil, venerated together with his sons David and Constantine.
Notes: Named family group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Zosimas the Hermit of Cilicia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Zosimas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A desert ascetic of Cilicia who, found by a governor while living among wild animals, confessed Christ and suffered martyrdom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theodore of Tarsus, Archbishop of Canterbury
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Greek monk from Tarsus who became Archbishop of Canterbury and organized the life of the English Church; one of the major pre-schism saints of England.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Prince Igor of Kiev and Chernigov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Passion-Bearer Igor, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prince caught in violent dynastic struggle who accepted monastic tonsure and was murdered during civil unrest, venerated as a passion-bearer.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Michael and Theodore of Chernigov
Also Known As: Michael of Chernigov; Theodore the Councilor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Michael and Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Prince Michael of Chernigov and his councilor Theodore refused to perform pagan rites before the Mongol khan and were killed for confessing Christ.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Oleg, Prince of Briansk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Prince Oleg, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Grandson of Saint Michael of Chernigov who left princely life to become a monk, remembered for piety and renunciation.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyr Hilarion of Saint Anne Skete
Also Known As: Hilarion of Mount Athos; John of Crete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Monastic Martyr Hilarion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Skete of Saint Anne on Mount Athos, born at Heraklion in Crete, who suffered martyrdom after confessing Christ under Ottoman rule.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Quadratus of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Quadratus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Seventy who preached at Athens and Magnesia, served as Bishop of Athens, and is remembered as an early Christian apologist and martyr.
Notes: Apostle of the Seventy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Daniel, Abbot of Shuzhgorsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Daniel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Komel tradition who withdrew to the Russian north and founded an ascetic community at Shuzhgorsk.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Joseph of Zaonikiev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A peasant who suffered from an eye disease and turned to prayer; after his healing he entered monastic life and was associated with the Zaonikiev Monastery.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Hypatius of Ephesus and Andrew the Presbyter
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyrs Hypatius and Andrew, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop and priest of Ephesus who defended the holy icons under Leo the Isaurian and were tortured and killed during the iconoclast persecution.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Isaac of Cyprus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Father Isaac, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A saint of Cyprus commemorated by the Church, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Meletius, Bishop of Cyprus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Meletius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Cyprus commemorated by the Church, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Eusebius of Phoenicia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Eusebius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr of Phoenicia commemorated by the Church, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Priscus of Phrygia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Priscus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr of Phrygia commemorated by the Church, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: 26 Monastic Martyrs of Zographou
Also Known As: Thomas; Barsanuphius; Cyril; the abbot and monks of Zographou and four laymen
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Zographou, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Monks and laymen of Zographou Monastery on Mount Athos who refused the Union of Lyons and were burned in the monastery tower in 1274.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Cosmas the Bulgarian of Zographou
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Cosmas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Bulgarian monk of Zographou Monastery who pursued hesychast asceticism and spiritual obedience.
Notes: Distinct from Cosmas of Zographou the anchorite commemorated Sep 22.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Phocas, Bishop of Sinope
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Phocas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Sinope who converted many to Christ and suffered martyrdom under Trajan's persecution.
Notes: Distinct from Phokas the Gardener of Sinope commemorated the same day.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Prophet Jonah
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Jonah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Old Testament prophet sent to Nineveh to preach repentance, whose three days in the great fish are read as a sign foreshadowing Christ's burial and Resurrection.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Jonah the Presbyter
Also Known As: Jonah, father of Theophanes the Hymnographer and Theodore Graptus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Jonah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Palestinian presbyter and father of Saints Theophanes the Hymnographer and Theodore the Branded, remembered for a holy family and priestly life.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Jonah, Abbot of Yashezersk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Jonah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Founder and abbot of a monastery near Yash Lake in the Russian north, where he gathered monks and established monastic worship in 1580.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Phokas the Gardener of Sinope
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Phokas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A poor gardener of Sinope known for hospitality and charity, who confessed Christ when soldiers came to kill him, prepared his own grave, and received martyrdom with courage.
Notes: Distinct from Bishop Phocas of Sinope on the same date.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Peter the Tax-Collector
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A harsh chief tax-collector under Justinian who repented after a vision and became merciful and generous to the poor.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Macarius, Wonderworker of Zhabynka
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wonderworker of Zhabynka and Belev commemorated by the Church, of whom little detailed life survives.
Notes: OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Cosmas of Zographou
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Cosmas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Bulgarian of noble birth who left worldly life for Mount Athos and lived as an anchorite of Zographou Monastery.
Notes: Distinct from Cosmas the Bulgarian of Zographou commemorated Sep 21.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Xanthippe and Polyxena
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Xanthippe and Polyxena, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Sisters in Spain among the first to hear the apostolic preaching of Saint Paul and become disciples of Christ.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Andrew, John, Peter, and Antoninus of Syracuse
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Andrew, John, Peter, and Antoninus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Christians captured after the fall of Syracuse and taken to Africa, where John and his children Peter and Antoninus, with Andrew, suffered martyrdom.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Peter the Aleut
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Orthodox Aleut youth captured by Spanish authorities in California and killed after refusing to abandon Orthodoxy; honored as the Protomartyr of America.
Notes: Among the Synaxis of All Saints of Alaska.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Juvenal of Alaska
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Juvenal, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Valaam missionary priest-monk sent to Alaska in 1794 who was killed while preaching; honored as a protomartyr of America.
Notes: Among the Synaxis of All Saints of Alaska.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Nikander of Pskov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nikander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A peasant-born ascetic who longed for solitary prayer from childhood and lived in the wilderness near Pskov, revered as a wonderworker.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyr Galacteon of Vologda
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Monastic Martyr Galacteon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prince's son hidden from Ivan the Terrible who became a monk and suffered martyrdom during the Time of Troubles.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Copres of Palestine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Copres, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Found as an abandoned infant by the monks of Saint Theodosius' monastery, he was raised in monastic life and became known for holiness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Abramius, Abbot of Mirozh
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Abramius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Builder and first abbot of the Savior-Transfiguration Monastery of Mirozh in Pskov, founded around 1156.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Vladislav of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy King Vladislav, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian king known for virtue and charity who built the Mileševa Monastery, where he was buried; reposed in 1239.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Dorothy of Kashin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Dorothy, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A noblewoman who became a nun amid civil unrest, invasion, and plague, remembered for her monastic endurance.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Simon of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Simon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Serbia commemorated by the Church, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable David of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father David, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A nephew of Saint Stephen who founded a monastery at Brodarova and became a monk named David.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Stephen the First-Crowned of Serbia
Also Known As: Stephen the First-Crowned; Simeon in monasticism
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy King Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The first crowned king of Serbia and son of Saint Stephen Nemanja, who accepted monastic tonsure as Simeon before his death in 1224.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Euphrosyne of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Euphrosyne, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wealthy Alexandrian woman who fled an arranged marriage and entered a men's monastery in disguise as the monk Smaragdus to pursue the ascetic life.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Paphnutius and 546 Companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Paphnutius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Egyptian desert ascetic who confessed Christ under Diocletian and was martyred with hundreds of companions.
Notes: Named numerical group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Arsenius the Great, Catholicos of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Arsenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian hierarch and disciple of the Khandzta monastic tradition who served as Catholicos and strengthened the life of the Georgian Church.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Ephraim, Abbot of Perekop
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Ephraim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian monk drawn to solitude from youth who founded the Perekop monastery near Novgorod, remembered as a wonderworker and monastic father.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Callistratus and 49 Companions
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Callistratus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian soldier whose secret prayer revealed his faith during Diocletian's persecution; forty-nine fellow soldiers were converted and martyred with him.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Sabbatius of Solovki
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Sabbatius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk who helped begin the Solovki monastic life with Saint German, remembered for humility, obedience, and wilderness asceticism.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Mark of the Seventy
Also Known As: John Mark; Mark, Bishop of Byblos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Mark, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A co-worker of Paul and Barnabas, also called John Mark, counted among the Seventy and remembered as Bishop of Byblos.
Notes: Apostle of the Seventy; the OCA cycle distinguishes him from Mark the Evangelist (OS-1061). Oct 30 = his commemoration as Bishop of Apollonia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Zenas of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Zenas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned Christian worker mentioned by Saint Paul in the Epistle to Titus, counted among the Seventy.
Notes: Apostle of the Seventy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Epicharis of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Epicharis, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian woman of Rome arrested under Diocletian who confessed Christ and endured torture before her martyrdom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Ignatius of Christ the Savior Monastery
Also Known As: Ignatius of Batheos Ryakos; Ignatius of Deep River
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Ignatius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the Monastery of Christ the Savior, also called Deep River, dedicated to monastic life from youth and known for ascetic discipline.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Aquilina of Thessalonica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Aquilina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Christian girl near Thessalonica pressured to accept Islam after her father's conflict with a neighbor, who confessed Christ and died from torture in 1764.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Chariton of Syanzhemsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Chariton, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint Euthymius of Syanzhemsk who shared in founding and sustaining monastic life by the Syanzhema River.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Herodion, Abbot of Iloezersk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Herodion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint Cornelius of Komel who settled at Elias Lake and founded a monastic community there.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Prophet Baruch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Baruch, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The disciple and secretary of the Prophet Jeremiah, who wrote down his prophecies and remained faithful during the national catastrophe of Judah.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Alexander and 30 others in Pisidia and Phrygia
Also Known As: Alexander; Alpheius; Zosimus; Mark the Shepherd; Nikon; Neon; Heliodorus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Alexander and those with him, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Christians who suffered in various places of Asia Minor under Diocletian, including Mark the shepherd and other named martyrs.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Kyriakos the Anchorite
