Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Kyriakos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Corinthian reader who became a monk in Palestine and lived as a desert anchorite, known for long life, ascetic struggle, and spiritual wisdom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Dadas, Gabdelas, and Kazdoa of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Dadas, Gabdelas, and Kazdoa, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Dadas, a high official, and Gabdelas and Kazdoa, children of the Persian king, confessed Christ and suffered martyrdom under Shapur.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Theophanes the Merciful of Gaza
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Theophanes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wealthy man of Gaza who spent his resources helping the poor, sick, and homeless, and accepted poverty and suffering with patience.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Onuphrius of David Gareji
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Onuphrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian nobleman who secretly practiced asceticism before becoming a monk at David Gareji, known for charity, prayer, and withdrawal from worldly honors.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Cyprian of Ustiug
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Cyprian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wealthy landowner who became a schema-monk and helped establish monastic life near Ustiug at the request of the local people.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Gregory of Pelsheme
Also Known As: Gregory, Abbot of Pelsheme; Wonderworker of Vologda
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A noble-born Russian monk who distributed his inheritance and founded a monastery at Pelsheme, remembered for ascetic life and wonderworking.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Rhipsime, Gaiane, and those with them
Also Known As: Hripsime; Gaiane; the Armenian women martyrs
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Rhipsime and Gaiane, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Christian women who fled imperial pressure at Rome and suffered martyrdom in Armenia; their witness is bound up with the conversion-era history of Armenia.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Ananias of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Ananias, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The first Bishop of Damascus, sent by Christ to restore the sight of Saul and baptize him, who preached openly and suffered martyrdom.
Notes: Apostle of the Seventy; distinct from other saints named Ananias.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Romanus the Melodist
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Romanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Syrian-born deacon and sacristan in Constantinople who became one of the greatest Orthodox hymnographers, especially of the kontakia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Savva, Abbot of Vishera
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Savva, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A noble-born monk who founded a monastery near the Vishera River by Novgorod, known for ascetic life and solitude.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Domninus of Thessalonica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Domninus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Thessalonica arrested under Maximian Galerius for preaching Christ, who endured torture and martyrdom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Michael, Abbot in Armenia, and 36 Fathers
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Michael and those with him, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot Michael and thirty-six monks of the Zobe Monastery who suffered martyrdom during an Arab raid after refusing to deny Christ.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable John Koukouzelis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A gifted singer trained at the imperial court who left worldly honor for monastic life on Mount Athos and became one of the most beloved Byzantine church musicians.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Gregory Domesticus of the Great Lavra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A leading chanter of the Great Lavra and contemporary of Saint John Koukouzelis, remembered for piety, ascetic life, and liturgical service.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Equals-to-the-Apostles Mirian and Nana of Georgia, with Sidonia
Also Known As: King Mirian III; Queen Nana; Sidonia of Mtskheta
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Equals-to-the-Apostles Mirian and Nana, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: King Mirian and Queen Nana accepted Christianity through Saint Nino's mission and helped establish Christian Georgia; Sidonia is connected with the tradition of the Robe of Christ at Mtskheta.
Notes: Named Georgian commemoration kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Melchizedek I, Catholicos-Patriarch of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Melchizedek, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Catholicos-Patriarch of Georgia in the early eleventh century who strengthened the Georgian Church during the reigns of Bagrat III, George I, and Bagrat IV.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Joseph of Bisericani
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Romanian hermit and founder of Bisericani Monastery who helped form monastic life in the Neamț region.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Andrew the Fool-for-Christ
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Andrew, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Slav living in Constantinople who embraced holy foolishness after a vision, connected with the vision of the Protection of the Theotokos.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Princess Anna of Kashin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Princess Anna, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A princess of Tver who endured the deaths of loved ones and later entered monastic life, venerated for patience, humility, and endurance.
Notes: Repose commemoration.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs David and Constantine, Princes of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs David and Constantine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Georgian princely brothers who resisted Arab invasion and refused to abandon Christ, suffering torture and martyrdom.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Admiral Theodore Ushakov
Also Known As: Theodore Ushakov of the Russian Fleet
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian naval commander remembered for courage, discipline, mercy, and Christian piety, who later lived quietly and gave generously to the poor.
Notes: Distinct from his uncle Saint Theodore of Sanaxar (OS-0693).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Dionysius the Areopagite
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Dionysius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Athenian member of the Areopagus converted by Saint Paul's preaching, remembered as Bishop of Athens and a martyr.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Rusticus the Presbyter and Eleutherius the Deacon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Rusticus and Eleutherius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Disciples of Saint Dionysius the Areopagite who suffered martyrdom at Athens during the persecution of Domitian.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Dionysius the Recluse of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Dionysius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A recluse of the Kiev Far Caves remembered in the Kiev Caves Paterikon, with a Paschal miracle associated with his censing of the relics of the saints.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John the Chozebite, Bishop of Caesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Egyptian monk who became Bishop of Caesarea in Palestine, known for opposing heresy, spiritual discernment, and wonderworking.
Notes: Also commemorated Oct 28.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Hesychius of Mount Horeb
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hesychius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Mount Horeb who, transformed after a near-death illness, lived the rest of his life in repentance and silence.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Damaris of Athens
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Damaris, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The first Athenian woman recorded as believing in Christ through Saint Paul's preaching, mentioned in Acts 17.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Hierotheus, Bishop of Athens
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Hierotheus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A member of the Areopagus converted by Saint Paul and consecrated as Bishop of Athens, connected in tradition with the funeral of the Theotokos.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Prince Vladimir Yaroslavich of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prince Vladimir, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Son of Yaroslav the Wise and prince of Novgorod, who governed the city and built its Cathedral of Saint Sophia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Helladius of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Helladius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Near Caves commemorated by the Church, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Onesimus of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Onesimus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Near Caves commemorated by the Church, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Ammon the Recluse of the Kiev Caves
Also Known As: Ammon of the Kiev Far Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Ammon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Kiev Caves monk and lover of labor who made pilgrimages to Athos and Jerusalem before returning to the ascetic life.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Gaius, Faustus, Eusebius, and Chaeremon of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Gaius and those with him, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Deacons and disciples of Saint Dionysius of Alexandria who were exiled, tortured, and beheaded under Valerian.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Peter of Capitolias
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A married man and father who became a monk and priest after his wife's death, openly confessed Christ, and was martyred under Muslim rule.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Domnina and her daughters of Syria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Domnina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian mother and her two daughters who fled their pagan relatives and chose death rather than violation and apostasy.
Notes: Named family group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Ammon of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Ammon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A married man who, by agreement with his wife, lived in continence and later became a desert monk and a founder of monastic life in Nitria.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Callisthene and her father Audactus of Ephesus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Callisthene and Audactus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Callisthene and her father Audactus, an eparch, suffered after refusing a pagan imperial marriage alliance; Callisthene later confessed Christ and was martyred.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Stephen Stiljanovic of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian ruler and defender of his people during the Ottoman invasions, remembered for piety, charity, and protection of the suffering.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Evdemoz, Catholicos of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Evdemoz, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Catholicos of Georgia under Persian domination who resisted the apostate ruler Rostom-Khan and was imprisoned and martyred for defending Orthodox Georgia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Elena of Serbia
Also Known As: Helen of Serbia; wife of Stephen Stiljanovic
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Elena, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Wife of Saint Stephen Stiljanovic who fled after his death and is connected with the later discovery of his relics, honored for piety and faithfulness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints Jonah and Nectarius of Kazan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Jonah and Nectarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two brothers who entered monastic life under Saint Gurias of Kazan, remembered among the early monastic saints of Kazan.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Damian the Healer, Jeremiah, and Matthew of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Damian, Jeremiah, and Matthew, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Kiev Caves monks remembered by Saint Nestor; Damian was known as a healer, while Jeremiah and Matthew were remembered for spiritual insight.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Charitina, Princess of Lithuania
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Charitina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Lithuanian princess who became a nun in Novgorod and later abbess, remembered for monastic virtue and leadership.
Notes: Distinct from Martyr Charitina of Amisos (OS-0200) on the same date.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Dionysius the Great of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Dionysius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A convert and pupil of Origen who led the Alexandrian catechetical school and became Bishop of Alexandria, enduring persecution and guiding the Church through crisis.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Memelchtha of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Memelchtha, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former pagan priestess converted to Christianity by her sister, who was stoned after appearing in her baptismal robe and martyred in 344.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Gregory of Khandzta
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A great builder of Georgian monasticism, raised in the Kartlian court, who founded monastic communities in the Klarjeti wilderness during the Arab period.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Eudokimos of Vatopedi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Eudokimos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Vatopedi Monastery on Mount Athos whose holiness was revealed when his fragrant relics were discovered in 1840.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Methodia of Kimolos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Methodia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A nun of the island of Kimolos, born in 1865, revered for her ascetic life and spiritual gifts.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Thomas
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Thomas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Twelve Apostles, remembered for confessing the risen Christ as Lord and God; tradition says he preached as far as India and was martyred there.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyr Macarius of Saint Anne Skete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Monastic Martyr Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monastic martyr of the Skete of Saint Anne on Mount Athos, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Sergius and Bacchus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Sergius and Bacchus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: High-ranking soldiers under Maximian who were exposed as Christians, refused pagan worship, and suffered torture and martyrdom.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Sergius, Abbot of Nurma
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Sergius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Greek Athonite monk who became a disciple of Saint Sergius of Radonezh and founded the Nurma monastery in Vologda.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Julian the Presbyter and Caesarius the Deacon of Terracina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Julian and Caesarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A presbyter and deacon martyred at Terracina in Italy; Caesarius was imprisoned for rebuking the worship of pagan gods.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saints; named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Polychronius the Presbyter
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Polychronius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest raised in Christian piety who worked as a vineyard laborer and suffered martyrdom for Christ.
Notes: Distinct from Hieromartyr Polychronius, Bishop of Babylon (OS-1610, Jul 30).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: 99 Holy Fathers of Crete
Also Known As: John the Wonderworker and the Fathers of Crete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers of Crete, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A community of ninety-nine ascetic fathers of Crete, whose leader John was a renowned wonderworker.
Notes: Named numerical group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Joseph the Elder of Khevi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A native priest of Khevi in northern Georgia, revered as an elder and wonderworker.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Pelagia the Penitent
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Pelagia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A famous performer of Antioch who repented after hearing Bishop Nonnus, was baptized, and lived in asceticism near Jerusalem disguised as a monk.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Dositheus, Abbot of Verkneostrov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Dositheus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint Euphrosynus of Pskov who founded the Peter and Paul Monastery on Lake Pskov.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Tryphon, Archimandrite of Vyatka
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Tryphon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of peasant birth who founded and guided monastic life in Vyatka, known for discipline, prayer, and missionary zeal.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyr Pelagia of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Virgin Martyr Pelagia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Christian noblewoman of Antioch who chose death rather than violation by the soldiers sent to arrest her, honored as a virgin martyr.
Notes: Distinct from Pelagia the Penitent on the same date.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyr Ignatius of Bulgaria
Also Known As: Ignatius the Monk; John of Eski Zagora
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Ignatius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Bulgarian who became a monk on Mount Athos and later confessed Christ publicly under Ottoman rule, suffering martyrdom at Constantinople.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle James, son of Alphaeus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle James, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Twelve Apostles and brother of the Evangelist Matthew, who preached Christ after Pentecost and suffered for the Gospel.
Notes: Not James son of Zebedee or James the Brother of the Lord.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Andronicus and his wife Athanasia of Egypt
Life Experience: Loss of a Child; Grief / Bereavement
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Andronicus and Athanasia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A married couple who gave to the poor and entered monastic life after the deaths of their children; Athanasia lived disguised as a male monk, and they were reunited only near death.
Notes: Named married pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Forefather Abraham
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Forefather Abraham, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The biblical patriarch called by God to leave his homeland and become the father of many nations, remembered for faith and obedience.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Lot
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Lot, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Nephew of Abraham who lived in Sodom and was rescued before its destruction, remembered as righteous amid a corrupt city.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Publia the Confessor, Deaconess of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessor Publia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A widowed deaconess who led a women's ascetic community and openly resisted Julian's pagan revival, and was beaten for singing psalms against idolatry.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Peter the former soldier
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former soldier under the iconoclast Emperor Theophilus who left military life for monastic asceticism, remembered for repentance and perseverance.
Notes: Distinct from Peter the Hermit of Galatia (OS-0575).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Dionysius of Paris
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Dionysius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A missionary bishop sent to preach in Gaul, beheaded at Paris with his companions, and venerated as a patron of France.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; not Dionysius the Areopagite.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Stephen the Blind of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A fifteenth-century Serbian ruler who was blinded and endured exile, remembered for patience and faithfulness to Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Eulampius and Eulampia and 200 Martyrs
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Eulampius and Eulampia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A brother and sister of Nicomedia who confessed Christ under Maximian and endured torture; many witnesses were converted and martyred with them.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Amphilochius, Bishop of Vladimir in Volhynia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Amphilochius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An early bishop of Vladimir-Volhynia in one of the oldest dioceses of Rus', remembered among the saints of Volhynia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Andrew of Totma the Fool-for-Christ
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Andrew, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian holy fool who left the world as a young man and lived in voluntary poverty, known for spiritual endurance and hidden holiness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Theotecnus of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Theotecnus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A military commander of Antioch under Maximian who refused pagan sacrifice and suffered martyrdom for Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Bassian of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Bassian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An ascetic of Syrian origin who became abbot of a monastery in Constantinople under Emperor Marcian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theophilus the Confessor of Bulgaria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theophilus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monastic confessor who suffered for the veneration of the holy icons during the iconoclast persecution.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Hieromartyr Peter (Polyansky), Metropolitan of Krutitsy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Metropolitan of Krutitsy and locum tenens of the patriarchal throne who suffered imprisonment and martyrdom in the Soviet persecution.
Notes: Among the New Martyrs and Confessors of Russia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Philotheus, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Philotheus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Sinai and Athos who became Patriarch of Constantinople, supported hesychasm, and wrote lives and liturgical texts.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Philip of the Seventy
Also Known As: Philip the Deacon; Philip the Evangelist
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Philip, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Seven Deacons and a member of the Seventy, who preached in Samaria and baptized the Ethiopian eunuch.
Notes: Apostle of the Seventy; not Philip of the Twelve.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theophanes the Confessor and Hymnographer, Bishop of Nicaea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theophanes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Brother of Theodore the Branded, educated in the Lavra of Saint Sabbas, who suffered for the holy icons and later became Bishop of Nicaea and a hymnographer.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Theophanes the Faster of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theophanes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Near Caves known for strict fasting, whose relics repose in the Near Caves of Saint Anthony.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyrs Zenaida and Philonilla of Tarsus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Zenaida and Philonilla, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Women of Tarsus related to the Apostle Paul, learned in medicine, who treated the sick freely and preached Christ.
Notes: Distinct from Martyr Zenaida of Caesarea (OS-1310).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Ethelburga of Barking
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Ethelburga, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A seventh-century East Anglian royal who became the first abbess of the monastery of Barking in Essex.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint; distinct from Aethelburh of Kent (OS-0309).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Leonid of Optina
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Leonid, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The first of the great Optina Elders, who established the tradition of eldership and spiritual guidance at the Optina hermitage.
Notes: Glorified among the Optina Elders.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Probus, Tarachus, and Andronicus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Probus, Tarachus, and Andronicus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three Christians martyred at Tarsus in 304, who endured repeated interrogation and torture for refusing pagan sacrifice.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Cosmas the Hymnographer, Bishop of Maiuma
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Cosmas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Raised with Saint John of Damascus, he became a monk and later Bishop of Maiuma, writing many hymns and canons for Orthodox worship.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Amphilochius, Abbot of Glushetsa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Amphilochius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest-monk who joined Saint Dionysius of Glushetsa as his close disciple and later guided the Glushetsa monastic community.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Domnina of Anazarbus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Domnina, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian woman of Cilicia who confessed Christ and endured beatings, burning, and imprisonment, dying in prison in 286.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Martin the Merciful, Bishop of Tours
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Martin, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former Roman soldier who became a monk and Bishop of Tours, remembered for mercy, pastoral courage, and care for the poor.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint. Also commemorated Nov 11.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Edwin, King and Martyr of Northumbria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy King Edwin, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: King of Northumbria who accepted Christianity and supported its spread, dying in battle and venerated as a martyr.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Hieromartyr John, Archbishop of Riga
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Riga and Latvia who guided the Orthodox Church amid hardship and suffered a martyr's death.
Notes: Among the New Martyrs of the twentieth century.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Carpus, Papylus, Agathodorus, and Agathonica at Pergamum
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Carpus and those with him, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Christians of Pergamum who refused the pagan festivals and suffered under Decius; Agathonica joined their confession and martyrdom.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Benjamin of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Benjamin, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A wealthy merchant who renounced his possessions after hearing the Gospel warning about riches and became a monk of the Kiev Caves.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Florentius of Thessalonica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Florentius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Thessalonica who fearlessly unmasked idolatry, brought others to faith, and was tortured and burned for Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Benjamin the Deacon of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Benjamin, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Persian deacon who converted many pagans to Christianity and suffered for his preaching during the Persian persecution.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Niketas the Confessor of Paphlagonia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessor Niketas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An imperial court patrician of Paphlagonia who represented Empress Irene at the Seventh Ecumenical Council and later confessed the veneration of the icons.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Zlata of Meglena
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Zlata, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A virgin of the village of Slatena in the Meglena region who refused to accept Islam or marriage and was tortured to death for Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints Anthony and Jacob of Chqondidi
Also Known As: Anthony, Metropolitan of Chqondidi; Jacob the Elder
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Fathers Anthony and Jacob, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Georgian saints of Chqondidi; Anthony, of princely Georgian descent, served as metropolitan, while Jacob was revered as an elder.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Gervasius, Nazarius, Protasius, and Celsus of Milan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Gervasius, Nazarius, Protasius, and Celsus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Nazarius preached Christ in Gaul and Milan and suffered with the young Celsus; the twin brothers Gervasius and Protasius of Milan are commemorated with them.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saints; named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Paraskevi of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Paraskevi, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A pious woman of Epivates who left wealth behind for ascetic and monastic life, whose relics became widely venerated across the Balkans.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Nicholas Sviatosha, Prince of Chernigov
Also Known As: Nicholas, Wonderworker of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nicholas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prince of Chernigov, great-grandson of Yaroslav the Wise, who renounced worldly rank and became a humble monk of the Kiev Caves, known for obedience and service.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Euthymius the New of Thessalonica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Euthymius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Galatian who left family life for monastic asceticism and became a major Athonite and Thessalonian monastic figure.
Notes: Possible overlap with the row 'Euthymius the New and those with him' (OS-1165) — verify.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Lucian the Presbyter of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Lucian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An orphan who gave away his possessions and became a presbyter and biblical scholar at Antioch, suffering martyrdom during persecution.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John, Bishop of Suzdal
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk who became the first Bishop of Suzdal and Nizhny Novgorod, remembered for humility, pastoral holiness, and miracles.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Lucian of the Kiev Caves
Also Known As: Lucian the Presbyter of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Lucian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A presbyter of the Kiev Caves who suffered martyrdom during Batu's invasion around 1243.
Notes: Few details of his life are preserved.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Sabinus, Bishop of Catania
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Sabinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Catania in Sicily who withdrew to the wilderness for ascetic life and was remembered for wonderworking and discernment.
Notes: OCA gives limited details; pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Longinus the Centurion
Also Known As: Longinus who stood at the Cross
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Longinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Roman centurion present at the Crucifixion who confessed Christ as the Son of God, later preached the faith, and suffered martyrdom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Longinus the Gatekeeper of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Longinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Far Caves who served in humble obedience as gatekeeper and was remembered for discernment and prayerful care for pilgrims.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Longinus of Yarenga
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Longinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the northern Russian monastic tradition honored as a wonderworker of Yarenga.
Notes: Also commemorated Jul 3.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Longinus of Asistavi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Longinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A martyr of Asistavi in Georgia commemorated by the Church, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Eupraxia of Pskov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Eupraxia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A princess of Pskov who entered monastic life after her husband abandoned her and was later murdered in Livonia, venerated for holiness and suffering.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Domna of Tomsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Domna, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A noble-born woman who lived as a fool-for-Christ in Siberia, embracing poverty, prayer, and humility while serving the poor.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Prophet Hosea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Hosea, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The first of the Twelve Minor Prophets, who called Israel back from idolatry and revealed God's covenant love through the imagery of marriage.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyr Andrew of Crete
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Monastic Martyr Andrew, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk who defended the holy icons under Constantine Kopronymos and was tortured and killed for resisting iconoclasm.
Notes: Not Andrew of Crete the author of the Great Canon (OS-1468).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Anthony, Abbot of Leokhnov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Anthony, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian monk and hermit of noble Tver lineage who founded monastic life near Lake Ilmen and guided the Leokhnov monastery.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Joseph, Catholicos of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Joseph, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian hierarch educated at David-Gareji and later Catholicos of Georgia, remembered for wisdom, pastoral care, and healing prayers.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Kozman of Kartli
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Kozman, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian martyr of Kartli commemorated by the Church, of whom little detail is preserved.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives little detail.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Marinus the Elder
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Marinus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian elder of Anazarbus in Cilicia who was beaten and killed under Diocletian for confessing Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Julian the Hermit of Mesopotamia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Julian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit who lived near the Euphrates in fasting and prayer, who is said to have foretold the death of Julian the Apostate.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint James the Deacon of York
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Father James, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon who assisted Saint Paulinus in evangelizing northern England and continued teaching the faith through plague, famine, and upheaval.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Peter of Cetinje
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Peter, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop and ruler of Montenegro who guided his people through war, poverty, and turmoil, remembered for pastoral care and defense of Orthodox Montenegro.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable David of Serpukhov
Also Known As: David, Abbot of the Ascension Hermitage
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father David, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint Paphnutius of Borovsk who founded the Ascension Davidov Hermitage near Serpukhov.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Prophet Joel
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Joel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Twelve Minor Prophets, who foretold desolation and the outpouring of the Holy Spirit, words quoted at Pentecost in the New Testament.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Varus and Seven Monastic Martyrs
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Varus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A secret Christian soldier who strengthened imprisoned monks and joined them in martyrdom, suffering with seven monastic martyrs during persecution.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Cleopatra and her son John of Egypt
Life Experience: Loss of a Child; Grief / Bereavement
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Cleopatra and John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Cleopatra honored the martyr Varus and lived piously with her son John; after John's early death she endured grief and was consoled through prayer.
Notes: Named mother-son commemoration kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Greatmartyr Artemius of Antioch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Greatmartyr Artemius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A military commander and governor of Egypt who confessed Christ before Julian the Apostate and was tortured and executed for rebuking paganism and defending Christians.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Matrona of Chios
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Matrona, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A woman of a wealthy family of Chios who sought monastic life and founded a women's monastery, known for charity and asceticism.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Nikodemos of Belgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Nikodemos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop of Belgorod honored as a hieromartyr of the modern persecution, whose relics were uncovered and venerated with the Belgorod saints.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Hilarion the Great
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hilarion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint Anthony who became a founder of Palestinian desert monasticism, living in strict asceticism and spiritual combat.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Hilarion, Bishop of Meglin
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Hilarion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Bulgarian bishop and ascetic who defended Orthodoxy and cared for his flock, whose relics were translated to Trnovo.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Hilarion the Schemamonk of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Hilarion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A strict ascetic of the Kiev Caves and disciple of Saint Theodosius, known for tears, fasting, and the copying of books.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Theophilus and Jacob, Abbots of Omutch
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Theophilus and Jacob, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two monastic founders who established a wilderness monastery at Omutch after ascetic life near Konev.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Gaius, Dasius, and Zoticus of Nicomedia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Gaius, Dasius, and Zoticus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three Christians martyred under Diocletian after destroying a pagan temple, who endured torture and were drowned.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Philotheus of Dionysiou
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Philotheus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Dionysiou Monastery on Athos who survived childhood captivity and later embraced monastic asceticism.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Bessarion Sarai the Confessor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessor Bessarion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian monk who defended Orthodox Christians in Transylvania against forced union with Rome and endured imprisonment and persecution.
Notes: Among the Confessors of Transylvania.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Sophronius of Ciorara
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessor Sophronius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Romanian monk who organized Orthodox resistance to forced union and defended the village Orthodox communities of Transylvania.
Notes: Among the Confessors of Transylvania.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Oprea of Salistie
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Oprea, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Orthodox layman of Transylvania who suffered for defending the faith against forced religious union.
Notes: Among the Confessors of Transylvania.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Hilarion, Metropolitan of Kiev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Hilarion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The first native Rus' Metropolitan of Kiev, known for theological writing and pastoral leadership during the reign of Yaroslav the Wise.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Moses Macinic
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Moses, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Romanian Orthodox priest who opposed forced union and endured imprisonment for his ministry in Transylvania.
Notes: Among the Confessors of Transylvania.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John of Galesh
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessor John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Romanian Orthodox bishop who resisted Habsburg pressure against Orthodoxy and was imprisoned for refusing to abandon his flock.
Notes: Among the Confessors of Transylvania.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr John of Monemvasia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Christian of the Peloponnese who refused to renounce Christ under Ottoman pressure and suffered martyrdom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Averkios the Wonderworker, Bishop of Hieropolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Averkios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop of Hieropolis who preached Christ in a largely pagan city and converted many, remembered as a wonderworker and missionary bishop.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Alexander of Adrianopolis and those with him
Also Known As: Alexander; Heraclius; Anna; Elizabeth; Theodota; Glyceria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Bishop Alexander preached the Gospel and was martyred with several companions who confessed Christ.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints Paul and Theodore of Rostov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Paul and Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two ascetics who founded a monastery near Rostov dedicated to Saints Boris and Gleb.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Lot of Egypt
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Lot, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A desert ascetic of Egypt whose sayings are preserved among the Fathers, remembered for wisdom and monastic discernment.
Notes: Not the Righteous Forefather Lot (OS-1985).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Macarius the Roman of Mesopotamia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Macarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Roman-born ascetic discovered by three wandering monks living in a cave in Mesopotamia, whose story emphasizes hidden holiness and the desert life.
Notes: Traditional hagiographic account; distinct from Macarius the Roman of Novgorod (OS-0490).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle James the Brother of the Lord
Also Known As: James Adelphotheos; first Bishop of Jerusalem
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle James, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A kinsman of the Lord and the first Bishop of Jerusalem, who led the early Church there and suffered martyrdom.
Notes: Not James son of Zebedee or James son of Alphaeus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Ignatius, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Ignatius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A son of Emperor Michael I forced into monastic life who later became Patriarch of Constantinople, suffering deposition, exile, and imprisonment during church conflict.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Elisha of Lavreshev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Elisha, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Founder of a monastery near Novogrudok dedicated to Saint Laurence, remembered in the monastic tradition of the western Rus' lands.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Arethas and 4,299 Martyrs with him
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Arethas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Arethas, prefect of the Christian city of Najran in Arabia, suffered with thousands of Christians under the Himyarite ruler Dhu Nuwas, refusing to deny Christ.
Notes: Named numerical group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Arethas the Recluse of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Arethas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Kiev Caves hermit who repented after grief over stolen possessions revealed his attachment to wealth, remembered for repentance and ascetic life.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Sisoes the Recluse of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Sisoes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit of the Kiev Near Caves known in the general service for his radiant fasting.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Theophilus the Recluse of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theophilus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Kiev Near Caves hermit praised in the service to the Fathers of the Caves as resplendent in miracles.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Elesbaan, King of Ethiopia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy King Elesbaan, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian king of Ethiopia who fought to defend persecuted Christians in Arabia and later embraced repentance and humility.
Notes: OCA labels him blessed; commemorated in the Eastern Orthodox calendar.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Syncletica and her two daughters
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Syncletica, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A widowed Christian woman and her daughters who suffered under Dhu Nuwas during the persecution of Christians in Arabia.
Notes: Named family group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Athanasius I, Patriarch of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Athanasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk who became Patriarch of Constantinople twice, known for reform, asceticism, and pastoral discipline.
Notes: Also listed Oct 28 in the OCA cycle.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John, Hermit of Pskov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A hermit who lived through the Time of Troubles and the sieges around Pskov, remembered for ascetic endurance amid war.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Marcian and Martyrius the Notaries of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Marcian and Martyrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Church notaries serving Patriarch Paul the Confessor who resisted Arian pressure after his exile and were martyred for remaining Orthodox.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Martyrius the Deacon of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Martyrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon and recluse of the Kiev Far Caves praised for purity, labor, and healing gifts.
Notes: Also commemorated Aug 28.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Tabitha the Widow
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Tabitha, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A charitable Christian widow of Joppa, known for good works and care for the poor, who was raised from the dead by the Apostle Peter.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theophilus of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Theophilus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk who became Archbishop of Novgorod and later retired to the Kiev Caves, enduring political turmoil and illness near the end of his life.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Athanasius of Medikion
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Athanasius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Medikion Monastery and companion of Saint Nicetas who secretly sought monastic life and endured ascetic struggle.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Demetrius of Tsilibinsk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Demetrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint Stephen of Perm who founded the Archangel Tsilibinsk wilderness monastery and served the newly converted people.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Olga of Kwethluk
Also Known As: Tanqilria Arrsamquq; Matushka Olga of Alaska
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Mother Olga, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Yup'ik Orthodox matushka, midwife, and mother known for prayer, compassion, and care for women and families, glorified by the Orthodox Church in America.
Notes: Recently glorified OCA saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Nestor of Thessalonica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Nestor, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young disciple of Saint Demetrius who, with his blessing, defeated the gladiator Lyaeus and was executed for Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Nestor the Chronicler of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nestor, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk and hierodeacon of the Kiev Caves, traditionally associated with the early Rus' chronicle and the writing of saints' lives.
Notes: Distinct from Nestor of the Far Caves.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Right-believing Prince Andrew of Smolensk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prince Andrew, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prince who left worldly status and lived humbly as a church servant near Pereslavl, venerated after the uncovering of his relics.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Capitolina and Eroteis of Cappadocia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Capitolina and Eroteis, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Capitolina, a noble Christian woman, and Eroteis, her servant, confessed Christ under Diocletian and suffered martyrdom.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Claudia Procula
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Claudia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Identified by tradition as Pilate's wife, who warned him after a dream concerning Christ and is remembered among those who recognized His innocence.
Notes: Based on tradition; OCA includes her.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Mark, Soterichus, and Valentine
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Mark, Soterichus, and Valentine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Christians arrested in 304 and tortured for confessing Christ, whose relics were later brought to Thasos.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Demetrius of Basarabov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Demetrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Bulgarian ascetic who lived near Ruschuk and became widely venerated in Bucharest after the translation of his relics.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Nestor of the Far Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nestor, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Kiev Caves ascetic of the Far Caves, commemorated on the same day as his namesakes.
Notes: Explicitly not Nestor the Chronicler.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Alexander, Bishop of Guria and Samegrelo
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian hierarch who served Guria and Samegrelo and labored for education, pastoral care, and church life.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Stephen the Hymnographer
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Lavra of Saint Savva who composed liturgical hymns for the Triodion season.
Notes: Not Stephen the Wonderworker of Saint Savva's Monastery.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Arsenius, Archbishop of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Arsenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint Savva and Archbishop of Serbia who guided the Serbian Church and moved its seat to Peć amid invasion threats.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Greatmartyr Paraskevi of Iconium
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Greatmartyr Paraskevi, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian virgin of a wealthy family of Iconium who preached Christ, refused pagan worship, and suffered martyrdom during persecution.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Job, Abbot of Pochaev
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Job, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Ukrainian monastic elder and abbot of Pochaev who defended Orthodoxy and guided the monastery for many years.
Notes: Principal repose feast; also Aug 28 (recovery of relics).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Demetrius, Metropolitan of Rostov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Demetrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A bishop, preacher, and spiritual writer best known for compiling the lives of the saints, who served as Metropolitan of Rostov.
Notes: Principal repose feast; also Sep 21 (uncovering of relics).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Terence and Neonila and their children
Also Known As: Terence; Neonila; Sabelus; Photius; Theodoulus; Vele; Hierax; Nitus; Eunice
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Terence and Neonila, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian husband, wife, and their seven children who were denounced, tortured, and martyred together for confessing Christ.
Notes: Named family group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Cyriacus, Patriarch of Jerusalem
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Cyriacus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Jewish man who, by tradition, helped Saint Helen find the Cross, then became Christian and Patriarch of Jerusalem, suffering martyrdom under Julian.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Neophytus of Urbnisi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Neophytus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian hierarch descended from Persian fire-worshippers who was martyred during the Saracen attacks on Georgia.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Arsenius of Cappadocia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Arsenius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Cappadocian priest and spiritual father who served the Greek Orthodox of Farasa and baptized Saint Paisios the Athonite.
Notes: Modern Greek Orthodox saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyr Anastasia of Rome
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Monastic Martyr Anastasia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Orphaned as a child and raised in a women's monastery, she confessed Christ during persecution and endured torture and martyrdom.
Notes: Distinct from Anastasia of Thessalonica commemorated Oct 30.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Abramius the Recluse and his niece Mary of Mesopotamia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Abramius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abramius lived as a hermit and guided his niece Mary back after she fell into sin; their story is one of repentance, rescue, and ascetic mercy.
Notes: Named uncle-niece commemoration kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Abramius, Archimandrite of Rostov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Abramius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk who evangelized pagan Rostov and founded monastic life by Lake Nero, remembered as a missionary and founder.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Claudius, Asterius, Neon, and Theonilla of Aegae
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Claudius, Asterius, Neon, and Theonilla, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Christian siblings betrayed after their father's death by a stepmother over inheritance, who endured torture and martyrdom under Diocletian.
Notes: Named family group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Anna of Constantinople
Also Known As: Euthymianus; Anna and her son John
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Anna, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A widow who lived ascetically in male disguise under the name Euthymianus, together with her son John, remembered for hidden holiness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyr Timothy of Esphigmenou
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Monastic Martyr Timothy, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monastic martyr of Esphigmenou Monastery on Mount Athos, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no life details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Serapion of Zarzma
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Serapion, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian nobleman who longed for the hermit life and helped establish the Zarzma monastic community in Klarjeti.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Zenobios and his sister Zenobia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Zenobios and Zenobia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A brother and sister of Aegae raised in the faith who gave away their wealth; Zenobios became bishop, and both suffered martyrdom.
Notes: Named sibling pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Tertius of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Tertius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An apostolic helper who wrote down the Epistle to the Romans and later served as Bishop of Iconium, dying a martyr.
Notes: Apostle of the Seventy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Justus of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Justus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the candidates considered to replace Judas, counted among the Seventy, who became bishop and died a martyr at Eleutheropolis.
Notes: Apostle of the Seventy; not Justin Martyr.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Artemas of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Artemas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An apostolic worker counted among the Seventy and remembered as Bishop of Lystra, who reposed in peace.
Notes: Apostle of the Seventy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Marcian of Syracuse
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Marcian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of the Apostle Peter sent to Sicily to preach Christ, who served as Bishop of Syracuse and died a martyr.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Eutropia of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Eutropia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian woman of Alexandria who encouraged imprisoned believers and confessed Christ when arrested, martyred around 250.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Anastasia of Thessalonica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Anastasia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian woman associated with Thessalonica who endured tortures during the Roman persecutions and was executed in Rome.
Notes: Not Anastasia of Rome commemorated Oct 29.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Stephen Milutin of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy King Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian king known for church building, state leadership, and patronage of Orthodox life during a major period of Serbian expansion.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Dragutin of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy King Dragutin, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Brother of Stephen Milutin who abdicated the throne and lived in repentance and hidden asceticism as the monk Theoctistus.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Helen of Serbia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Queen Helen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Mother of Saints Stephen Milutin and Dragutin, remembered for charity, monastic patronage, and care for the poor.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Righteous Jotham Zedgenidze
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Jotham, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian nobleman remembered for loyalty to King George VIII and faithfulness amid war and political turmoil.
Notes: Local Georgian commemoration.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr John Kochurov
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Orthodox priest and missionary in America who later served in Russia and was killed in 1917, honored as the first clergy martyr of the Russian Revolution.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostles Stachys, Apelles, Amplias, Urban, and Narcissus of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostles of the Seventy, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Apostolic workers named in the New Testament who served as bishops and missionaries in the early Church.
Notes: Named group kept as one row; Apostles of the Seventy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Epimachus of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Epimachus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Egyptian ascetic who entered Alexandria during persecution, destroyed idols, confessed Christ, and was tortured and martyred.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Spyridon and Nikodemos the Prosphora-bakers of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Spyridon and Nikodemos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Two Kiev Caves monks who baked prosphora for thirty years in humble obedience, remembered for prayer, labor, and devotion to the Psalms.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Maura of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Maura, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deaconess and abbess who founded a monastery in Constantinople and lived in asceticism.
Notes: Commemorated Nov 9 in Greek usage.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: The 100,000 Martyrs of Tbilisi
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Tbilisi, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Orthodox Christians of Tbilisi killed after the city was captured by Jalal al-Din in 1227, who refused to desecrate the holy icons.
Notes: Named numerical group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theodota, mother of Cosmas and Damian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Righteous Theodota, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: The mother of Saints Cosmas and Damian, remembered for raising her sons in the Christian faith after their pagan father died.
Notes: Commemorated with the Unmercenaries Cosmas and Damian (OS-0036).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyrs John the Bishop and James the Presbyter of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyrs John and James, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Persian clergy who preached Christ under Shapur II and suffered torture and martyrdom during the persecution.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Kyriaina and Juliana
Also Known As: Kyriaina of Tarsus; Juliana of Roso
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Kyriaina and Juliana, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Christian women of Cilicia known for charity toward orphans, widows, and the poor, who were arrested under Maximian Galerius and martyred.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Hermenegild the Goth of Spain
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Hermenegild, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Gothic prince who left Arianism for Orthodox Christianity and, refusing to return to heresy under his father's pressure, was killed in 586.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Caesarius, Dacius, and those with them at Damascus
Also Known As: Caesarius; Dacius; Savva; Sabinian; Agrippa; Adrian; Thomas
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Caesarius and those with him, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A group of Christians who suffered martyrdom at Damascus in the seventh century.
Notes: Named group kept as one row; OCA gives brief details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyr James of Docheiariou and his disciples
Also Known As: James; James the Deacon; Dionysios the Monk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Monastic Martyr James, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of Docheiariou and spiritual elder who restored the Georgian Skete of Saint John the Baptist, and with two disciples suffered martyrdom under Ottoman rule.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyr Helen of Sinope
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Helen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Christian woman of Sinope raised in piety who suffered martyrdom under Ottoman pressure.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Acindynus, Pegasius, Aphthonius, Elpidephorus, Anempodistus, and 7,000 with them
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Acindynus and those with him, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Secret Christian courtiers of Shapur II who confessed Christ during the Persian persecution and suffered with thousands of other Christians.
Notes: Named numerical group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyrs Akepsimas, Joseph, and Aeithalas of Persia
Also Known As: Akepsimas the Bishop; Joseph the Presbyter; Aeithalas the Deacon
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyrs Akepsimas, Joseph, and Aeithalas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Persian church leaders arrested under Shapur II for their ministry, who endured imprisonment and torture before martyrdom.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Eudoxios, Agapios, Atticus, and those with them
Also Known As: Eudoxios; Agapios; Atticus; Marinus; Oceanus; Eustratios
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Eudoxios, Agapios, and Atticus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Christian soldiers in Sebaste who refused pagan sacrifice under Licinius and suffered martyrdom together.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Akepsimas the Hermit of Cyrrhus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Akepsimas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A desert hermit who lived for decades in fasting, silence, and prayer before being consecrated a bishop late in life.
Notes: Distinct from the Hieromartyr Akepsimas of Persia on the same date.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Snandulia of Persia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessor Snandulia, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Persian Christian woman remembered for visiting and serving imprisoned confessors during the persecution.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Pimen the Bulgarian of Zographou
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Pimen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Bulgarian monk of Zographou Monastery on Mount Athos, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Princess Anna Vsevolodna
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Anna, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A daughter of Grand Prince Vsevolod Yaroslavich who refused marriage and became a nun, helping establish women's monastic life and education in Kiev.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Nicholas, Radiant Star of the Georgians
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nicholas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian saint known chiefly as a writer of church services, of whom little else is preserved.
Notes: Distinct from Martyr Nicholas Dvali (OS-0647).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Seraphim (Samoilovich), Archbishop of Uglich
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Seraphim, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian hierarch who had served as a teacher in Alaska, who suffered under Soviet persecution and was martyred in 1937.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Joannicius the Great
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Joannicius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former soldier from Bithynia who became a great ascetic and defender of Orthodoxy during the iconoclast period.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyrs Nicander of Myra and Hermas
Also Known As: Nicander the Bishop; Hermas the Presbyter
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyrs Nicander and Hermas, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Disciples of Saint Titus ordained for pastoral ministry in Lycia, who suffered martyrdom for preaching Christ.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Mercurius the Faster of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Mercurius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Caves remembered as a faster, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Nicander, Abbot of Gorodensk
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nicander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An abbot of Gorodensk in the Novgorod region commemorated by the Church, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints John, Stephen, and Isaiah of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Fathers John, Stephen, and Isaiah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three Georgian saints commemorated together for lives of service, confession, and holiness.
Notes: Named group kept as one row; OCA gives a general notice.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Simon of Yurievets
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Simon, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A holy fool abandoned by his parents who lived barefoot and in poverty, enduring hardship for Christ and honored for hidden holiness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Gabriel of Atonelni
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Gabriel, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian monk commemorated by the Church, of whom no detailed life survives.
Notes: Honest stub; OCA gives no details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Galaction and Episteme
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Galaction and Episteme, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A married couple who chose virginity and monastic life after conversion and suffered martyrdom under persecution.
Notes: Named couple kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Jonah, Archbishop of Novgorod
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Jonah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An orphan raised by a pious widow who became Archbishop of Novgorod, known for pastoral care, mercy, and miracles.
Notes: Repose commemoration.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostles Patrobus, Hermes, Linus, Gaius, and Philologus of the Seventy
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostles of the Seventy, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Apostolic workers of Saint Paul's circle who preached the Gospel and served as bishops in various cities.
Notes: Named group kept as one row; Apostles of the Seventy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Gregory, Archbishop of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Gregory, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Alexandria who suffered under the iconoclast Emperor Leo, being flogged and imprisoned, and died in prison.
