Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Paul the Confessor, Archbishop of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Paul, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Archbishop of Constantinople who defended Orthodoxy against Arian pressure, endured repeated exile, and was killed in Armenia.
Notes: Distinct from Paul the Confessor, Bishop of Plousias (OS-0791).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Luke the Steward of the Kiev Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Luke, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk of the Kiev Near Caves who fulfilled the obedience of steward, praised for humble monastic labor.
Notes: Not Luke the Evangelist.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Herman, Archbishop of Kazan
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Herman, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian monk who became Archbishop of Kazan and helped strengthen Orthodoxy in the newly Christianized region.
Notes: Principal repose feast; the translation of his relics is commemorated Sep 25.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Luke of Sicily
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Luke, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Sicilian ascetic who left family and betrothed for the wilderness monastic life near Mount Etna and later guided others.
Notes: Pre-schism Western/Italian saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Virgin Martyr Manatho
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Virgin Martyr Manatho, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian virgin arrested under Maximian who was publicly humiliated, tortured, and burned alive.
Notes: Nov 13 = Manetha of Caesarea (likely the same virgin martyr; verify).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: 33 Holy Martyrs of Melitene
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs of Melitene, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A group of Christian soldiers led by Hieron who refused pagan military demands under Diocletian and were taken to Melitene and martyred.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Lazarus of Mount Galesius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Lazarus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A monk formed in Palestine who settled near Ephesus and founded a monastic community on Mount Galesius, living in severe asceticism as a stylite.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Melasippus, Karina, their son Antoninus, and forty children
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Melasippus, Karina, and Antoninus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian husband, wife, and son tortured under Julian the Apostate; forty children converted by their witness joined them in martyrdom.
Notes: Named family/group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Auctus, Taurion, and Thessalonica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Auctus, Taurion, and Thessalonica, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Thessalonica was cast out by her pagan father for becoming Christian; Auctus and Taurion defended her and suffered martyrdom with her.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Onesiphorus and Porphyrius of Ephesus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Onesiphorus and Porphyrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Christians of Ephesus who suffered under Diocletian, being beaten, dragged by wild horses, and dying for Christ.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Matrona, Abbess of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Matrona, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A married woman of Perge who left worldly life for monastic asceticism and later became an abbess in Constantinople.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Theoktiste of Lesbos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mother Theoktiste, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An orphaned girl raised in a monastery who later lived as a solitary ascetic on Paros after escaping captivity.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Onesiphorus the Confessor of the Kiev Near Caves
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Onesiphorus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A priest-monk of the Kiev Near Caves known for ascetic life and clairvoyance, who reposed in 1148.
Notes: Not Apostle Onesiphorus of the Seventy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Alexander of Thessalonica
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Alexander, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Thessalonica who openly confessed the faith and rejected pagan sacrifice under Maximian and was beheaded for Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Anthony of Apamea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Anthony, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Syrian stone-mason who began building a church with his bishop's blessing and was attacked and killed by pagans for his Christian work.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable John the Short
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An Egyptian desert father in the monastic tradition of Saint Pimen, remembered for obedience, humility, and spiritual wisdom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints Eustolia and Sopatra of Constantinople
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Mothers Eustolia and Sopatra, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Eustolia, a Roman nun who came to Constantinople, and Sopatra, daughter of Emperor Maurice, founded and guided a women's monastery together.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Nectarius of Pentapolis
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch Nectarius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A modern Greek hierarch, preacher, and monastic founder who endured slander and hardship, reposed on Aegina, and became widely loved as a wonderworker.
Notes: Modern Orthodox saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saints Euthymius and Neophytus of Docheiariou
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Fathers Euthymius and Neophytus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An uncle and nephew of Byzantine aristocracy who became monks and founded Docheiariou Monastery on Mount Athos.
Notes: Named pair kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostles Erastus, Olympas, Herodion, Sosipater, Quartus, and Tertius of the Seventy
Also Known As: Erastus; Olympas; Herodion; Sosipater; Quartus; Tertius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostles of the Seventy, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Disciples of Saint Paul mentioned in Romans and apostolic tradition who preached the Gospel and served the early Church in various places.
Notes: Named group kept as one row; members have individual feasts (e.g. Tertius, OS-2117).
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Orestes the Physician of Cappadocia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Orestes, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A learned physician of Tyana who confessed Christ under Diocletian and was tortured and martyred for refusing pagan worship.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Hieromartyr Milos the Wonderworker and his two disciples
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hieromartyr Milos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former general who became Bishop of Susa and received gifts of healing and prophecy, who with two disciples suffered martyrdom in Persia.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Theocteristus, Abbot of Symbola
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theocteristus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the Symbola Monastery on Mount Olympus who suffered under the iconoclast persecution.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Constantine, King of Georgia
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Constantine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Georgian ruler who resisted Arab pressure and refused to abandon the Christian faith, suffering martyrdom for Christ.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Victor of Damascus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Victor, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian soldier under Marcus Aurelius who refused pagan sacrifice and endured torture and martyrdom.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Vincent of Spain
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Vincent, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A deacon trained by Bishop Valerian who preached boldly during persecution and endured torture and martyrdom under Diocletian.
Notes: Pre-schism Western saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Stephanida of Damascus
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Stephanida, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young Christian woman who witnessed Saint Victor's torments, confessed Christ, and was martyred after joining his witness.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Theodore the Studite
Also Known As: Theodore the Confessor; Abbot of Studion
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Theodore, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Abbot of the Studion Monastery and a major defender of the holy icons, who suffered exile and persecution for Orthodox teaching.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Blessed Maximus of Moscow
Short Prayer (Intercession): Blessed Maximus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A holy fool of Moscow who embraced poverty, exposure, and hardship for Christ and became loved as a hidden intercessor for the city.
Notes: Principal repose feast; the uncovering of his relics is commemorated Aug 13.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Stephen of Decani
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy King Stephen, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian king who endured blinding, exile, and betrayal; he founded Dečani Monastery and was later killed amid dynastic conflict.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Varnava (Nastic) of Hvosno
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Confessor Varnava, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Serbian Orthodox bishop and confessor who suffered under communist persecution.
Notes: Modern Serbian saint; OCA gives no further details.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint John the Merciful, Patriarch of Alexandria
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Hierarch John, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A widower who became Patriarch of Alexandria and was famed for radical almsgiving and tireless care for the poor.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Neilos the Ascetic of Sinai
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Neilos, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A former imperial official and disciple of Saint John Chrysostom who became a Sinai ascetic and spiritual writer.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Prophet Ahijah
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Prophet Ahijah, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A prophet of Shiloh, contemporary of Solomon, who foretold Jeroboam's rule over the northern tribes and later prophesied judgment for apostasy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Nilus the Myrrhgusher of Mount Athos
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Nilus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Greek monk raised by his hieromonk uncle who became an Athonite ascetic and is venerated as a myrrh-streaming saint.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Nikephoros, Antoninus, and Germanus of Caesarea
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Nikephoros, Antoninus, and Germanus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Three Christians beheaded at Caesarea in Palestine under Maximian by the ruler Firmilian.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Monastic Martyr Damascene of the Lavra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Monastic Martyr Damascene, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Constantinopolitan orphan who became a monk and, after a fall into apostasy, repented and confessed Christ, suffering martyrdom under Ottoman rule.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Apostle Philip
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Apostle Philip, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: One of the Twelve Apostles, called by Christ and known for bringing Nathanael to Him, who preached the Gospel and suffered for Christ.
Notes: Not Philip the Deacon / of the Seventy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Philip, Abbot of Irap
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Philip, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: An orphan who entered monastic life under Saint Cornelius of Komel and founded the Irap wilderness monastery near Novgorod.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Justinian the Emperor
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Emperor Justinian, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Byzantine emperor remembered for defending Orthodox doctrine, building churches, and supporting church order, whose reign included major legal and architectural works.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Theodora the Empress, wife of Justinian
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Empress Theodora, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Wife of Emperor Justinian, remembered in Orthodox tradition for repentance and pious rule after an earlier dissolute life.
Notes: Distinct from Theodora the Empress who restored the icons (OS-0637); historical complexity around her Monophysite sympathies is noted.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Hieromartyr Demetrius (Benevolenskii) of Tver
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Demetrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Russian priest who served village parishes and was martyred during the Soviet persecution.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: New Martyr Constantine of Hydra
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy New Martyr Constantine, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A young man of Hydra who fell into apostasy while serving an Ottoman official, later repented, confessed Christ, and was martyred on Rhodes.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs and Confessors Gurias, Samonas, and Habibus of Edessa
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Gurias, Samonas, and Habibus, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Gurias and Samonas were arrested as Christian preachers under Diocletian, and Habibus the deacon suffered under Licinius; they are honored together as martyrs and confessors of Edessa, invoked for the protection of marriage.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyrs Elpidius, Marcellus, and Eustochius
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyrs Elpidius, Marcellus, and Eustochius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: Christians who suffered under Julian the Apostate; Elpidius, a senator, and his companions endured torture and were killed for Christ.
Notes: Named group kept as one row.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Martyr Demetrius of Thrace
Short Prayer (Intercession): Holy Martyr Demetrius, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A Christian of Thrace who was arrested under Maximian and suffered many tortures before being beheaded.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Venerable Paisios Velichkovsky
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Paisios, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A great monastic elder who revived patristic spiritual life through hesychast teaching and translations, deeply shaping Slavic and Romanian Orthodoxy.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints

Name: Saint Philip of Rabanga
Short Prayer (Intercession): Venerable Father Philip, pray to God for us.
Brief Life: A disciple of Saint Dionysius of Glushitsa who founded the Savior-Transfiguration Monastery near Kadnikov.
Sources: OCA Synaxarion (oca.org), Lives of the Saints
